[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.3 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 1.2 seconds\nOutput:\n\r\n===== PAGE 1 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n1\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO\r\nPhase 1 - Complete Codex Implementation Prompt\r\nAuthoring Studio  |  Asset Library  |  Publishing  |  Player Runtime  |  Captain Control  |  Future Vision \r\nIntegration\r\nOne shared story model. One authoritative progression engine. Three interfaces: \r\nStudio, Player, and Captain. A fourth interface, the future vision helper, must plug \r\ninto the same verification pipeline without redesigning the platform.\r\nPrepared as a direct, copyable implementation brief for Codex\r\nJuly 2026\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 2 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n2\r\nHOW TO USE THIS DOCUMENT\r\nCopy the implementation prompt beginning on the next page into Codex from the root of the intended repository.\r\nThe prompt deliberately instructs Codex to inspect the existing application before changing it, preserve unrelated work, adapt \r\nroute and technology details to the real repository, and continue beyond planning into tested implementation.\r\nThis brief treats the future vision helper as an extension of the same verification system used by Captain approval and player \r\nconfirmation. That is the structural safeguard that prevents Phase 2 from requiring a rewrite.\r\nThe prompt is intentionally demanding. Software tends to obey the least convenient interpretation unless one removes its \r\nhiding places.\r\nIMPLEMENTATION PROMPT BEGINS\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 3 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n3\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO - PHASE 1\r\nSUPER-DETAILED CODEX IMPLEMENTATION PROMPT\r\nMISSION\r\nImplement a complete, production-quality Phase 1 Tall Tale Studio inside the existing Tall Tale Companion project. This is not \r\na mockup, isolated demo, or disconnected editor. It must become the authoring system that creates the same Tall Tales the \r\nplayer experience displays and the Captain experience controls.\r\nThe implementation must preserve the current player and Captain experiences, integrate with the existing application \r\narchitecture and visual language, and establish clean extension points for a future local helper application that will perform \r\nscreen capture and machine-vision verification. Do not implement the helper application or real computer vision in this phase. \r\nDo implement the contracts, event model, verification abstractions, identifiers, persistence, and development simulator needed \r\nso that vision can be added later without replacing the story engine.\r\nThe final system must make it easy to\r\n1. Create a new Tall Tale from a dedicated Studio route.\r\n2. Organize the Tall Tale into chapters.\r\n3. Build the main story by dragging, dropping, configuring, and reordering reusable story blocks.\r\n4. Upload and reuse images and other media through a proper asset library.\r\n5. Pair original and modified images for cinematic transformations.\r\n6. Create locations, artifacts, riddles, notes, directions, confirmations, reveals, and chapter endings.\r\n7. Preview an individual block or play the tale from any point without corrupting real progress.\r\n8. Publish immutable versions.\r\n9. Automatically expose published Tall Tales on the player-facing Tall Tale selection page.\r\n10. Let the Captain monitor and manually control active sessions now.\r\n11. Let future verification providers, including a local machine-vision helper, submit the same standardized completion events \r\nlater.\r\n12. Extend the platform with additional block types, verification methods, assets, and branching logic without redesigning the \r\ndatabase or player runtime.\r\nTreat this as a foundational product architecture task. Implement it carefully, coherently, and end to end.\r\n1. FIRST ACTIONS: INSPECT BEFORE MODIFYING\r\nBefore changing code\r\n• Inspect the repository structure, framework, package manager, routing, state management, API layer, persistence layer, \r\nauthentication, Captain tools, player pages, animation system, media handling, testing conventions, deployment \r\nassumptions, and coding standards.\r\n• Identify the canonical source of truth for current Tall Tale content and player progress.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 4 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n4\r\n• Identify whether the current project already has concepts similar to tales, chapters, clues, stages, sessions, unlocks, users, \r\nCaptain controls, assets, or published content.\r\n• Identify current route patterns and preserve them.\r\n• Identify how the player and Captain clients receive live updates, if live updates already exist.\r\n• Identify how database migrations are created and tested.\r\n• Identify how user-facing timestamps, save states, loading states, and errors are displayed.\r\n• Identify the current visual design tokens, spacing, typography, parchment treatments, animation utilities, modal patterns, \r\nform controls, and responsive breakpoints.\r\nWrite a brief internal implementation map before coding, but do not stop at planning. Continue through implementation, \r\nmigration, integration, tests, validation, and documentation.\r\nDo not build a parallel mini-application with separate fake data. Use the existing application and database conventions unless \r\na well-justified migration is required.\r\n2. NON-NEGOTIABLE PRODUCT RULES\r\n2.1 End-to-end truth\r\nEvery important Studio action must be backed by real persistence and real runtime behavior. Do not ship decorative controls \r\nthat do nothing.\r\nExamples\r\n• Creating a Tall Tale must create a persistent Tall Tale record.\r\n• Publishing must create a persistent immutable published version.\r\n• A newly published Tall Tale must appear on the player-facing selection page without hand-editing source files.\r\n• Reordering blocks must change the order used by the player runtime.\r\n• Uploading an image must create a reusable asset record and actual stored file.\r\n• Replacing an asset must update the rendered result without breaking every block reference.\r\n• Captain unlock actions must update the active player session through the real progression engine.\r\n• Preview mode must be isolated from real player progress.\r\n2.2 Preserve existing behavior\r\nDo not remove or regress current player features, Captain features, authentication, styling, transitions, story content, routes, or \r\ndeployment behavior. Migrate existing hard-coded or older tale content safely where required.\r\n2.3 No fake status indicators\r\nStatus labels must tell the truth. Never display \"Saved,\" \"Published,\" \"Updated just now,\" \"Connected,\" \"Vision ready,\" or \r\nsimilar claims unless the underlying state confirms it.\r\nUse explicit states such as\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 5 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n5\r\n• Unsaved changes\r\n• Saving\r\n• Saved at [real timestamp]\r\n• Save failed\r\n• Draft validation failed\r\n• Publishing\r\n• Published as version X\r\n• Captain connection lost\r\n• Awaiting verification\r\n• Manually verified by Captain\r\n• Player-confirmed\r\n• Vision provider unavailable\r\n2.4 Durable identifiers\r\nUse stable unique IDs for tales, draft revisions, published versions, chapters, blocks, assets, asset variants, collections, \r\nlocations, artifacts, sessions, progress records, unlock events, verification requests, and verification events.\r\nNever use visible names or array indexes as durable foreign keys.\r\n2.5 Extensibility over hard-coding\r\nBlock rendering, block configuration, verification handling, and completion logic must be registry-driven or otherwise modular. \r\nAdding a new block type later should not require a giant chain of conditionals spread across the entire application.\r\n2.6 Accessibility and responsiveness\r\nThe Studio is desktop-first because drag-and-drop authoring benefits from space, but it must remain usable on smaller \r\nscreens. The player experience must remain fully responsive for desktop and iPhone. Preserve keyboard access, visible focus \r\nstates, semantic labels, contrast, reduced-motion support, and screen-reader-friendly status messaging.\r\n2.7 Visual quality\r\nDo not produce a generic gray SaaS dashboard. The Studio should feel like a refined creative tool within the existing pirate \r\nstory world: parchment, cartography, ink, brass, wood, subtle depth, and cinematic motion, while keeping controls readable \r\nand efficient.\r\nAuthoring interactions should be responsive and not needlessly slow. Player-facing reveals may be longer and theatrical. \r\nAvoid meaningless 250 ms fades for major story moments, but also avoid ten-second blank fades that merely waste the \r\nplayer's remaining lifespan.\r\n3. REQUIRED ROUTE AND EXPERIENCE MODEL\r\nAdapt exact route syntax to the existing router, but provide clear separation among the three experiences.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 6 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n6\r\n3.1 Player experience\r\nProvide or integrate routes equivalent to\r\n• /tales or the existing player home/catalog route\r\n• /play/:taleSlug\r\n• /play/:taleSlug/session/:sessionId when sessions are explicit\r\n• /play/:taleSlug/history or the existing completed-tale history pattern\r\nThe player must see only published, available Tall Tales. Drafts must never appear publicly.\r\n3.2 Captain experience\r\nProvide or integrate routes equivalent to\r\n• /captain\r\n• /captain/tales/:taleId\r\n• /captain/sessions/:sessionId\r\nThe Captain must be able to choose a Tall Tale, view active sessions, inspect the current story position, see pending \r\nverification requests, manually complete or reject the current verification, trigger permitted presentation events, and recover \r\nfrom mistakes through controlled rollback or jump actions.\r\n3.3 Studio experience\r\nCreate a dedicated authoring route equivalent to\r\n• /studio\r\n• /studio/tales/new\r\n• /studio/tales/:taleId\r\n• /studio/tales/:taleId/settings\r\n• /studio/tales/:taleId/assets\r\n• /studio/tales/:taleId/locations\r\n• /studio/tales/:taleId/artifacts\r\n• /studio/tales/:taleId/versions\r\nExact nested routing may differ, but the Studio must be independently addressable and must not be hidden inside the Captain \r\nsession-control page.\r\n3.4 Authorization\r\nUse the project's existing authentication and authorization model. At minimum\r\n• Players may view and play eligible published tales.\r\n• Captains may control sessions they are authorized to control.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 7 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n7\r\n• Creators/editors may create and modify drafts.\r\n• Only authorized creators/publishers may publish.\r\n• Draft assets, unpublished content, private Captain notes, reference images, internal verification metadata, and Studio APIs \r\nmust not leak into public player payloads.\r\nIf the project currently has a single trusted creator account, still implement permissions through explicit roles/capabilities rather \r\nthan hard-coding one email address throughout the codebase.\r\n4. CORE DOMAIN MODEL\r\nUse the existing persistence technology and naming conventions, but implement equivalent concepts and relationships.\r\n4.1 Tall Tale\r\nRepresents the long-lived identity of an adventure.\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• slug\r\n• title\r\n• subtitle\r\n• shortDescription\r\n• longDescription\r\n• coverAssetId\r\n• themeId or theme configuration\r\n• status: draft, published, archived\r\n• visibility: private, unlisted, public, or the closest supported model\r\n• creatorId\r\n• createdAt\r\n• updatedAt\r\n• currentDraftRevisionId\r\n• latestPublishedVersionId\r\n• defaultPlayerCountMin\r\n• defaultPlayerCountMax\r\n• estimatedDuration\r\n• contentWarnings or notes if supported\r\n• sortOrder or featured state for player catalog ordering\r\n4.2 Draft revision\r\nThe editable working state of a Tall Tale. The implementation may use one mutable draft or revisioned drafts, depending on \r\nthe current architecture, but publishing must create a stable snapshot.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 8 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n8\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• taleId\r\n• revisionNumber\r\n• basedOnPublishedVersionId\r\n• createdBy\r\n• createdAt\r\n• updatedAt\r\n• autosaveVersion or optimistic concurrency token\r\n• validationState\r\n4.3 Published version\r\nAn immutable snapshot of all player-runtime content required to play a specific version.\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• taleId\r\n• versionLabel, such as 1.0, 1.1, or sequential integer\r\n• versionNumber\r\n• publishedAt\r\n• publishedBy\r\n• releaseNotes\r\n• contentSnapshot or normalized version-bound records\r\n• schemaVersion\r\n• checksum or integrity marker\r\n• isCurrent\r\nA running player session must remain pinned to the published version it started with unless an explicit migration workflow is \r\nimplemented. Editing or republishing a draft must not silently change an active or completed session.\r\n4.4 Chapter\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• draftRevisionId or publishedVersionId\r\n• title\r\n• subtitle\r\n• description\r\n• orderIndex\r\n• coverAssetId\r\n• estimatedDuration\r\n• entryBlockId\r\n• completionBlockId\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 9 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n9\r\n• isOptional\r\n• metadata\r\n4.5 Story block\r\nEvery unit in the story timeline is a block.\r\nSuggested shared fields\r\n• id\r\n• chapterId\r\n• blockType\r\n• title\r\n• internalLabel\r\n• orderIndex\r\n• configuration JSON or typed child configuration\r\n• presentation configuration\r\n• completion configuration\r\n• nextBlockId for linear flow\r\n• createdAt\r\n• updatedAt\r\n• isEnabled\r\n• creatorNotes, private\r\nThe database may normalize block-specific fields or store versioned JSON. Choose the approach that best fits the project, but \r\nvalidate configuration with a schema per block type.\r\n4.6 Block connection\r\nEven though Phase 1 presents a mostly linear vertical timeline, the data model must allow future branching.\r\nRepresent connections explicitly or reserve compatible fields for\r\n• sourceBlockId\r\n• targetBlockId\r\n• connectionType: default, choice, hint, success, failure, optional, return\r\n• conditionExpression or future condition reference\r\n• label\r\n• orderIndex\r\nFor Phase 1, the editor may maintain one default next connection per block, plus optional connections where required by \r\nChoice or Hint behavior.\r\n4.7 Asset\r\nRepresents a stable reusable logical asset, not merely a filename.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 10 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n10\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• taleId or library scope\r\n• mediaType: image, video, audio, document, model, other\r\n• displayName\r\n• description\r\n• originalFilename\r\n• storageKey\r\n• mimeType\r\n• fileSize\r\n• width\r\n• height\r\n• duration\r\n• checksum\r\n• createdBy\r\n• createdAt\r\n• updatedAt\r\n• currentVariantId or currentFileRevisionId\r\n• deletedAt for safe soft deletion\r\n4.8 Asset file revision or variant\r\nSupport replacing or reprocessing a file without changing the logical asset ID.\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• assetId\r\n• role: original, optimized, thumbnail, preview, blurredPlaceholder, mobile, poster, waveform, other\r\n• storageKey\r\n• mimeType\r\n• fileSize\r\n• width\r\n• height\r\n• duration\r\n• checksum\r\n• processingState\r\n• createdAt\r\nPreserve the uploaded original. Generate web-friendly derivatives where the existing stack supports it.\r\n4.9 Asset role assignment\r\nAllow an asset to have multiple semantic roles without duplicating the file.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 11 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n11\r\nRoles should include at least\r\n• storyArtwork\r\n• mapImage\r\n• transformationBefore\r\n• transformationAfter\r\n• cinematic\r\n• audioCue\r\n• locationReference\r\n• objectReference\r\n• negativeReference\r\n• cover\r\n• artifactArtwork\r\n• captainOnlyReference\r\nSome roles are future-facing and need not change player behavior yet, but they must be representable.\r\n4.10 Asset collection\r\nGroup related media into reusable collections.\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• taleId\r\n• name\r\n• description\r\n• collectionType: general, transformationSet, locationReferenceSet, objectReferenceSet, chapterSet\r\n• locationId optional\r\n• artifactId optional\r\n• createdAt\r\n• updatedAt\r\nCollection membership should support labels such as frontAngle, leftAngle, night, negativeNearbyRock, transformationBefore, \r\ntransformationAfter, and creator-defined tags.\r\n4.11 Tagging\r\nSupport reusable tags and search by tags. Tags should not be stored only as comma-separated display strings if the current \r\ndatabase can reasonably normalize them.\r\n4.12 Location\r\nRepresents a reusable story location.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 12 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n12\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• taleId\r\n• name\r\n• slug or internal code\r\n• region\r\n• generalDescription\r\n• playerFacingDescription\r\n• captainNotes\r\n• mapAssetId\r\n• displayAssetId\r\n• referenceCollectionId\r\n• verificationProfileId optional\r\n• orderIndex\r\n• createdAt\r\n• updatedAt\r\n4.13 Artifact\r\nRepresents the story-world meaning of an ordinary in-game object.\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• taleId\r\n• name\r\n• shortDescription\r\n• loreDescription\r\n• ordinaryGameObjectLabel\r\n• artworkAssetId\r\n• revealVideoAssetId\r\n• optionalModelAssetId\r\n• inventoryCategory\r\n• collectionGroup\r\n• sortOrder\r\n• persistentAfterUnlock\r\n• createdAt\r\n• updatedAt\r\n4.14 Player session\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• taleId\r\n• publishedVersionId\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 13 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n13\r\n• ownerPlayerId or partyId\r\n• captainId optional\r\n• status: notStarted, active, paused, completed, abandoned\r\n• currentChapterId\r\n• currentBlockId\r\n• startedAt\r\n• updatedAt\r\n• completedAt\r\n• lastHeartbeatAt if applicable\r\n• previewMode false for real sessions\r\n4.15 Progress and event history\r\nDo not store only one mutable currentBlock value. Preserve an append-only or auditable event history sufficient to explain how \r\nprogression occurred.\r\nEvents should include\r\n• sessionStarted\r\n• blockEntered\r\n• verificationRequested\r\n• verificationSatisfied\r\n• verificationRejected\r\n• captainManualUnlock\r\n• playerConfirmation\r\n• textAnswerAccepted\r\n• blockCompleted\r\n• presentationTriggered\r\n• chapterCompleted\r\n• artifactGranted\r\n• sessionPaused\r\n• sessionResumed\r\n• sessionCompleted\r\n• captainRollback\r\n• captainJump\r\nEvery event should include\r\n• id\r\n• sessionId\r\n• publishedVersionId\r\n• blockId where applicable\r\n• eventType\r\n• sourceType\r\n• sourceId or actorId where applicable\r\n• idempotencyKey\r\n• payload\r\n• createdAt\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

FUTURE VISION-HELPER COMPATIBILITY\r\nThis section is mandatory. Phase 1 must be designed so that a future local helper application can be added without rebuilding \r\nprogression.\r\n5.1 Verification provider abstraction\r\nImplement a server-side verification abstraction with provider types equivalent to:\r\n• captainManual\r\n• playerConfirmation\r\n• textAnswer\r\n• timer\r\n• visionLocation\r\n• visionObject\r\n• externalWebhook or futureExternalProvider\r\nDo not implement actual vision inference now. Do implement the shared interface and persisted configuration.\r\nConceptual interface\r\nVerificationProvider.canHandle(type)\r\nVerificationProvider.createRequest(session, block, configuration)\r\nVerificationProvider.evaluateSubmission(request, submission)\r\nVerificationProvider.cancelRequest(request)\r\nThe progression engine must consume standardized outcomes rather than contain provider-specific logic.\r\n5.2 Verification request model\r\nSuggested fields\r\n• id\r\n• sessionId\r\n• blockId\r\n• providerType\r\n• status: pending, satisfied, rejected, expired, cancelled\r\n• requestedAt\r\n• resolvedAt\r\n• expiresAt optional\r\n• configurationSnapshot\r\n• providerCorrelationId optional\r\n• satisfiedByEventId optional\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 15 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n15\r\n5.3 Standardized verification event contract\r\nCreate an internal and externally documentable event contract similar to\r\n{\r\n  \"schemaVersion\": 1,\r\n  \"eventId\": \"uuid\",\r\n  \"idempotencyKey\": \"stable-unique-key\",\r\n  \"eventType\": \"verification.observation\" or \"verification.result\",\r\n  \"providerType\": \"visionLocation\",\r\n  \"providerInstanceId\": \"local-helper-device-id\",\r\n  \"sessionId\": \"uuid\",\r\n  \"publishedVersionId\": \"uuid\",\r\n  \"blockId\": \"uuid\",\r\n  \"verificationRequestId\": \"uuid\",\r\n  \"observedAt\": \"ISO-8601 timestamp\",\r\n  \"result\": \"match\" | \"notMatch\" | \"uncertain\",\r\n  \"confidence\": 0.972,\r\n  \"evidence\": {\r\n    \"referenceCollectionId\": \"uuid\",\r\n    \"matchedReferenceAssetId\": \"uuid\",\r\n    \"consecutivePassingFrames\": 4,\r\n    \"windowFrames\": 6\r\n  }\r\n}\r\nThe exact JSON may follow existing API conventions, but include schema versioning, idempotency, session identity, block \r\nidentity, published-version identity, request identity, provider identity, timestamp, result, confidence, and extensible evidence.\r\n5.4 Security for future helper app\r\nDesign but do not fully expose an unsafe public endpoint. Establish a documented plan and server-side model for:\r\n• Pairing a helper instance to a user/session through a short-lived pairing code or device token.\r\n• Scoping the helper to one player session.\r\n• Rotating and revoking tokens.\r\n• Rejecting events for a different session, version, block, or expired verification request.\r\n• Validating idempotency and replay attacks.\r\n• Rate limiting.\r\n• Never accepting arbitrary \"advance story\" commands from the helper.\r\n• Accepting only verification evidence against an existing pending request.\r\n5.5 Development simulator\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 16 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n16\r\nAdd a developer-only verification simulator in the Captain or Studio preview tooling. It should let an authorized developer \r\nsubmit simulated outcomes for a pending request:\r\n• Match\r\n• Not match\r\n• Uncertain\r\n• Custom confidence\r\n• Repeated duplicate event to verify idempotency\r\n• Stale request event\r\n• Wrong-block event\r\nThis simulator proves that future vision integration can use the same event path. It must be clearly labeled as a \r\ndevelopment/testing tool and unavailable to ordinary players in production.\r\n5.6 Block configuration compatibility\r\nAny block that may later use vision must store provider-independent completion configuration plus provider-specific settings.\r\nExample\r\n{\r\n  \"completionMode\": \"verification\",\r\n  \"verification\": {\r\n    \"providerType\": \"captainManual\",\r\n    \"futureProviderOptions\": {\r\n      \"visionLocation\": {\r\n        \"referenceCollectionId\": \"uuid\",\r\n        \"coarseThreshold\": 0.86,\r\n        \"minimumGeometricInliers\": 35,\r\n        \"requiredPassingFrames\": 4,\r\n        \"windowFrames\": 6\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n}\r\nDo not make manual Captain approval a special one-off field that later has to be deleted. It should be one provider \r\nconfiguration selected from the same verification model.\r\n6. STUDIO INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE\r\n6.1 Studio landing page\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 17 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n17\r\nCreate a polished Studio landing page containing\r\n• Create New Tall Tale\r\n• Continue editing recent drafts\r\n• Published tales\r\n• Archived tales\r\n• Search\r\n• Sort\r\n• Duplicate tale\r\n• Archive tale\r\n• Open player preview\r\n• Open Captain controls\r\n• Last real save timestamp\r\n• Draft validation state\r\n• Latest published version\r\nEach tale card should clearly distinguish Draft, Published, Draft with unpublished changes, and Archived.\r\n6.2 New Tall Tale flow\r\nCreate a guided but quick creation flow with\r\n• Title\r\n• Slug generated but editable with validation\r\n• Subtitle\r\n• Short description\r\n• Long description\r\n• Cover image selection or upload\r\n• Theme selection or inherited project default\r\n• Player count range\r\n• Estimated duration\r\n• Visibility\r\n• Initial chapter title\r\nAfter creation, open the editor immediately.\r\n6.3 Editor shell\r\nThe editor should include\r\nTop bar\r\n• Tall Tale title and draft indicator\r\n• Back to Studio\r\n• Undo\r\n• Redo\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 18 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n18\r\n• Real autosave state\r\n• Preview\r\n• Play From Here when a block is selected\r\n• Validate\r\n• Publish\r\n• More menu for settings, duplicate, archive, version history\r\nLeft panel\r\n• Block Library tab\r\n• Chapters tab\r\n• Story Outline tab\r\n• Search blocks\r\n• Collapsible categories: Story, Direction and Location, Media, Reveal, Interaction, Logic\r\nCenter canvas\r\n• Vertical chapter timeline\r\n• Chapter headers\r\n• Block cards in order\r\n• Drag handles\r\n• Insertion indicators\r\n• Selected-block state\r\n• Multi-select only if safely implemented\r\n• Empty chapter drop zone\r\n• Add block button between cards\r\n• Clear connection/flow indicators\r\n• Collapsible chapters\r\n• Optional compact overview mode\r\nRight inspector\r\n• Selected block title and type\r\n• Block content fields\r\n• Presentation settings\r\n• Completion/verification settings\r\n• Asset pickers\r\n• Private creator notes\r\n• Validation errors and warnings\r\n• Delete block\r\n• Duplicate block\r\n• Move to chapter\r\nBottom or slide-up asset drawer\r\n• Images\r\n• Video\r\n• Audio\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 19 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n19\r\n• Documents\r\n• Collections\r\n• Search\r\n• Filters\r\n• Upload drop zone\r\n• Recently used\r\n• Unused assets\r\n• Drag asset onto compatible block fields\r\nUse the layout pattern that best fits the existing UI framework, but preserve these functional regions.\r\n6.4 Drag-and-drop behavior\r\nUse an established accessible drag-and-drop library already present in the project or add a well-supported one appropriate to \r\nthe framework.\r\nRequired behavior\r\n• Drag a block type from the library into a chapter.\r\n• Drag existing blocks within a chapter.\r\n• Drag blocks across chapters.\r\n• Show exact insertion position.\r\n• Persist order.\r\n• Update default next connections safely.\r\n• Support keyboard reordering or equivalent accessible controls.\r\n• Prevent accidental loss if persistence fails.\r\n• Roll back the local visual order or clearly show unsaved failure if the server rejects the change.\r\n• Do not trigger drag when the user is selecting text or using controls inside a block.\r\n6.5 Undo and redo\r\nImplement practical undo/redo for editor operations if compatible with the current architecture. At minimum support recent local \r\nstructural and field changes before autosave commits. Do not fake undo buttons.\r\n6.6 Autosave and concurrency\r\nImplement debounced autosave with truthful status. Prevent one stale browser tab from silently overwriting newer changes.\r\nUse optimistic concurrency through a revision number, updatedAt token, ETag, or equivalent.\r\nWhen a conflict occurs\r\n• Stop claiming the draft is saved.\r\n• Preserve the user's unsaved local work.\r\n• Explain that the draft changed elsewhere.\r\n• Offer a safe reload/compare path consistent with project capabilities.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 20 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n20\r\n7. REQUIRED BLOCK REGISTRY AND BLOCK TYPES\r\nImplement a block registry containing, for each block type\r\n• Stable type identifier\r\n• Display name\r\n• Category\r\n• Icon\r\n• Description\r\n• Default configuration\r\n• Validation schema\r\n• Editor inspector component\r\n• Player renderer component\r\n• Preview renderer\r\n• Completion behavior\r\n• Asset field compatibility\r\n• Migration/schema version\r\nDo not scatter block-type knowledge throughout unrelated components.\r\nImplement the following Phase 1 block types.\r\n7.1 Narrative block\r\nPurpose: story narration or exposition.\r\nFields\r\n• Heading\r\n• Body rich text or safe formatted text\r\n• Optional narrator label\r\n• Optional background asset\r\n• Text alignment\r\n• Width style\r\n• Entrance animation\r\n• Continue behavior: automatic after animation, player button, Captain approval, or configured verification\r\nPlayer rendering\r\n• Cinematic but readable\r\n• Preserves paragraph spacing\r\n• Supports reduced motion\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 21 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n21\r\n7.2 Captain's Note block\r\nPurpose: handwritten letter, journal page, or Captain message.\r\nFields\r\n• Title\r\n• Body\r\n• Signature\r\n• Paper style\r\n• Ink style\r\n• Optional portrait/seal asset\r\n• Optional narration audio\r\n• Page-turn or ink-reveal animation\r\n• Continue button label\r\n• Completion mode\r\n7.3 Riddle block\r\nFields\r\n• Riddle title\r\n• Riddle text\r\n• Optional illustration\r\n• Optional answer configuration\r\n• Accepted answer variants\r\n• Case sensitivity\r\n• Whitespace normalization\r\n• Optional fuzzy matching threshold only if safely supported\r\n• Attempt limit optional\r\n• Hint sequence\r\n• Hint delay or Captain-controlled hint release\r\n• Wrong-answer feedback\r\n• Completion mode\r\nDo not expose correct answers to public player payloads before submission. Validate answers server-side.\r\n7.4 Information block\r\nPurpose: straightforward rules, context, safety notes, or instructions.\r\nFields\r\n• Heading\r\n• Body\r\n• Importance level\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 22 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n22\r\n• Icon\r\n• Optional asset\r\n• Acknowledgment required\r\n• Button label\r\n7.5 Travel Direction block\r\nFields\r\n• Heading\r\n• Direction text\r\n• Destination visibility\r\n• Compass heading optional\r\n• Region optional\r\n• Estimated travel time optional\r\n• Location reference\r\n• Map asset\r\n• Additional Captain-only notes\r\n• Continue/completion mode\r\n7.6 Location block\r\nPurpose: introduce or represent a reusable destination.\r\nFields\r\n• Location selection\r\n• Player-facing title override\r\n• Player-facing description override\r\n• Display asset\r\n• Map asset\r\n• Arrival instructions\r\n• Completion mode\r\n• Future vision reference collection\r\n• Future vision profile settings\r\n7.7 Arrival Check block\r\nPurpose: pause progression until the player is considered at the correct place.\r\nPhase 1 provider choices\r\n• Captain manual approval\r\n• Player confirmation button\r\n• Text phrase/code\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 23 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n23\r\nFuture provider choices visible as disabled or \"coming later\" only if that matches the product design:\r\n• Vision location\r\nConfiguration\r\n• Prompt shown to player\r\n• Pending-state text\r\n• Captain notification text\r\n• Verification provider\r\n• Timeout optional\r\n• Allow Captain override\r\n• Future reference collection\r\n7.8 Image block\r\nFields\r\n• Asset\r\n• Caption\r\n• Alt text\r\n• Display mode: inline, full-bleed, fullscreen, journal frame, map fragment, memory, background\r\n• Object fit/crop/focal point\r\n• Entrance motion\r\n• Optional pan/zoom\r\n• Duration\r\n• Completion mode\r\n7.9 Image Transformation block\r\nThis block is central to Phase 1.\r\nFields\r\n• Before asset\r\n• After asset\r\n• Optional intermediate assets or reserved future support\r\n• Transition preset\r\n• Duration\r\n• Hold-before duration\r\n• Hold-after duration\r\n• Audio cue\r\n• Caption or message revealed\r\n• Alignment data for before and after assets\r\n• Mobile crop/focal point\r\n• Completion mode\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 24 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n24\r\nRequired transition presets should include at least\r\n• Crossfade\r\n• Ink spread\r\n• Ancient carving reveal\r\n• Moonlight reveal\r\n• Fog reveal\r\n• Water wash\r\n• Magical glow\r\n• Camera push-in\r\nImplement the presets with the project's animation system. A preset may initially be composed from CSS, canvas, masks, \r\ngradients, image overlays, and existing animation libraries. Do not require creators to write code.\r\nAlignment editor\r\n• Show before and after images overlaid.\r\n• Provide opacity comparison slider.\r\n• Allow translate X/Y, scale, rotation, crop, and focal point adjustments for the after image relative to the before image.\r\n• Provide reset alignment.\r\n• Preview the full transition.\r\n• Persist alignment parameters.\r\n7.10 Cinematic block\r\nFields\r\n• Video asset\r\n• Poster asset\r\n• Audio behavior\r\n• Autoplay where permitted\r\n• Captions/subtitles asset optional\r\n• Skippable configuration\r\n• Minimum watch duration\r\n• Completion after playback or manual action\r\n• Fallback for browsers that block autoplay\r\n7.11 Audio block\r\nFields\r\n• Audio asset\r\n• Title\r\n• Optional transcript\r\n• Playback mode\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 25 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n25\r\n• Loop\r\n• Volume default\r\n• Require completion or allow background continuation\r\n• Fade in/out\r\nAudio may also be attached to other compatible blocks. Avoid double playback or orphaned audio when navigation changes.\r\n7.12 Artifact Reveal block\r\nFields\r\n• Artifact selection\r\n• Ordinary in-game object label\r\n• Reveal artwork\r\n• Reveal video optional\r\n• Lore title\r\n• Lore description\r\n• Reveal animation\r\n• Audio cue\r\n• Add to collection after reveal\r\n• Completion mode\r\n7.13 Hidden Message Reveal block\r\nFields\r\n• Base image\r\n• Revealed image or message layer\r\n• Message text optional\r\n• Reveal style\r\n• Duration\r\n• Audio\r\n• Completion mode\r\nThis may share rendering infrastructure with Image Transformation but remains a distinct semantic block for creator clarity.\r\n7.14 Collection Update block\r\nFields\r\n• Artifact or collection item\r\n• Quantity\r\n• Progress label\r\n• Total expected count optional\r\n• Celebration style\r\n• Persist to session inventory\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 26 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n26\r\n• Optional continue action\r\nThe operation must be idempotent. Re-entering or refreshing the block must not grant duplicates unless explicitly configured.\r\n7.15 Confirmation block\r\nFields\r\n• Prompt\r\n• Primary button label\r\n• Optional secondary action\r\n• Confirmation style\r\n• Captain override\r\n7.16 Choice block\r\nFields\r\n• Prompt\r\n• Two or more choices\r\n• Choice label\r\n• Optional description and image\r\n• Target block for each choice\r\n• Whether choice is reversible\r\n• Captain visibility\r\nThe Phase 1 canvas may show choices inside the inspector and outline even if a full node graph is deferred.\r\n7.17 Text Answer block\r\nFields\r\n• Prompt\r\n• Accepted answers\r\n• Normalization rules\r\n• Attempt behavior\r\n• Feedback\r\n• Hint integration\r\n• Completion on accepted answer\r\nValidate server-side and avoid exposing accepted answers to the player client.\r\n7.18 Captain Approval block\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

PREVIEW AND TESTING WORKFLOWS\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 33 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n33\r\n10.1 Block preview\r\nFrom any selected block, allow the creator to render only that block using its draft configuration and assets.\r\nRequirements\r\n• Isolated preview state\r\n• No real session mutation\r\n• Accurate desktop and mobile viewport preview\r\n• Reduced-motion preview option\r\n• Replay transition\r\n• View validation errors\r\n10.2 Play From Here\r\nCreate a temporary preview session pinned to the current draft revision.\r\nRequirements\r\n• Start from selected block\r\n• Optionally seed required prior variables/artifacts\r\n• Clearly label Preview Mode\r\n• Never appear as a real active player session\r\n• Never write to real player history, achievements, or completed-tale records\r\n• Allow Captain simulation in a separate preview control panel or embedded test drawer\r\n• Dispose or expire preview sessions\r\n10.3 Full Tale preview\r\nAllow a creator to play the entire draft without publishing, still isolated from real progress.\r\n10.4 Device preview\r\nAt minimum, provide desktop and common mobile viewport presets. Do not pretend a pixel-perfect emulator is a real device, \r\nbut make responsive testing convenient.\r\n11. DRAFT VALIDATION AND PUBLISHING\r\n11.1 Validation engine\r\nBefore publishing, validate the complete draft.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 34 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n34\r\nErrors that block publishing should include\r\n• Missing title or invalid slug\r\n• No chapters\r\n• Chapter without an entry block\r\n• Broken or missing default connections\r\n• Block references a deleted or unavailable asset\r\n• Required field missing\r\n• Choice target missing\r\n• Condition target missing\r\n• Unreachable required block\r\n• Obvious infinite loop where detectable\r\n• Missing Tale Complete endpoint unless intentionally configured otherwise\r\n• Invalid text-answer configuration\r\n• Unsupported media type\r\n• Asset processing failed\r\n• Future vision provider selected as active even though not available in Phase 1\r\n• Private/internal asset assigned to a public player field\r\nWarnings may include\r\n• Unused assets\r\n• Empty optional descriptions\r\n• No cover image\r\n• Very large media\r\n• No mobile crop\r\n• Missing alt text\r\n• Chapter estimated duration missing\r\n• Block cannot be reached except through Captain jump\r\nDisplay validation issues in a panel with links that focus the affected chapter, block, asset, location, or artifact.\r\n11.2 Publish flow\r\nPublishing must\r\n1. Broadcast catalog invalidation/update through the existing data-refresh mechanism where applicable.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 35 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n35\r\n11.3 Unpublished changes\r\nAfter publishing, any draft edit must visibly change the tale state to \"Published with unpublished changes\" rather than silently \r\naltering the published version.\r\n11.4 Version history\r\nProvide a version history page showing\r\n• Version\r\n• Publish timestamp\r\n• Publisher\r\n• Release notes\r\n• Current/non-current status\r\n• Number of active sessions pinned to version\r\n• Preview published version\r\n• Compare summary if feasible\r\n• Restore/copy published version into a new draft, without mutating the old version\r\n11.5 Catalog eligibility\r\nA published Tall Tale should appear on the player page only when\r\n• A current published version exists.\r\n• Visibility permits the current player.\r\n• It is not archived.\r\n• Availability rules, if any, pass.\r\nDraft-only tales must never appear.\r\n12. PLAYER EXPERIENCE INTEGRATION\r\n12.1 Dynamic tale catalog\r\nReplace or augment any hard-coded tale list with a real catalog query backed by published Tall Tales.\r\nEach player-facing card should support\r\n• Cover art\r\n• Title\r\n• Subtitle or description\r\n• Estimated duration\r\n• Player count\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 36 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n36\r\n• Status: new, in progress, completed, replayable, unavailable\r\n• Continue button when progress exists\r\n• Start button when eligible\r\n• Replay button when allowed\r\n• Version pinning handled internally\r\n12.2 New tale availability\r\nWhen an authorized creator publishes a new Tall Tale, it must become visible on the player page through the normal data \r\npath. No source-code edit, static JSON edit, manual route addition, or restart-specific hack should be necessary beyond \r\nnormal deployment/cache invalidation.\r\n12.3 Starting a tale\r\nStarting must\r\n• Create a real player session.\r\n• Pin it to the current published version.\r\n• Enter the first chapter and entry block.\r\n• Record history events.\r\n• Associate or invite a Captain according to current product rules.\r\n• Render the correct block through the shared player block registry.\r\n12.4 Runtime block rendering\r\nThe player runtime must render published block configurations, not Studio-only draft objects.\r\nBlock transitions should\r\n• Persist progress server-side.\r\n• Be idempotent.\r\n• Resume correctly after refresh.\r\n• Avoid replaying one-time grants.\r\n• Allow replaying presentation where explicitly permitted without re-completing progression.\r\n• Coordinate audio cleanup.\r\n• Respect reduced motion.\r\n12.5 History\r\nCompleted and prior sessions should remain viewable using the published version they used. Do not resolve old sessions \r\nagainst whichever draft happens to exist today.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 37 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n37\r\n13. CAPTAIN CONTROL INTEGRATION\r\n13.1 Captain dashboard\r\nShow\r\n• Available Tall Tales\r\n• Active sessions\r\n• Waiting-for-Captain sessions\r\n• Player names/party label where available\r\n• Current chapter\r\n• Current block\r\n• Time waiting\r\n• Last real event timestamp\r\n• Connection status if live connections exist\r\n• Preview sessions clearly separated\r\n13.2 Session control page\r\nShow\r\n• Tale and pinned version\r\n• Player/party\r\n• Current chapter and block\r\n• Current player-facing content summary\r\n• Pending verification request\r\n• Recent event timeline\r\n• Session inventory/artifacts\r\n• Variables relevant to debugging\r\n• Captain-only instructions from current block\r\n• Available actions\r\n13.3 Required Captain actions\r\nImplement\r\n• Approve pending Captain verification\r\n• Reject/request retry where supported\r\n• Manually satisfy a player-confirmation or future verification request through explicit override\r\n• Trigger a configured presentation event without duplicating progression\r\n• Pause/resume session\r\n• Roll back to previous safe block\r\n• Jump to a selected block with a warning and explicit reason\r\n• Re-send current block/state to connected player if live synchronization supports it\r\n• Release hint\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 38 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n38\r\n• Add Captain note to session history if supported\r\nEvery destructive or progression-changing action must\r\n• Require authorization.\r\n• Use a confirmation dialog where appropriate.\r\n• Record actor, reason, timestamp, source block, target block, and resulting state.\r\n• Be idempotent where applicable.\r\n13.4 Manual control through common verification\r\nCaptain approval must satisfy an existing pending verification request. Do not directly mutate currentBlock from the UI without \r\npassing through progression rules.\r\n13.5 Live synchronization\r\nUse the existing real-time mechanism if one exists. If none exists, implement the smallest reliable abstraction consistent with \r\nthe current stack, such as WebSocket, server-sent events, or short polling.\r\nRequired behavior\r\n• Player sees Captain approval without a full manual refresh.\r\n• Captain sees player progression and pending requests promptly.\r\n• Reconnection fetches canonical state.\r\n• Duplicate messages do not duplicate completion.\r\n• UI connection indicators are truthful.\r\n14. STORY RUNTIME AND PROGRESSION ENGINE\r\n14.1 Single authoritative engine\r\nCreate or refactor toward one progression service responsible for\r\n• Entering blocks\r\n• Creating verification requests\r\n• Accepting standardized verification outcomes\r\n• Completing blocks\r\n• Executing idempotent side effects\r\n• Applying variables\r\n• Granting artifacts\r\n• Resolving next connections\r\n• Completing chapters\r\n• Completing tales\r\n• Recording events\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 39 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n39\r\n• Returning the next canonical player state\r\nThe Studio preview, real player runtime, Captain controls, and future vision helper must all use this engine or a safely isolated \r\npreview instance of it.\r\n14.2 Idempotency\r\nAll completion and side-effect operations must tolerate retries.\r\nExamples\r\n• Duplicate Captain approval must not advance twice.\r\n• Browser refresh during Artifact Reveal must not grant the artifact twice.\r\n• Replayed helper event must not complete a later block.\r\n• A late response for a cancelled request must be ignored and recorded safely.\r\n14.3 Transaction boundaries\r\nWhere supported, complete a block, apply side effects, create the next block-entry event, update session state, and create the \r\nnext verification request within a safe transaction or equivalent consistency boundary.\r\n14.4 State reconstruction\r\nThe current session state should be explainable from persisted state and history. Do not rely only on transient client state.\r\n14.5 Unknown block types\r\nPublished content should be schema-versioned. If a client encounters an unknown block type:\r\n• Do not crash into a blank screen.\r\n• Show a controlled incompatibility message.\r\n• Record diagnostics.\r\n• Prevent unsafe progression.\r\n15. API AND SERVICE BOUNDARIES\r\nAdapt naming to the existing backend. SECURITY, PRIVACY, AND MEDIA SAFETY\r\n• Enforce authorization server-side.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 41 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n41\r\n• Validate all block configuration server-side.\r\n• Sanitize rich text or use a safe structured editor format.\r\n• Prevent path traversal in uploads.\r\n• Validate MIME type and file signatures where practical.\r\n• Store uploads under opaque keys.\r\n• Prevent draft/private media from leaking through public URLs or payloads.\r\n• Use signed URLs or authorized media endpoints if the existing storage model requires access control.\r\n• Rate limit answer submissions, Captain actions, upload endpoints, and future helper events as appropriate.\r\n• Do not log correct riddle answers, sensitive tokens, or full private asset URLs unnecessarily.\r\n• Record security-relevant Captain overrides.\r\n• Keep future vision evidence local by default in the eventual helper design. MIGRATION OF EXISTING CONTENT\r\nIf existing Tall Tale content is hard-coded, stored in static files, or represented by a simpler schema:\r\n• Create a migration or import path into the new Tall Tale, chapter, block, asset, and published-version model.\r\n• Preserve the current playable Forever Treasure experience.\r\n• Preserve current URLs or add safe redirects.\r\n• Preserve current active progress where feasible.\r\n• Do not require the user to manually rebuild existing content in the Studio merely to keep the site working.\r\n• Document any content that cannot be migrated automatically.\r\nCreate one valid editable draft corresponding to the current tale and one published version that reproduces the current player \r\nexperience as closely as practical.\r\n18. VISUAL AND INTERACTION REQUIREMENTS\r\n18.1 Studio visual language\r\nThe Studio should feel like a premium Cartographer's Table rather than a generic CMS.\r\nUse the existing design system and consider\r\n• Layered parchment surfaces\r\n• Subtle map-grid or chart lines\r\n• Brass/wood accents\r\n• Inked section labels\r\n• Restrained glass only where already used\r\n• Soft elevation and shadows\r\n• Clearly differentiated interactive controls\r\n• Animated but efficient panel transitions\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 42 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n42\r\nDo not sacrifice readability for theme.\r\n18.2 Motion\r\nStudio motion\r\n• Smooth panel opening\r\n• Clear drag lift and drop settling\r\n• Chapter collapse/expand\r\n• Asset drawer movement\r\n• Save-state transitions\r\n• Preview opening\r\nPlayer motion\r\n• More cinematic and deliberate\r\n• Transition durations configurable per block\r\n• Major image transformations capable of multi-second sequences\r\n• No blank waiting periods disguised as cinematic pacing\r\n18.3 Empty and error states\r\nDesign useful states for\r\n• No Tall Tales\r\n• No chapters\r\n• Empty chapter\r\n• No assets\r\n• Upload failed\r\n• Asset processing\r\n• Draft conflict\r\n• Validation failed\r\n• No published version\r\n• No active Captain sessions\r\n• Waiting for Captain\r\n• Connection lost\r\n• Preview session expired\r\n18.4 Confirmation dialogs\r\nUse explicit confirmation for\r\n• Archive tale\r\n• Delete referenced block\r\n• Delete/replace referenced asset\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 43 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n43\r\n• Publish\r\n• Rollback player session\r\n• Jump player session\r\n• Reset preview\r\n• Discard unsaved changes\r\n19. PERFORMANCE AND RELIABILITY\r\n• Lazy-load thumbnails and large media.\r\n• Avoid loading original full-resolution images into every asset grid card.\r\n• Paginate or virtualize large asset libraries.\r\n• Debounce search and autosave.\r\n• Cache published catalog safely with explicit invalidation.\r\n• Ensure block editor rerenders remain manageable with long tales.\r\n• Do not upload the same file repeatedly during autosave.\r\n• Resume or safely retry interrupted uploads where practical.\r\n• Use background derivative generation if the current stack supports jobs; otherwise return a truthful processing state.\r\n• Never block the entire editor while one thumbnail processes.\r\n20. TESTING REQUIREMENTS\r\nAdd automated tests using the repository's existing test stack.\r\n20.1 Unit tests\r\nCover\r\n• Block schema validation\r\n• Block registry behavior\r\n• Slug generation/validation\r\n• Draft validation\r\n• Connection resolution\r\n• Text-answer normalization\r\n• Verification-provider dispatch\r\n• Idempotency handling\r\n• Artifact grant idempotency\r\n• Condition evaluation\r\n• Published-version immutability\r\n• Catalog eligibility\r\n20.2 Service/integration tests\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 44 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n44\r\nCover\r\n• Create tale -> add chapter -> add blocks -> upload asset -> publish -> player catalog displays tale\r\n• Draft edits after publishing do not alter published session behavior\r\n• Start session pins correct version\r\n• Captain approval satisfies pending request and advances once\r\n• Duplicate approval does not advance twice\r\n• Player confirmation uses same verification outcome path\r\n• Simulated vision match uses same verification outcome path\r\n• Stale simulated vision event is rejected\r\n• Wrong-block simulated event is rejected\r\n• Artifact reveal refresh does not duplicate collection item\r\n• Preview session does not appear in real player history\r\n• Asset replacement preserves block references\r\n• Deleting a referenced asset is blocked or safely handled\r\n• Current existing tale still plays after migration\r\n20.3 End-to-end tests\r\nWhere the project supports browser E2E testing, cover the main golden path\r\n1. Creator opens Studio.\r\n2. Creates Tall Tale.\r\n3. Creates first chapter.\r\n4. Drags Narrative, Riddle, Arrival Check, Image Transformation, Artifact Reveal, and Tale Complete blocks into the timeline.\r\n5. Uploads before and after images.\r\n6. Configures alignment and previews transformation.\r\n7. Validates and publishes.\r\n8. Opens player catalog and sees the tale.\r\n9. Starts a session.\r\n10. Player reaches Arrival Check.\r\n11. Captain sees pending request and approves.\r\n12. Player receives transition without manual refresh.\r\n13. Image transformation plays.\r\n14. Artifact is granted once.\r\n15. Tale completes and appears in history.\r\n20.4 Accessibility tests\r\nAt minimum\r\n• Keyboard navigation through Studio controls\r\n• Accessible names for icon buttons\r\n• Focus handling in dialogs and drawers\r\n• Drag-and-drop alternative controls\r\n• Alt-text validation for player-facing images\r\n• Reduced-motion behavior\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 45 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n45\r\n• Status announcements for saving, publishing, errors, and Captain approval\r\n20.5 Migration tests\r\n• Existing data migrates successfully.\r\n• Rollback or backup path is documented.\r\n• Existing published tale remains playable.\r\n• No orphaned current sessions are created.\r\n21. OBSERVABILITY AND DIAGNOSTICS\r\nAdd structured diagnostics consistent with the project\r\n• Draft save failures\r\n• Publish validation failures\r\n• Publish completion\r\n• Asset upload/processing failures\r\n• Session progression failures\r\n• Verification request creation and resolution\r\n• Captain overrides\r\n• Duplicate/idempotent event handling\r\n• Unknown block type\r\n• Real-time connection failures\r\nDo not log sensitive answer values or private tokens.\r\nCreate a creator-visible diagnostics area or clear error details where appropriate, not merely console errors.\r\n22. DOCUMENTATION\r\nAdd or update project documentation covering\r\n• Tall Tale Studio overview\r\n• Domain model\r\n• Block registry and how to add a new block type\r\n• Draft versus published versions\r\n• Asset storage and derivatives\r\n• Player session/version pinning\r\n• Captain control flow\r\n• Verification provider abstraction\r\n• Future local helper pairing/event contract\r\n• Development verification simulator\r\n• Migration of existing content\r\n• Required environment variables\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 46 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n46\r\n• Storage configuration\r\n• Test commands\r\n• Build/run commands\r\nInclude a concise \"Future Vision Helper Integration\" document with\r\n• Pairing concept\r\n• Event schema\r\n• Security assumptions\r\n• Request lifecycle\r\n• Idempotency rules\r\n• Match/not-match/uncertain semantics\r\n• Why the helper may satisfy only an existing pending verification request\r\n• Which Phase 1 tables/services/endpoints it will use\r\n23. IMPLEMENTATION ORDER\r\nUse the following order unless repository realities require a documented adjustment.\r\nPhase A - Audit and domain foundation\r\n• Inspect current architecture.\r\n• Define migrations and model relationships.\r\n• Add block registry and schemas.\r\n• Add draft/published distinction.\r\n• Add asset storage abstraction.\r\n• Add progression and verification abstractions.\r\nPhase B - Runtime integration\r\n• Migrate or adapt existing tale content.\r\n• Render published blocks in the player experience.\r\n• Implement session pinning and progression events.\r\n• Route Captain approval through common verification.\r\nPhase C - Studio shell\r\n• Studio landing page.\r\n• New tale flow.\r\n• Chapter/timeline editor.\r\n• Inspector.\r\n• Autosave and validation status.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 47 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n47\r\nPhase D - Asset/location/artifact tools\r\n• Upload pipeline.\r\n• Asset browser/picker.\r\n• Collections and roles.\r\n• Location library.\r\n• Artifact library.\r\n• Image transformation alignment editor.\r\nPhase E - Preview and publishing\r\n• Block preview.\r\n• Play From Here.\r\n• Draft validation.\r\n• Immutable publishing.\r\n• Version history.\r\n• Dynamic player catalog.\r\nPhase F - Captain and future-provider seam\r\n• Captain dashboard/session control integration.\r\n• Live state updates.\r\n• Development verification simulator.\r\n• Helper event contract and docs.\r\nPhase G - QA and polish\r\n• Automated tests.\r\n• Responsive checks.\r\n• Accessibility checks.\r\n• Migration validation.\r\n• Visual polish.\r\n• Documentation.\r\nDo not call the task complete after producing only Phase A or UI scaffolding. Complete the end-to-end Phase 1 scope.\r\n24. REQUIRED ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA\r\nThe implementation is complete only when all of the following are true.\r\nStudio and authoring\r\n• An authorized creator can open a dedicated Studio route.\r\n• A creator can create, rename, duplicate, and archive a Tall Tale.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 48 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n48\r\n• A creator can create and reorder chapters.\r\n• A creator can drag new blocks into the story and reorder them.\r\n• Every required Phase 1 block type is registered, editable, validated, previewable, and renderable, or any deliberately \r\ndeferred secondary block is explicitly documented with no broken placeholder controls.\r\n• Autosave is real and truthful.\r\n• Conflicts do not silently overwrite newer work.\r\nAssets\r\n• A creator can drag-and-drop multiple images.\r\n• Uploaded files persist.\r\n• Thumbnails/optimized variants are generated or truthfully marked as processing.\r\n• Assets are searchable and reusable.\r\n• Assets support tags, roles, collections, and usage tracking.\r\n• Replacing an asset preserves logical references.\r\n• Referenced assets cannot be silently destroyed.\r\n• Before/after transformation images can be aligned and previewed.\r\nPublishing\r\n• Server-side validation prevents broken publication.\r\n• Publishing creates an immutable version.\r\n• Draft changes after publishing do not mutate the published version.\r\n• Version history is visible.\r\n• The newly published tale appears on the player catalog through real data.\r\nPlayer\r\n• The player can start the new tale.\r\n• The session pins the published version.\r\n• Blocks render from the published block registry.\r\n• Progress survives refresh.\r\n• One-time effects remain idempotent.\r\n• Completion appears in player history.\r\nCaptain\r\n• The Captain sees active sessions and current block.\r\n• The Captain sees pending verification.\r\n• Captain approval advances the player through the standard verification flow.\r\n• Reject/retry and override behavior are recorded.\r\n• Rollback and jump are controlled and audited.\r\n• Player state updates without requiring an unreliable manual refresh.\r\nFuture vision compatibility\r\n• Verification requests are persisted.\r\n• Provider types include future vision providers.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 49 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n49\r\n• Standard event schema is implemented or formally versioned in code.\r\n• Development simulator can submit match, not-match, and uncertain events.\r\n• Simulated match advances only the correct pending request.\r\n• Duplicate, stale, wrong-session, wrong-version, and wrong-block events are rejected or safely ignored.\r\n• No helper can send an unrestricted \"advance\" command.\r\n• Future reference images and negative references can already be stored in collections.\r\nQuality\r\n• Existing tale behavior is preserved or migrated.\r\n• Tests pass.\r\n• Lint/type checks pass.\r\n• No obvious dead controls remain.\r\n• No fake saved/published/connected claims remain.\r\n• Responsive layouts work at target desktop and mobile sizes.\r\n• Accessibility basics are verified.\r\n• Documentation is complete.\r\n25. DELIVERABLES AND FINAL REPORT\r\nWhen implementation is finished, provide a precise final report containing\r\n1. Outcome summary.\r\n2. Repository path and branch.\r\n3. Starting and ending commit identifiers if commits are permitted.\r\n4. Every migration added and its purpose.\r\n5. Major architecture decisions.\r\n6. Exact Studio routes.\r\n7. Exact player routes affected.\r\n8. Exact Captain routes affected.\r\n9. Block types implemented.\r\n10. Asset storage behavior.\r\n11. Draft/publish/version behavior.\r\n12. Existing-content migration result.\r\n13. Future vision-helper extension points.\r\n14. Development simulator instructions.\r\n15. Test commands and results.\r\n16. Build/lint/type-check results.\r\n17. Known limitations that are real and specific.\r\n18. Manual validation steps.\r\n19. Files changed, grouped by subsystem.\r\n20. Whether any unrelated pre-existing changes were present and whether they were left untouched.\r\nDo not claim success for tests, migrations, publishing, image processing, live synchronization, or player integration unless \r\nactually verified.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 50 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n50\r\n26. SOURCE CONTROL AND CHANGE DISCIPLINE\r\n• Work only in the intended repository.\r\n• Inspect git status before changing anything.\r\n• Preserve unrelated pre-existing changes.\r\n• Do not reset, discard, overwrite, or \"clean up\" unrelated work.\r\n• Keep migrations additive and reversible where practical.\r\n• Use focused commits if repository policy permits commits.\r\n• Do not push, deploy, tag, or merge unless separately authorized.\r\n• Do not alter production secrets or credentials.\r\n• Do not replace current data with sample data.\r\n• Seed/demo content must be clearly separated from real content.\r\n27. FINAL PRODUCT INTENT\r\nThis platform should let a creator build a Tall Tale as a sequence of meaningful, reusable story blocks instead of hand-coding \r\nevery adventure. The Studio, player experience, Captain controls, and future vision helper are not separate products. They are \r\ndifferent interfaces over one shared, versioned story model and one authoritative progression engine.\r\nThe correct architectural relationship is\r\nTall Tale Studio\r\n  -> creates a draft story graph and reusable assets\r\n  -> validates and publishes an immutable version\r\n  -> automatically appears in the player catalog\r\n  -> starts version-pinned player sessions\r\n  -> produces pending interaction or verification requests\r\n  -> receives player, Captain, timer, or future vision outcomes through one standardized \r\nverification path\r\n  -> advances through one authoritative progression engine\r\n  -> records an auditable history\r\n  -> preserves completed adventures and memories exactly as they were played\r\nImplement the full Phase 1 foundation with enough polish that it feels intentional now and enough modularity that automatic \r\nvisual verification can be attached later without rebuilding the platform.\r\nEND OF IMPLEMENTATION PROMPT\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 51 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n51\r\nREFERENCE ARCHITECTURE AT A GLANCE\r\nSubsystem Responsibility\r\nTall Tale Studio Creates and validates editable draft content, reusable \r\nassets, locations, artifacts, blocks, and connections.\r\nPublishing service Freezes an immutable, schema-versioned release and \r\nmakes eligible tales available to the player catalog.\r\nPlayer runtime Starts version-pinned sessions and renders published \r\nblocks through the shared block registry.\r\nCaptain controls Observes sessions and resolves pending verification \r\nrequests through audited manual actions.\r\nVerification layer Normalizes Captain, player, timer, text-answer, simulator, \r\nand future vision outcomes.\r\nProgression engine Completes blocks idempotently, applies side effects, records \r\nhistory, and determines the next canonical state.\r\nFuture helper app Pairs to one session and submits evidence only for an \r\nexisting pending vision request. It cannot issue arbitrary \r\nadvancement commands.\r\nCritical invariant: No interface advances a session by directly editing the current block. Every outcome passes through the \r\nsame verification and progression services.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

The Forever Treasure Companion\n\nProduction-oriented foundation and fully automated local demo for a private nautical-fantasy date-night Tall Tale. All committed story material is generic development seed content; no final surprise content belongs in this public repository.\n\n## Current phase\n\nPhase three rebuilds that companion as a cinematic, event-synchronized experience. A shared animation director coordinates the harbor arrival, physical invitation and authentication, page-flip journal, chapter ceremonies, chart routes, artifacts, side quests, log, sealed finale, and Quartermaster instruments without allowing presentation timing to outrun server truth. All committed content remains fictional development seed material.\n\n## Stack\n\n- Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, strict TypeScript\n- Prisma 6.19 with SQLite for local/test and an equivalent MySQL production schema\n- GSAP 3.15 for orchestrated scenes and SVG/text work; Motion 12 for React interaction and presence\n- StPageFlip for the journal; local Rive and Lottie runtimes with explicit static fallbacks\n- bcrypt password/access-code hashing, server-side database sessions, CSRF tokens, and rate limiting\n- Vitest and Playwright\n\n## Reproducible setup\n\nRequirements: Windows PowerShell 5.1+, Node.js 24, npm 11, and Git. MySQL 8 is only required for production parity; the supported local/demo/test strategy is SQLite.\n\n### Fastest Windows demo\n\nAfter cloning, double-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd`, or run:\n\n```powershell\nnpm run dev:full\n```\n\nThe launcher creates an ignored `.env` when absent, installs the exact lockfile, generates Prisma, applies migrations, seeds generic disposable data, starts the app, checks health, and prints the URLs and credentials. Network/UNC workspaces are mirrored to an ignored local runtime under `%LOCALAPPDATA%` because Node and SQLite are unreliable on network shares. Stop it with `npm run dev:stop`.\n\n- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\n- Player phrase: `development-moonwake`\n- GM: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\n- GM development[REDACTED: LOGIN CREDENTIAL]\n\nThese values are disposable local defaults, never production credentials. To opt into LAN access, run `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan`; Windows Firewall and network policy still determine whether another device can connect.\n\n### Portable manual setup\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\ncd treasurehuntSoT\nnpm ci\ncp .env.example .env\nnpm run db:generate\nnpm run db:migrate\nnpm run db:seed\nnpm run dev\n```\n\nSet unique local values in `.env`; the seedâ€™s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\n\n## Commands\n\n| Command                           | Purpose                                                                |\n| --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| `npm run dev:full`                | Idempotent setup, migration, seed, health check, and background server |\n| `npm run dev:stop`                | Safely stop only the recorded development process                      |\n| `npm run validate`                | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof          |\n| `npm run dev`                     | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                        |\n| `npm run build`                   | Production build                                                       |\n| `npm start`                       | Start production build                                                 |\n| `npm test`                        | Deterministic domain, director, ownership, asset, and page tests       |\n| `npm run assets:validate`         | Validate local Lottie/Rive contracts and fallback paths                |\n| `npm run test:e2e`                | Five Chromium/WebKit checks plus one Chromium mutation workflow        |\n| `npm run db:migrate`              | Apply local SQLite migrations                                          |\n| `npm run db:seed`                 | Load replaceable development content                                   |\n| `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage` | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset                |\n| `npm run db:generate:mysql`       | Generate production MySQL client                                       |\n\nFull setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Animation ownership, scene contracts, assets, performance, and the development lab are indexed from [animation architecture](docs/animation/architecture.md).\n\n## Repository workflow\n\n`main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\n\n## Important limitations\n\nThe full story, final Rive artwork/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural. The sole third-party visual sample is MIT-licensed, local, documented, and development-only; production uses original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\n\n## Next steps\n\nAdd CI for the complete validation gate, exercise the parallel MySQL schema in an integration environment, and replace process-local SSE fan-out before multi-instance deployment. Real surprise content remains blocked until repository visibility is verified private.\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Float    @default(50)\n  displayY    Float    @default(50)\n  assemblyGroup String?\n  assemblyPosition String?\n  connectedArtifactKey String?\n  chapterOrdinal Int?\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  awards      ArtifactAward[]\n  @@unique([campaignId, key])\n}\n\nmodel ArtifactAward {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  artifactId  String\n  eventId     String?  @unique\n  awardedAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  artifact    Artifact @relation(fields: [artifactId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([campaignId, artifactId])\n}\n\nmodel SideQuest {\n  id @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  objectives  SideQuestObjective[]\n  @@unique([campaignId, key])\n}\n\nmodel SideQuestObjective {\n  id          String   @id onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([sideQuestId, ordinal])\n}\n\nmodel JournalEntry {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  title       String\n  body        String\n  kind  exactness   String   @default(\"APPROXIMATE\")\n  chapterOrdinal Int?\n  sideQuestKey String?\n  x           Float\n  y           Float\n  mobileX     Float?\n  mobileY     Float?\n  revealedAt ordinal])\n}\n\nmodel ProgressEvent {\n  id             String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId     String\n  type           String\n  payload        String\n  actor          String\n  sequence   Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([campaignId, sequence])\n  @@index([campaignId, releaseAt])\n}\n\nmodel CampaignSnapshot {\n  id @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([campaignId, sequence])\n}\n\nmodel AdminAuditLog {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId Cascade)\n  user        GameMasterUser? @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)\n  @@index([campaignId, createdAt])\n}\n\nmodel AudioPreference {\n  id             String   @id @default(cuid())\n  playerAccessId String   @unique\n  muted          Boolean  @default(false)\n  masterVolume   Float    @default(0.45)\n  ambientVolume  Float    @default(0.25)\n  effectsVolume  Float    @default(0.55)\n  motionMode     String   @default(\"SYSTEM\")\n  textScale      Float    @default(1)\n  ambientEffects Boolean  @default(true)\n  textureIntensity Float  @default(1)\n  fullscreenPreferred Boolean @default(false)\n  updatedAt      DateTime @updatedAt\n  playerAccess   PlayerAccess @relation(fields: [playerAccessId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n}\n\nmodel ContentVersion {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  key         String\n  version     Int\n  checksum    String\n  publishedAt DateTime?\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  @@unique([key, version])\n}\n\nmodel SaveStateSnapshot {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  sequence    Int\n  state       String\n  reason      String\n  createdAt LoginAttempt {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  fingerprint String\n  succeeded   Boolean\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  @@index([fingerprint, createdAt])\n}\n\r\ngenerator client {\n  provider = \"prisma-client-js\"\n}\n\ndatasource db {\n  provider = \"mysql\"\n  url      = env(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n}\n\nmodel Campaign {\n  id              String   @id @default(cuid())\n  slug            String   @unique\n  title           String\n  status          String   @default(\"PAUSED\")\n  accessCodeHash  String\n  currentSequence Int      @default(0)\n  finaleState     String   @default(\"SEALED\")\n  finaleTeaser    String?\n  finaleRequirements String @default(\"[]\")\n  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt\n  chapters        Chapter[]\n  artifacts       Artifact[]\n  mapLocations    MapLocation[]\n  mapRoutes       MapRoute[]\n  sideQuests      SideQuest[]\n  events          ProgressEvent[]\n  snapshots lastSeenAt  DateTime?\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  audio       AudioPreference?\n  viewedContent ViewedContent[]\n  @@index([campaignId])\n}\n\nmodel GameMasterUser {\n  id           String   @id @default(cuid())\n  username     String   @unique\n  passwordHash String\n  createdAt expiresAt DateTime\n  createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n  user      GameMasterUser @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@index([userId, expiresAt])\n}\n\nmodel Chapter {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  ordinal     Int\n  state       String   @default(\"LOCKED\")\n  contentId   String\n  revealedAt  DateTime?\n  solvedAt    DateTime?\n  safeTeaser  String?\n  relatedMapKey String?\n  relatedArtifactKey String?\n  relatedSideQuestKey String?\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  content     ChapterContent @relation(fields: [contentId], references: [id])\n  clues       Clue[]\n  hints Clue {\n  id        String   @id @default(cuid())\n  chapterId String\n  ordinal   Int\n  body      String\n  chapter   Chapter @relation(fields: [chapterId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n String?\n  chapterOrdinal Int?\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  awards      ArtifactAward[]\n  @@unique([campaignId, key])\n}\n\nmodel @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  artifact    Artifact @relation(fields: [artifactId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([campaignId, artifactId])\n}\n\nmodel SideQuest {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  key         String\n  title       String\n  state       String   @default(\"UNDISCOVERED\")\n @@index([campaignId, releaseAt])\n}\n\nmodel CampaignSnapshot {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  sequence    Int\n  version     Int      @default(1)\n  state campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  user        GameMasterUser? @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)\n masterVolume   Float    @default(0.45)\n  ambientVolume  Float    @default(0.25)\n  effectsVolume  Float    @default(0.55)\n  motionMode     String   @default(\"SYSTEM\")\n  textScale      Float    @default(1)\n  ambientEffects Boolean  @default(true)\n  textureIntensity Float  @default(1)\n  fullscreenPreferred Boolean @default(false)\n  updatedAt      DateTime @updatedAt\n  playerAccess   PlayerAccess @relation(fields: [playerAccessId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n}\n\nmodel ViewedContent {\n  id             String   @id @default(cuid())\n  playerAccessId String\n  contentType    String\n  contentKey     String\n  viewedAt       DateTime @default(now())\n  playerAccess   PlayerAccess @relation(fields: [playerAccessId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([playerAccessId, contentType, contentKey])\n  @@index([playerAccessId, viewedAt])\n}\n\nmodel ContentVersion {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  key         String\n  version
