\"scripts/validate-animation-assets.ts\")\nInvoke-ValidationStep -Name \"Verifying seeded database\" -Arguments @(\"node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs\", \"scripts/verify-database.ts\")\nInvoke-ValidationStep -Name \"Running browser acceptance tests\" -Arguments @(\"node_modules/playwright/cli.js\", \"test\")\nInvoke-ValidationStep -Name \"Verifying accepted database state\" -Arguments @(\"node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs\", \"scripts/verify-database.ts\", \"--acceptance\")\nInvoke-ValidationStep -Name \"Creating production build\" -Arguments @(\"node_modules/next/dist/bin/next\", \"build\")\n\nfunction Test-ProductionStart {\n    param([int]$Port)\n    $stdout = Join-Path $runtimeRoot \"artifacts\\validation\\production-$Port.out.log\"\n -FilePath $node -ArgumentList \"node_modules/next/dist/bin/next\", \"start\", \"-H\", \"127.0.0.1\", \"-p\", \"$Port\" -WorkingDirectory $runtimeRoot -WindowStyle Hidden -RedirectStandardOutput $showcaseResponse = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri \"http://127.0.0.1:$Port/dev/animations\" -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 10\n            $showcaseStatus = [int]$showcaseResponse.StatusCode\n        } catch {\n            if ($_.Exception.Response) { $showcaseStatus = [int]$_.Exception.Response.StatusCode }\n            else { throw }\n        }\n        if ($showcaseStatus -ne 404) { throw \"Development animation showcase returned HTTP $showcaseStatus in production.\" }\n    }\n    finally { if (-not $process.HasExited) { Stop-Process -Id $process.Id -Force; [void]$process.WaitForExit(10000) } }\n}\n\nWrite-Host \"`n==> Proving production restart safety\" -ForegroundColor Cyan\nTest-ProductionStart -Port 3200\nTest-ProductionStart -Port 3200\nWrite-Host \"`nFull validation passed. ## Tool Result: call_ellzAHNK2xr9gViILj531J7f
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-17T21:29:09.085Z

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.0 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.9 seconds\nOutput:\nimport { PrismaClient } from \"@prisma/client\";\n\nconst db = new PrismaClient();\nconst acceptance = process.argv.includes(\"--acceptance\");\nconst preset = process.argv.includes(\"--preset\");\n\nfunction assert(condition: unknown, message: string): asserts condition {\n  if (!condition) throw new Error(`Database verification failed: ${message}`);\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  const campaign = await db.campaign.findUnique({\n    where: { slug: \"development-forever-treasure\" },\n    include: {\n      chapters: true,\n      artifacts: true,\n      mapLocations: true,\n      mapRoutes: true,\n      sideQuests: true,\n      events: { orderBy: { sequence: \"asc\" } },\n      snapshots: true,\n      awards: true,\n      auditLogs: true,\n      saveStates: true,\n      playerAccesses: true,\n    },\n  });\n  assert(campaign, \"the development campaign is missing\");\n  assert(campaign.chapters.length >= 5, \"the complete development chapter set is required\");\n  assert(campaign.artifacts.length >= 5, \"the complete development artifact set is required\");\n  assert(campaign.mapLocations.length >= 5, \"the complete development map set is required\");\n  assert(campaign.mapRoutes.length >= 2, \"development route segments are required\");\n  assert(campaign.sideQuests.length >= 4, \"development side-quest states are required\");\n  assert(campaign.playerAccesses.length === 1, \"exactly one development player access is required\");\n  assert(campaign.currentSequence === campaign.events.length, \"campaign sequence must equal its ordered event count\");\n  assert(\n    campaign.events.every((event, index) => event.sequence === index + 1),\n    \"event sequences must be contiguous\",\n  );\n  assert(\n    new Set(campaign.events.map((event) => event.sequence)).size === campaign.events.length,\n    \"event sequences must be unique\",\n  );\n  assert(campaign.snapshots.length === campaign.events.length, \"every progression event must have a campaign snapshot\");\n  assert(campaign.auditLogs.length === campaign.events.length, \"every progression event must have an audit record\");\n  assert(campaign.awards.length <= campaign.artifacts.length, \"artifact awards must remain unique\");\n\n  if (!acceptance && !preset) {\n    assert(campaign.currentSequence === 0, \"a fresh validation database must start at sequence zero\");\n    assert(campaign.chapters[0].state === \"LOCKED\", \"a fresh chapter must be locked\");\n  } else if (acceptance) {\n    const eventTypes = new Set(campaign.events.map((event) => event.type));\n    for (const expected of [\n      \"CHAPTER_PREPARED\",\n      \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\",\n      \"ARTIFACT_AWARDED\",\n      \"CAMPAIGN_PAUSED\",\n      \"CAMPAIGN_RESUMED\",\n      \"CHAPTER_SOLVED\",\n      \"STATE_REVERTED\",\n    ]) {\n      assert(eventTypes.has(expected), `acceptance run did not record ${expected}`);\n    }\n    assert(campaign.awards.length >= 1, \"acceptance run must persist an artifact award\");\n    assert(\n      campaign.mapLocations.some((location) => location.revealedAt),\n      \"acceptance run must persist a released map location\",\n    );\n    assert(campaign.saveStates.length > 0, \"acceptance run must retain undo save states\");\n  }\n\n  console.log(\n    `Database verified: ${campaign.events.length} events, ${campaign.auditLogs.length} audit entries, sequence ${campaign.currentSequence}.`,\n  );\n}\n\nmain().finally(() => db.$disconnect());\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\forever-treasure-companion\\scripts\\verify-database.ts\n@@\n   assert(campaign.awards.length <= campaign.artifacts.length, \"artifact awards must remain unique\");\n+  const studioTale = await db.tallTale.findUnique({\n+    where: { slug: \"development-studio-voyage\" },\n+    include: {\n+      drafts: { include: { chapters: { include: { blocks: true } } } },\n+      versions: true,\n+    },\n+  });\n+  assert(studioTale, \"the migrated Studio development tale is missing\");\n+  assert(studioTale.drafts.length === 1, \"the migrated tale requires one editable draft\");\n+  assert(studioTale.versions.length === 1, \"the migrated tale requires one immutable published version\");\n+  assert(studioTale.latestPublishedVersionId === studioTale.versions[0].id, \"the player catalog must point to the current version\");\n+  assert(studioTale.drafts[0].chapters.length === 2, \"the migrated Studio draft requires two chapters\");\n+  assert(studioTale.drafts[0].chapters.flatMap((chapter) => chapter.blocks).some((block) => block.blockType === \"taleComplete\"), \"the migrated Studio draft requires a Tale Complete endpoint\");\n+  const snapshot = JSON.parse(studioTale.versions[0].contentSnapshot) as { schemaVersion?: number; chapters?: unknown[] };\n+  assert(snapshot.schemaVersion === 1 && Array.isArray(snapshot.chapters), \"the published Studio snapshot must be schema-versioned\");\n@@\n-    `Database verified: ${campaign.events.length} events, ${campaign.auditLogs.length} audit entries, sequence ${campaign.currentSequence}.`,\n+    `Database verified: ${campaign.events.length} legacy events, ${campaign.auditLogs.length} audit entries, sequence ${campaign.currentSequence}; Studio version ${studioTale.versions[0].versionLabel}.`,\n*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

<form onSubmit={(event) => { event.preventDefault(); void act(\"answer\"); }}><label><span>Your answer</span><input value={answer} onChange={(event) => setAnswer(event.target.value)} autoComplete=\"off\" required /></label><button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy}>{busy ? \"Testing the answerâ€¦\" : \"Submit answer\"}</button></form> : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"timer\" ? <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy || waitRemaining > 0} onClick={() => void act(\"timer\")}>{waitRemaining > 0 ? `Continue in ${Math.ceil(waitRemaining / 1000)}s` : \"Continue\"}</button> : choices.length ? <div className=\"choice-actions\"><p>{String(state.block?.configuration.prompt ?? \"Choose a course.\")}</p>{choices.map((choice) => <button key={String(choice.id ?? choice.label)} disabled={busy || !choice.targetBlockId} onClick={() => void act(\"choice\", { targetBlockId: choice.targetBlockId })}><strong>{String(choice.label ?? \"Choice\")}</strong>{choice.description && <span>{String(choice.description)}</span>}</button>)}</div> : <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy || state.block?.blockType === \"condition\" || state.block?.blockType === \"setVariable\"} onClick={() => void act(\"continue\")}>{busy ? \"Writing progressâ€¦\" : String(state.block?.configuration.buttonLabel ?? state.block?.configuration.primaryLabel ?? \"Continue\")}</button>}{error && <p className=\"runtime-error\" role=\"alert\">{error}</p>}</div></section><footer><span>Version {state.session.versionId.startsWith(\"draft:\") ? <form onSubmit={(event) => { event.preventDefault(); void act(\"answer\"); }}><label><span>Your answer</span><input value={answer} onChange={(event) => setAnswer(event.target.value)} autoComplete=\"off\" required /></label><button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy}>{busy ? \"Testing the answer…\" : \"Submit answer\"}</button></form> : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"timer\" ? <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy || waitRemaining > 0} onClick={() => void act(\"timer\")}>{waitRemaining > 0 ? `Continue in ${Math.ceil(waitRemaining / 1000)}s` : \"Continue\"}</button> : choices.length ? <div className=\"choice-actions\"><p>{String(state.block?.configuration.prompt ?? \"Choose a course.\")}</p>{choices.map((choice) => <button key={String(choice.id ?? choice.label)} disabled={busy || !choice.targetBlockId} onClick={() => void act(\"choice\", { targetBlockId: choice.targetBlockId })}><strong>{String(choice.label ?? \"Choice\")}</strong>{choice.description && <span>{String(choice.description)}</span>}</button>)}</div> : <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy || state.block?.blockType === \"condition\" || state.block?.blockType === \"setVariable\"} onClick={() => void act(\"continue\")}>{busy ? \"Writing progress…\" : String(state.block?.configuration.buttonLabel ?? state.block?.configuration.primaryLabel ?? \"Continue\")}</button>}{error && <p className=\"runtime-error\" role=\"alert\">{error}</p>}</div></section><footer><span>Version {state.session.versionId.startsWith(\"draft:\") ? \"draft preview\" : state.session.versionId.slice(0, 8)}</span><span>Event sequence {state.session.currentSequence}</span></footer></main>;\n+  return <main className={`player-runtime ${state.session.previewMode ? \"preview-mode\" : \"\"}`}><header><div><p className=\"eyebrow\">{state.session.previewMode ? \"Preview Mode — no real progress\" : state.chapter?.title ?? \"Tall Tale\"}</p><h1>{state.tale.title}</h1></div><div className={`runtime-connection ${connected ? \"connected\" : \"reconnecting\"}`}><i />{connected ? \"Captain channel connected\" : \"Reconnecting to canonical state\"}</div></header><section className=\"runtime-stage\">{state.block ? <PublishedBlockView block={state.block} assets={state.assets} /> : <article className=\"runtime-block unknown-block\"><h2>No safe story block is available.</h2></article>}<div className=\"runtime-actions\" aria-live=\"polite\">{state.session.status === \"COMPLETED\" ? <><strong>This Tall Tale is complete.</strong><Link className=\"brass-button\" href={`/play/${state.tale.slug}/history`}>View voyage history</Link><Link href=\"/tales\">Choose another tale</Link></> : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"captainManual\" ? <div className=\"awaiting-captain\"><span>◷</span><strong>{String(state.block?.configuration.waitingText ?? \"Awaiting the Captain's approval…\")}</strong><p>The player view will update automatically when the Captain responds.</p></div> : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"textAnswer\" ? <form onSubmit={(event) => { event.preventDefault(); void act(\"answer\"); }}><label><span>Your answer</span><input value={answer} onChange={(event) => setAnswer(event.target.value)} autoComplete=\"off\" required /></label><button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy}>{busy ? \"Testing the answer…\" : \"Submit answer\"}</button></form> : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"timer\" ? <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy || waitRemaining > 0} onClick={() => void act(\"timer\")}>{waitRemaining > 0 ? `Continue in ${Math.ceil(waitRemaining / 1000)}s` : \"Continue\"}</button> : choices.length ? <div className=\"choice-actions\"><p>{String(state.block?.configuration.prompt ?? \"Choose a course.\")}</p>{choices.map((choice) => <button key={String(choice.id ?? choice.label)} disabled={busy || !choice.targetBlockId} onClick={() => void act(\"choice\", { targetBlockId: choice.targetBlockId })}><strong>{String(choice.label ?? \"Choice\")}</strong>{Boolean(choice.description) && <span>{String(choice.description)}</span>}</button>)}</div> : <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy || state.block?.blockType === \"condition\" || state.block?.blockType === \"setVariable\"} onClick={() => void act(\"continue\")}>{busy ? \"Writing progress…\" : String(state.block?.configuration.buttonLabel ?? state.block?.configuration.primaryLabel ?? \"Continue\")}</button>}{error && <p className=\"runtime-error\" role=\"alert\">{error}</p>}</div></section><footer><span>Version {state.session.versionId.startsWith(\"draft:\") ? \"draft preview\" : state.session.versionId.slice(0, 8)}</span><span>Event sequence {state.session.currentSequence}</span></footer></main>;\n*** End Patch";

Object.values(blockRegistry)) {\n+      expect(definition.type).toBeTruthy();\n+      expect(definition.displayName).toBeTruthy();\n+      expect(definition.fields).toBeInstanceOf(Array);\n+ it(\"validates required player-facing fields\", () => {\n+    expect(blockRegistry.narrative.validationSchema.safeParse(blockRegistry.narrative.defaultConfiguration).success).toBe(true);\n+ \"asset-1\", altText: \"\" }).success).toBe(false);\n+  });\n+\n+  it(\"dispatches all completion modes through provider identifiers\", () => {\n+    expect(providerForBlock(\"captainApproval\", {})).toBe(\"captainManual\");\n+    expect(providerForBlock(\"textAnswer\", {})).toBe(\"textAnswer\");\n+    expect(providerForBlock(\"wait\", {})).toBe(\"timer\");\n+    expect(providerForBlock(\"arrivalCheck\", { verificationProvider: \"visionLocation\" })).toBe(\"visionLocation\");\n+    expect(providerForBlock(\"narrative\", { completionMode: \"automatic\" The validation runtime is the mirrored local copy, not the network worktree [Task 1]\n102-- `scripts/dev-common.ps1` and `Initialize-ForeverRuntime` own that mirror setup. `scripts/test-all.ps1` is the real end-to-end gate: it runs format, lint, typecheck, unit, seeded DB verification, browser acceptance, accepted DB verification, production build, and restart proofs [Task 1]\n103-- The successful Phase 3 reconciliation pattern was additive: Phase 2 had landed on `origin/main` at `ede3764`, so the feature branch needed a normal merge via `8174f28` rather than a rebase/force-push. In conflicts, let Phase 2 own player rendering/visibility and seed presets while Phase 3 owns the additive administrative layer [Task 1]\n104-- The implemented Phase 3 surface included Prisma models/migrations for `PreparedAction`, `CommandExecution`, and `PlayerPresence`, new admin endpoints for execution/preview/staging/status/presence, a routed Quartermaster UI, expanded seed data, and test coverage for command semantics and command-center workflows [Task 1]\n105:- The final user-visible publication state for the branch was `origin/feature/game-master-command-center`, with draft PR `https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT/pull/1`; pre-refresh-fix SHA `78b59c14693ec660248c5f0f298a195815e2cf95`, then refresh-fix SHA `b0b7e06` [Task 1][Task 2]\n106-- The refresh flash root cause was split state persistence: `sessionStorage` already remembered the intro \"seen\" flag, but `opened` did not survive refresh. The fix stored `forever-journal-open:<campaign>` and restored it only after hydration so the prompt/title overlay never reappeared on reload [Task 2]\n107-- The live verification that mattered was against `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`, where a Playwright check confirmed `openJournalButtons: 0`, `titleOverlays: 0`, and `workspaceHidden: \"false\"` after refresh [Task 2]\n--\n110-\n111-- Symptom: merge conflicts appear across shared docs, schema, seeds, player shell, Quartermaster UI, and styles after mainline changes land mid-rollout. Cause: Phase 2 and Phase 3 both touched common files. Fix: merge additively, keep Phase 2 player behavior/visibility intact, layer Phase 3 admin capabilities on top, and avoid wholesale replacement of shared files [Task 1]\n112:- Symptom: lint or validation fails unexpectedly in the UNC checkout. Cause: dependencies and the reliable runtime live only in the launcher mirror. Fix: run validation in `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\development` instead of the network worktree [Task 1]\n113-- Symptom: refresh still shows stale UI even after code changes. Cause: the launcher-owned shared runtime is serving older mirrored files. Fix: restart with `scripts\\stop-dev.ps1` and `scripts\\start-dev.ps1`, then verify the mirrored runtime picked up the updated branch state [Task 2]\n114-- Symptom: the first hydration fix trips `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`. Cause: state restoration happened synchronously in an effect. Fix: defer the restore with `queueMicrotask` or derive initial state from a non-React source instead of calling `setState` immediately in the effect [Task 2]\n", "type": "input_text"}]

});\r\n  });\r\n  return { asset, duplicate: false };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function assetUsages(taleId: string, assetId: string) {\r\n  const studio = await getStudioTale(taleId);\r\n  const field });\r\n  return usages;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function updateAsset(\r\n  assetId: string,\r\n  input: { displayName?: string; description?: string; tags?: string[]; roles?: string[]; PublishedBlock[];\r\n};\r\n\r\nexport type PublishedTaleSnapshot = {\r\n  schemaVersion: 1;\r\n  tale: {\r\n    id: string;\r\n    slug: string;\r\n    title: string;\r\n    subtitle: string | number;\r\n    playerCountMax: number;\r\n    estimatedDuration: number | null;\r\n    contentWarnings: string | null;\r\n  };\r\n  chapters: PublishedChapter[];\r\n  assets: Array<{\r\n    id: string;\r\n    mediaType: string;\r\n    displayName: string;\r\n    description: string | null;\r\n    mimeType: string;\r\n    width: number | null;\r\n    height: number | null;\r\n    roles: string[];\r\n    variants?: Array<{ id: string; role: string; mimeType: string; processingState: string }>;\r\n  }>;\r\n  locations: Array<Record<string, unknown>>;\r\n  artifacts: Array<Record<string, unknown>>;\r\n  publishedAt: string;\r\n};\r\n\r\nexport type VerificationProviderType =\r\n  | \"captainManual\"\r\n  | \"playerConfirmation\"\r\n  | \"textAnswer\"\r\n  | \"timer\"\r\n  | \"visionLocation\"\r\n  | \"visionObject\"\r\n  | \"externalWebhook\";\r\n\r\nexport type VerificationSubmission = {\r\n  schemaVersion: 1;\r\n  eventId: string;\r\n  idempotencyKey: string;\r\n  eventType: \"verification.observation\" | \"verification.result\";\r\n  providerType: VerificationProviderType;\r\n  providerInstanceId?: string;\r\n  sessionId: string;\r\n  publishedVersionId: string;\r\n  blockId: string;\r\n  verificationRequestId: string;\r\n  observedAt: string;\r\n  result: \"match\" | \"notMatch\" | \"uncertain\";\r\n  confidence?: number;\r\n  evidence?: JsonObject;\r\n};\r\n\r\nexport function parseJsonObject(value: string | null | undefined): JsonObject {\r\n  if (!value) return {};\r\n  try {\r\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(value);\r\n    return parsed && typeof parsed === \"object\" && !Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : {};\r\n  } catch {\r\n    return {};\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function parseJsonArray<T>(value: string | null | undefined): T[] {\r\n  if (!value) return [];\r\n  try {\r\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(value);\r\n    return Array.isArray(parsed) ? (parsed as T[]) : [];\r\n  } catch {\r\n    return [];\r\n  }\r\n}\r\nimport { createHash, randomBytes, timingSafeEqual } from \"node:crypto\";\r\nimport { cookies, headers } from \"next/headers\";\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\r\n\r\nconst PLAYER_COOKIE = \"forever_player\";\r\nconst GM_COOKIE = \"forever_gm\";\r\nconst sessionAge = 1000 * 60 * 60 * 10;\r\n\r\nexport const hashToken = (value: string) => createHash(\"sha256\").update(value).digest(\"hex\");\r\nexport const makeToken = (bytes = 32) => randomBytes(bytes).toString(\"base64url\");\r\nexport function safeEqual(a: string, b: string) {\r\n  const left = Buffer.from(a);\r\n  const right = Buffer.from(b);\r\n  return left.length === right.length && timingSafeEqual(left, right);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function setPlayerSession(playerAccessId: string) {\r\n  const jar = await cookies();\r\n  jar.set(PLAYER_COOKIE, playerAccessId, {\r\n    httpOnly: true,\r\n    sameSite: \"strict\",\r\n    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\r\n    path: \"/\",\r\n    maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30,\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function requirePlayer(campaignSlug: string) {\r\n  const jar = await cookies();\r\n  const id = jar.get(PLAYER_COOKIE)?.value;\r\n  if (!id) return null;\r\n  return db.playerAccess.findFirst({\r\n    where: { id, campaign: { slug: campaignSlug }, OR: [{ expiresAt: null }, { expiresAt: { gt: new Date() } }] },\r\n    include: { campaign: true },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function createGmSession(userId: string) {\r\n  const id = makeToken();\r\n  const csrfToken = makeToken(24);\r\n  await db.gameMasterSession.create({\r\n    data: { id: hashToken(id), userId, csrfToken, expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + sessionAge) },\r\n  });\r\n  const jar = await cookies();\r\n  jar.set(GM_COOKIE, id, {\r\n    httpOnly: true,\r\n    sameSite: \"strict\",\r\n    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\r\n    path: \"/\",\r\n    maxAge: sessionAge / 1000,\r\n  });\r\n  return csrfToken;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function requireGm() {\r\n  const raw = (await cookies()).get(GM_COOKIE)?.value;\r\n  if (!raw) return null;\r\n  return db.gameMasterSession.findFirst({\r\n    where: { id: hashToken(raw), expiresAt: { gt: new Date() } },\r\n    include: { user: true },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport type GmCapability = \"CAPTAIN\" | \"CREATE_TALES\" | \"PUBLISH_TALES\" | \"MANAGE_ASSETS\";\r\n\r\nconst roleCapabilities: Record<string, GmCapability[]> = {\r\n  CAPTAIN: [\"CAPTAIN\"],\r\n  CREATOR: [\"CREATE_TALES\", \"MANAGE_ASSETS\"],\r\n  PUBLISHER: [\"CREATE_TALES\", \"PUBLISH_TALES\", \"MANAGE_ASSETS\"],\r\n  CAPTAIN_CREATOR: [\"CAPTAIN\", \"CREATE_TALES\", \"PUBLISH_TALES\", \"MANAGE_ASSETS\"],\r\n};\r\n\r\nexport function gmCan(user: { role: string; capabilities: string }, capability: GmCapability) {\r\n  let explicit: string[] = [];\r\n  try {\r\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(user.capabilities);\r\n    if (Array.isArray(parsed)) explicit = parsed.filter((item): item is string => typeof item === \"string\");\r\n  } catch {}\r\n  return roleCapabilities[user.role]?.includes(capability) || explicit.includes(capability);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function requireGmCapability(capability: GmCapability) {\r\n  const session = await requireGm();\r\n  return session && gmCan(session.user, capability) ? session : null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function verifyCsrf(session: { csrfToken: string }) {\r\n  const provided = (await headers()).get(\"x-csrf-token\") ?? \"\";\r\n  return safeEqual(provided, session.csrfToken);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function clearGmSession() {\r\n  const jar = await cookies();\r\n  const raw = jar.get(GM_COOKIE)?.value;\r\n  if (raw) await db.gameMasterSession.deleteMany({ where: { id: hashToken(raw) } });\r\n  jar.delete(GM_COOKIE);\r\n}\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\r\nimport { requireGmCapability } from \"@/lib/security\";\r\nimport { listCaptainSessions } from \"@/tall-tale/progression\";\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\r\n\r\nexport async function GET() {\r\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"CAPTAIN\");\r\n  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Captain authentication required.\" }, { status: 401 });\r\n  const tales = await db.tallTale.findMany({\r\n    where: { archivedAt: null, latestPublishedVersionId: { not: null } },\r\n    orderBy: { title: \"asc\" },\r\n    select: { id: true, slug: true, title: true, status: true, visibility: true },\r\n  });\r\n  return NextResponse.json({ csrfToken: session.csrfToken, sessions: await listCaptainSessions(), tales });\r\n}\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\r\nimport { requireGmCapability, verifyCsrf } from \"@/lib/security\";\r\nimport { apiError } from \"@/tall-tale/api\";\r\nimport { captainSessionAction, getTaleSessionState } from \"@/tall-tale/progression\";\r\n\r\nexport async function GET(_: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ sessionId: string }> }) {\r\n  if (!(await requireGmCapability(\"CAPTAIN\")))\r\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Captain authentication required.\" }, { status: 401 });\r\n  try {\r\n    return NextResponse.json(await getTaleSessionState((await context.params).sessionId, undefined, true));\r\n  } catch (cause) {\r\n    return apiError(cause);\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function POST(request: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ sessionId: string }> }) {\r\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"CAPTAIN\");\r\n  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Captain authentication required.\" }, { status: 401 });\r\n  if (!(await verifyCsrf(session)))\r\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"The Captain session expired.\" }, { status: 403 });\r\n  try {\r\n    return NextResponse.json(\r\n      await captainSessionAction((await context.params).sessionId, session.userId, await request.json()),\r\n    );\r\n  } catch (cause) {\r\n    return apiError(cause);\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

!data) return;\r\n    const form = new FormData();\r\n    for (const file of Array.from(files)) form.append(\"files\", file);\r\n    setSaveState(`Uploading ${files.length} file${files.length === 1 ? form,\r\n    });\r\n    const body = (await response.json()) as { error?: string };\r\n    if (!response.ok) {\r\n      setError(body.error ?? \"Upload failed.\");\r\n      setSaveState(\"Upload  </LibraryPanel>\r\n      )}\r\n      {initialSection === \"artifacts\" && (\r\n        <LibraryPanel\r\n          title=\"Artifacts\"\r\n          eyebrow=\"Lore and collection rewards\"\r\n <h3>{item.name}</h3>\r\n                <p>{item.shortDescription || item.loreDescription || \"No lore has been written.\"}</p>\r\n                <button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"artifact\", \"archive\", item.id)}>Archive</button>\r\n              </article>\r\n            ))}\r\n          </div>\r\n        </LibraryPanel>\r\n      )}\r\n      {initialSection === \"versions\" && (\r\n        <LibraryPanel\r\n          title=\"Version History\"\r\n          eyebrow=\"Immutable releases\"\r\n          action={<button onClick={() => void publish()}>Publish current draft</button>}\r\n        >\r\n          <div className=\"version-list\">\r\n            {!data.versions.length && <p>No published version exists yet.</p>}\r\n            {data.versions.map((version) => (\r\n              <article key={version.id}>\r\n                <span className={version.isCurrent ? \"current\" : \"\"}>v{version.versionLabel}</span>\r\n                <div>\r\n                  <strong>{new Date(version.publishedAt).toLocaleString()}</strong>\r\n                  <p>{version.releaseNotes || \"No release notes.\"}</p>\r\n                </div>\r\n                <small>\r\n                  {version.activeSessions} pinned session{version.activeSessions === 1 ? \"\" : \"s\"}\r\n                </small>\r\n              </article>\r\n            ))}\r\n          </div>\r\n        </LibraryPanel>\r\n      )}\r\n      {(assetDrawer || initialSection === \"assets\") && (\r\n        <aside className={`asset-drawer ${assetDrawer ? \"open\" : \"page-open\"}`} aria-label=\"Asset drawer\">\r\n          <header>\r\n            <div>\r\n              <p className=\"eyebrow\">Media hold</p>\r\n              <h2>Upload and reuse assets</h2>\r\n            </div>\r\n            <button onClick={() => setAssetDrawer(false)} aria-label=\"Close asset drawer\">\r\n              Ã—\r\n            </button>\r\n          </header>\r\n          <label\r\n            className=\"upload-drop\"\r\n            onDragOver={(event) => event.preventDefault()}\r\n            onDrop={(event) => {\r\n              event.preventDefault();\r\n              void upload(event.dataTransfer.files);\r\n            }}\r\n          >\r\n            <input\r\n              type=\"file\"\r\n              multiple\r\n              accept=\"image/png,image/jpeg,image/webp,image/avif,video/mp4,video/webm,audio/mpeg,audio/ogg,audio/wav,application/pdf\"\r\n              onChange={(event) => void upload(event.target.files)}\r\n            />\r\n            <strong>Drop files or choose from this device</strong>\r\n            <span>Originals are preserved; image thumbnails and player variants are generated.</span>\r\n          </label>\r\n          <div className=\"drawer-assets\">\r\n            {data.assets.slice(0, 12).map((asset) => (\r\n              <button\r\n                key={asset.id}\r\n                onClick={() => {\r\n                  if (selected)\r\n                    updateSelected((block) => {\r\n                      const firstAssetField = selectedDefinition?.fields.find(\r\n                        (field) =>\r\n                          field.kind === \"asset\" &&\r\n                          (!field.mediaTypes?.length || field.mediaTypes.includes(asset.mediaType)),\r\n                      );\r\n                      if (firstAssetField) block.configuration[firstAssetField.key] = asset.id;\r\n                    });\r\n                }}\r\n              >\r\n                <span>\r\n                  {asset.mediaType === \"IMAGE\" ? (\r\n                    <img src={`/api/media/${asset.id}?variant=THUMBNAIL`} alt=\"\" />\r\n                  ) : (\r\n                    asset.mediaType\r\n                  )}\r\n                </span>\r\n                <strong>{asset.displayName}</strong>\r\n              </button>\r\n            ))}\r\n          </div>\r\n        </aside>\r\n      )}\r\n    </main>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction DropZone({ onDrop }: { onDrop: (event: React.DragEvent) => void }) {\r\n  return (\r\n    <div\r\n      className=\"timeline-drop\"\r\n      onDragOver={(event) => {\r\n        event.preventDefault();\r\n        event.dataTransfer.dropEffect = event.dataTransfer.effectAllowed === \"copy\" ? \"copy\" : \"move\";\r\n      }}\r\n      onDrop={onDrop}\r\n    >\r\n      <span>Drop here</span>\r\n    </div>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Field({\r\n  field,\r\n  value,\r\n  assets,\r\n  locations,\r\n  artifacts,\r\n  onChange,\r\n}: {\r\n  field: InspectorField;\r\n  value: unknown;\r\n  assets: Asset[];\r\n  locations: LibraryRecord[];\r\n  artifacts: LibraryRecord[];\r\n  onChange: (value: unknown) => void;\r\n}) {\r\n  const label = (\r\n    <span>\r\n      {field.label}\r\n      {field.required && <b aria-label=\"required\"> *</b>}\r\n    </span>\r\n  );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"textarea\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <textarea rows={5} value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value)} />\r\n        {field.help && <small>{field.help}</small>}\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"boolean\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label className=\"check-field\">\r\n        <input type=\"checkbox\" checked={Boolean(value)} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.checked)} />\r\n        {label}\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"number\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <input\r\n          type=\"number\"\r\n          value={typeof value === \"number\" ? value : \"\"}\r\n          onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value === \"\" ? null : Number(event.target.value))}\r\n        />\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"select\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <select value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value)}>\r\n          {field.options?.map((option) => (\r\n            <option key={option.value} value={option.value}>\r\n              {option.label}\r\n            </option>\r\n          ))}\r\n        </select>\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"asset\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <select value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value || null)}>\r\n          <option value=\"\">No asset selected</option>\r\n          {assets\r\n            .filter((asset) => !field.mediaTypes?.length || field.mediaTypes.includes(asset.mediaType))\r\n            .map((asset) => (\r\n              <option key={asset.id} value={asset.id}>\r\n                {asset.displayName} Â· {asset.mediaType.toLocaleLowerCase()}\r\n              </option>\r\n            ))}\r\n        </select>\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"location\" || field.kind === \"artifact\") {\r\n    const records = field.kind === \"location\" ? locations : artifacts;\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <select value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value || null)}>\r\n          <option value=\"\">Choose {field.kind}</option>\r\n          {records.map((item) => (\r\n            <option key={item.id} value={item.id}>\r\n              {item.name}\r\n            </option>\r\n          ))}\r\n        </select>\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  }\r\n  if (field.kind === \"json\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <textarea\r\n          rows={5}\r\n          defaultValue={JSON.stringify(value ?? (field.key === \"acceptedAnswers\" ? [] : {}), null, 2)}\r\n          onBlur={(event) => {\r\n            try {\r\n              onChange(JSON.parse(event.target.value));\r\n              event.target.setCustomValidity(\"\");\r\n            } catch {\r\n              event.target.setCustomValidity(\"Enter valid JSON.\");\r\n              event.target.reportValidity();\r\n            }\r\n          }}\r\n        />\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  return (\r\n    <label>\r\n      {label}\r\n      <input value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value)} />\r\n      {field.help && <small>{field.help}</small>}\r\n    </label>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction AlignmentEditor({\r\n  value,\r\n  assets,\r\n  beforeId,\r\n  afterId,\r\n  onChange,\r\n}: {\r\n  value: unknown;\r\n  assets: Asset[];\r\n  beforeId: string;\r\n  afterId: string;\r\n  onChange: (value: JsonObject) => void;\r\n}) {\r\n  const alignment =\r\n    value && typeof value === \"object\" && !Array.isArray(value)\r\n      ? (value as JsonObject)\r\n      : { x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1, rotation: 0, opacity: 50, focalX: 50, focalY: 50 };\r\n  const before = assets.find((asset) => asset.id === beforeId);\r\n  const after = assets.find((asset) => asset.id === afterId);\r\n  const set = (key: string, next: number) => onChange({ ...alignment, [key]: next });\r\n  return (\r\n    <fieldset className=\"alignment-editor\">\r\n      <legend>Before / after alignment</legend>\r\n      <div className=\"alignment-stage\">\r\n        {before && <img src={`/api/media/${before.id}?variant=PREVIEW`} alt=\"Before alignment reference\" />}\r\n        {after && (\r\n          <img\r\n            src={`/api/media/${after.id}?variant=PREVIEW`}\r\n            alt=\"After alignment reference\"\r\n            style={{\r\n              opacity: Number(alignment.opacity ?? 50) / 100,\r\n              transform: `translate(${Number(alignment.x ?? 0)}px, ${Number(alignment.y ?? 0)}px) scale(${Number(alignment.scale ?? 1)}) rotate(${Number(alignment.rotation ?? 0)}deg)`,\r\n            }}\r\n          />\r\n        )}\r\n      </div>\r\n      {(\r\n        [\r\n          [\"opacity\", 0, 100, 1],\r\n          [\"x\", -200, 200, 1],\r\n          [\"y\", -200, 200, 1],\r\n          [\"scale\", 0.5, 2, 0.01],\r\n          [\"rotation\", -20, 20, 0.5],\r\n        ] as const\r\n      ).map(([key, min, max, step]) => (\r\n        <label key={key}>\r\n          <span>\r\n            {key} <b>{String(alignment[key] ?? (key === \"scale\" ? 1 : 0))}</b>\r\n          </span>\r\n          <input\r\n            type=\"range\"\r\n            min={min}\r\n            max={max}\r\n            step={step}\r\n            value={Number(alignment[key] ?? (key === \"scale\" ? 1 : 0))}\r\n            onChange={(event) => set(key, Number(event.target.value))}\r\n          />\r\n        </label>\r\n      ))}\r\n      <button\r\n        type=\"button\"\r\n        onClick={() => onChange({ x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1, rotation: 0, opacity: 50, focalX: 50, focalY: 50 })}\r\n      >\r\n        Reset alignment\r\n      </button>\r\n    </fieldset>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction LibraryPanel({\r\n  title,\r\n  eyebrow,\r\n  action,\r\n  children,\r\n}: {\r\n  title: string;\r\n  eyebrow: string;\r\n  action: React.ReactNode;\r\n  children: React.ReactNode;\r\n}) {\r\n  return (\r\n    <section className=\"editor-single-panel library-panel\">\r\n      <header>\r\n        <div>\r\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">{eyebrow}</p>\r\n          <h2>{title}</h2>\r\n        </div>\r\n        {action}\r\n      </header>\r\n      {children}\r\n    </section>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

\"\").trim() || null,\r\n            collectionGroup: String(input.data.collectionGroup ?? \"\").trim() || null,\r\n          },\r\n        }),\r\n      );\r\n    if (!input.id) throw new Error(\"Artifact ID required.\");\r\n    if (input.action === \"archive\")\r\n      return NextResponse.json(\r\n        await db.taleArtifact.update({ where: { id: input.id }, data: { archivedAt: new Date() } }),\r\n      );\r\n    return NextResponse.json(\r\n      await db.taleArtifact.update({\r\n        where: { id: input.id },\r\n        data: {\r\n          name: input.data.name ? ## Tool Result: call_ryhcHa5MslcTYVGUBC9uwsVn
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-17T21:40:11.411Z

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.4 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 1.3 seconds\nOutput:\n# The Forever Treasure Companion\r\n\r\nProduction-oriented foundation and fully automated local demo for a private nautical-fantasy date-night Tall Tale. All committed story material is generic development seed content; no final surprise content belongs in this public repository.\r\n\r\n## Current phase\r\n\r\nPhase three rebuilds that companion as a cinematic, event-synchronized experience. A shared animation director coordinates the harbor arrival, physical invitation and authentication, page-flip journal, chapter ceremonies, chart routes, artifacts, side quests, log, sealed finale, and Quartermaster instruments without allowing presentation timing to outrun server truth. All committed content remains fictional development seed material.\r\n\r\n## Stack\r\n\r\n- Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, strict TypeScript\r\n- Prisma 6.19 with SQLite for local/test and an equivalent MySQL production schema\r\n- GSAP 3.15 for orchestrated scenes and SVG/text work; Motion 12 for React interaction and presence\r\n- StPageFlip for the journal; local Rive and Lottie runtimes with explicit static fallbacks\r\n- bcrypt password/access-code hashing, server-side database sessions, CSRF tokens, and rate limiting\r\n- Vitest and Playwright\r\n\r\n## Reproducible setup\r\n\r\nRequirements: Windows PowerShell 5.1+, Node.js 24, npm 11, and Git. MySQL 8 is only required for production parity; the supported local/demo/test strategy is SQLite.\r\n\r\n### Fastest Windows demo\r\n\r\nAfter cloning, double-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd`, or run:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\nnpm run dev:full\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe launcher creates an ignored `.env` when absent, installs the exact lockfile, generates Prisma, applies migrations, seeds generic disposable data, starts the app, checks health, and prints the URLs and credentials. Network/UNC workspaces are mirrored to an ignored local runtime under `%LOCALAPPDATA%` because Node and SQLite are unreliable on network shares. Stop it with `npm run dev:stop`.\r\n\r\n- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\r\n- Player phrase: `development-moonwake`\r\n- GM: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\r\n- GM development[REDACTED: LOGIN CREDENTIAL]\r\n\r\nThese values are disposable local defaults, never production credentials. To opt into LAN access, run `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan`; Windows Firewall and network policy still determine whether another device can connect.\r\n\r\n### Portable manual setup\r\n\r\n```bash\r\ngit clone https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\r\ncd treasurehuntSoT\r\nnpm ci\r\ncp .env.example .env\r\nnpm run db:generate\r\nnpm run db:migrate\r\nnpm run db:seed\r\nnpm run dev\r\n```\r\n\r\nSet unique local values in `.env`; the seedâ€™s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\r\n\r\n## Commands\r\n\r\n| Command                           | Purpose                                                                |\r\n| --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |\r\n| `npm run dev:full`                | Idempotent setup, migration, seed, health check, and background server |\r\n| `npm run dev:stop`                | Safely stop only the recorded development process                      |\r\n| `npm run validate`                | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof          |\r\n| `npm run dev`                     | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                        |\r\n| `npm run build`                   | Production build                                                       |\r\n| `npm start`                       | Start production build                                                 |\r\n| `npm test`                        | Deterministic domain, director, ownership, asset, and page tests       |\r\n| `npm run assets:validate`         | Validate local Lottie/Rive contracts and fallback paths                |\r\n| `npm run test:e2e`                | Five Chromium/WebKit checks plus one Chromium mutation workflow        |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate`              | Apply local SQLite migrations                                          |\r\n| `npm run db:seed`                 | Load replaceable development content                                   |\r\n| `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage` | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset                |\r\n| `npm run db:generate:mysql`       | Generate production MySQL client                                       |\r\n\r\nFull setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Animation ownership, scene contracts, assets, performance, and the development lab are indexed from [animation architecture](docs/animation/architecture.md).\r\n\r\n## Repository workflow\r\n\r\n`main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\r\n\r\n## Important limitations\r\n\r\nThe full story, final Rive artwork/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural. The sole third-party visual sample is MIT-licensed, local, documented, and development-only; production uses original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\r\n\r\n## Next steps\r\n\r\nAdd CI for the complete validation gate, exercise the parallel MySQL schema in an integration environment, and replace process-local SSE fan-out before multi-instance deployment. Real surprise content remains blocked until repository visibility is verified private.\r\n# Architecture\r\n\r\n## Phase 3 cinematic companion projection\r\n\r\nThe player reads one server-filtered `PublicSnapshot` containing chapters, released hints/annotations, visible map locations/routes, safe artifact states, visible optional mysteries, event-derived log entries, generic finale state, and per-section unseen counts. One SSE connection transports ordered sanitized events; section navigation is client-side and starts no independent polling.\r\n\r\nNew normalized records are `MapRoute` and `ViewedContent`. Existing content models gained release, relationship, placement, and safe-label fields rather than duplicating derived display tables. `AudioPreference` now carries the cross-device preference projection; immediate UI choices may also be cached locally for offline startup.\r\n\r\n```mermaid\r\nflowchart LR\r\n  P[Player journal + animation director] -->|snapshot + access cookie| N[Next.js server]\r\n  G[Quartermaster] -->|session + CSRF| N\r\n  N -->|Prisma transaction| D[(SQLite dev / MySQL prod)]\r\n  N -->|ordered SSE + replay| P\r\n  N --> A[Audit and save-state log]\r\n```\r\n\r\nServer Components enforce initial access. Route handlers own authentication, validation, snapshots, and SSE. `src/server/progression.ts` is the transaction boundary; `src/domain/story.ts` owns state rules. The UI receives only a public projection, never database rows. In-memory publish accelerates same-process delivery while database replay by sequence remains authoritative after reconnect/restart.\r\n\r\nThe client adds one animation director between ordered domain events and presentation. It starts server work immediately, allows only non-authoritative opening/idle motion while pending, and selects a success or failure timeline from the actual response. One queue owns SSE ceremonies, and the public snapshot remains authoritative after skip, cancellation, reconnect, or visual-runtime failure.\r\n\r\nMajor dependencies remain deliberately bounded: Prisma for persistence and transactions, bcryptjs for portable hashes, Zod for payload validation, GSAP for cinematic orchestration, Motion for React interaction/presence, StPageFlip for the journal surface, Rive/Lottie for isolated vector assets, Pino for structured redacted logs, and Vitest/Playwright for validation. See `docs/animation/architecture.md` and `docs/animation/library-ownership.md`.\r\n# Local development\r\n\r\n## One-command Windows startup\r\n\r\nDouble-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd` or run `npm run dev:full`. The idempotent launcher:\r\n\r\n1. Finds Node/npm and explains what is missing when neither a normal installation nor the bundled Codex runtime is available.\r\n2. Creates `.env` only when absent; existing local configuration is preserved and validated.\r\n3. Mirrors network-share checkouts to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\development` while keeping dependencies, databases, logs, and generated artifacts out of Git.\r\n4. Runs `npm ci` only when `package-lock.json` changed or dependencies are missing.\r\n5. Generates Prisma, applies the versioned SQLite migration, and runs the idempotent development seed.\r\n6. Refuses to take an occupied port, starts the server as a hidden recorded process, and waits for an HTTP health response.\r\n7. Prints the player and GM URLs, disposable credentials, and stop command.\r\n\r\nRun `npm run dev:stop` before switching branches or replacing the runtime. Shutdown verifies the recorded PID is the expected Next.js development process before stopping it.\r\n\r\n## URLs and credentials\r\n\r\n- Player URL: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\r\n- Invitation phrase: `development-moonwake`\r\n- GM URL: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\r\n- GM username/password: `kato` / `development-captain-only`\r\n- Animation showcase: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/dev/animations` (development only)\r\n\r\nThe launcher-generated `.env` is ignored. Change these values for any shared environment. Never reuse them for deployment.\r\n\r\n## LAN opt-in\r\n\r\n`powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan` binds Next.js to `0.0.0.0` and prints the best detected IPv4 URLs. LAN mode is intentionally not the one-click default because it exposes a development server and disposable credentials to the local network. If another device cannot connect, allow the selected TCP port through Windows Firewall and confirm that corporate Wi-Fi/client-isolation policy permits peer traffic.\r\n\r\n## Portable fallback\r\n\r\nOn macOS/Linux or when automation is unavailable:\r\n\r\n```bash\r\nnpm ci\r\ncp .env.example .env\r\nnpm run db:generate\r\nnpm run db:migrate\r\nnpm run db:seed\r\nnpm run dev\r\n```\r\n\r\nUse Node 24 and npm 11.9.0; `.nvmrc`, `.node-version`, `packageManager`, and `package-lock.json` define the reproducible toolchain.\r\n\r\n## Animation development\r\n\r\nOpen `/dev/animations` or use the development-only `TEST ANIMATIONS` button. The lab contains all registered scenes, a no-API trailer, transport/scrub/speed/mode controls, StPageFlip book, live Rive input proof, Lottie controls, fallback states, and local FPS/long-task/runtime metrics. The route intentionally returns 404 from a production server.\r\n\r\nUse `npm run assets:validate` after any asset-contract change. Keep all runtime files and fallbacks under `public`, add provenance to `docs/asset-licenses.md`, and never use a remote CDN placeholder. See `docs/animation/showcase.md` for the manual pass and `docs/animation/` for scene/asset extension contracts.\r\n\r\n## Clean-clone continuation\r\n\r\nAn authorized session on another computer needs only Git, Node/npm, and this repository. Clone `main`, copy `.env.example` or use the Windows launcher, then run `npm run validate`. No database, generated Prisma client, Playwright browser state, artifact, or machine path is required from the originating computer.\r\n\r\n## Troubleshooting\r\n\r\n- **Port 3000 is occupied:** stop that process or run `scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Port 3001`. The launcher never kills an unknown listener.\r\n- **Existing `.env` is incomplete:** the launcher preserves it and identifies each missing key. Add the key; it will not overwrite local secrets.\r\n- **Startup fails:** inspect `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\development\\.forever\\logs`.\r\n- **Database is disposable:** stop the app, remove the ignored local `prisma/dev.db`, and rerun startup. For validation, `npm run validate` always rebuilds `validation.db` from migrations.\r\n- **Browser binaries are missing:** `npm run validate` installs pinned Chromium and WebKit builds automatically.\r\n- **UNC/network checkout is slow:** the first mirror/install is expected to take longer; subsequent runs retain the dependency cache and only synchronize project files.\r\n# Testing\r\n\r\nRun the complete release gate with:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\nnpm run validate\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe command mirrors network-share checkouts into a clean local runtime, rebuilds disposable `validation.db`, and runs:\r\n\r\n1. Exact lockfile install, Prisma generation, migration deployment, seed, and baseline database invariants.\r\n2. Prettier, ESLint, strict TypeScript, Vitest component/unit tests, and local animation-asset validation.\r\n3. Seed verification, pinned Chromium/WebKit installation, and Playwright acceptance/accessibility workflows.\r\n4. Post-acceptance event/snapshot/audit and undo-state verification.\r\n5. Optimized Next.js production build.\r\n6. Two consecutive production start/health/stop cycles, including proof that `/dev/animations` returns 404.\r\n\r\nAnimation coverage includes director queue/synchronization/skip/failure/cancellation/cleanup, every scene builder in all modes, semantic labels, ownership, visibility observers, asset contracts, local Lottie lifecycle/controls/failure, local Rive inputs/lifecycle/WebGL fallback, StPageFlip initialization/update/destroy/keyboard/orientation/reduced behavior, journal secret filtering/stable pages, Motion variants/navigation/objective controls, modal focus restoration, first-arrival replay/skip, and the guarded showcase/trailer.\r\n\r\nThe browser workflow also covers access/GM failure and success, rate limiting, cookie isolation, live SSE chapter release, named scene checkpoints, replay without mutation, sound/motion preferences, refresh persistence, artifact/map progression, solve/undo, offline pause/resume reconciliation, heartbeat, deep links, responsive viewports, and axe serious/critical violations.\r\n\r\nRequired viewports are 2560 Ã— 1440, 1920 Ã— 1080, 1440 Ã— 900, 430 Ã— 932, 390 Ã— 844, and 844 Ã— 390. Stable screenshots are captured at semantic checkpoints rather than arbitrary delays. Reports, traces, screenshots, and production logs are ignored under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\validation\\artifacts\\validation` for network-share checkouts.\r\n\r\nFocused commands: `npm test`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm run assets:validate`, `npm run test:e2e`, and `npm run build`. Detailed animation test and manual leak/profile instructions are in `docs/animation/testing.md` and `docs/animation/performance.md`.\r\n# Known limitations\r\n\r\n- The repository is public. Final story, riddles, personal material, surprise content, production credentials, and private media remain intentionally blocked.\r\n- Invitation seal, journal clasp, voyage compass, and finale mechanism have typed Rive contracts but no final designer-authored `.riv` binaries. Production renders honest original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The sole working `.riv` file is an MIT-licensed development-only runtime sample.\r\n- Current Lottie effects are original shape-layer ambience/punctuation, not final designer-exported Bodymovin artwork.\r\n- Finale unlock/assembly remains structural and cannot enter unsupported `READY` or later states through current controls.\r\n- SSE fan-out is process-local. Database replay is durable, but multi-instance production needs Redis or equivalent pub/sub.\r\n- Initial Ship's Log projection is capped at 250 entries; cursor pagination is future scale work.\r\n- The parallel MySQL migration still needs exercise against a real MySQL 8 integration environment.\r\n- Development FPS/long-task metrics are observational and local, not production telemetry. Final Rive binaries require another mobile GPU/memory profile.\r\n- The complete mutation workflow runs once in Chromium to avoid shared-database contention; privacy, access gates, landing behavior, accessibility, responsive layouts, and mobile behavior also run in WebKit.\r\n- Windows LAN access is opt-in and can still be blocked by firewall or corporate network policy.\r\n- Final licensed audio, production monitoring, CI, deployment, and private content authoring remain later work.\r\n# Deployment\r\n\r\nTarget: Debian, Node 24, MySQL 8, NGINX, HTTPS, and systemd. Do not deploy from development seed credentials.\r\n\r\n1. Create MySQL database/user and set `DATABASE_URL=mysql://...`.\r\n2. For a new database run `npm ci`, `npm run db:generate:mysql`, `npm run db:migrate:mysql:init`, then `npm run build`. Record future MySQL migrations under `prisma/mysql-migrations` before production use.\r\n3. Install `deploy/forever-treasure.service`, place secrets in `/etc/forever-treasure.env`, and proxy with `deploy/nginx.conf`.\r\n4. Send application logs to journald; retain NGINX access/error logs under `/var/log/nginx`.\r\n\r\nBack up with daily encrypted `mysqldump --single-transaction`, retain seven daily/four weekly copies off-host, and test quarterly restore into a separate database. Restore by stopping the service, importing a verified dump, applying pending migrations, and starting/reconciling. Generate secrets with `openssl rand -base64 48`.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Tale Complete\n+\n+Each entry owns a stable type ID, category, defaults, inspector metadata, Zod configuration schema, asset-field declarations, and verification-provider dispatch. Unknown types render a safe diagnostic card and do not execute arbitrary behavior.\n+\n+The editor sends the complete normalized draft with its `autosaveVersion`. The server claims that exact token before replacing chapter and block rows. A stale browser receives `DRAFT_CONFLICT`; its local state is not discarded. Choice and condition targets become explicit `BlockConnection` records. Duplication assigns new chapter/block IDs and remaps branch targets.\n+\n+## Publishing and sessions\n+\n+Publishing runs server-side graph, configuration, asset, and reachability validation. Blocking errors prevent a release; warnings remain visible. A successful publish serializes tale metadata, chapters, blocks, connections, locations, artifacts, and exact asset variants into a checksummed immutable snapshot. The transaction advances `latestPublishedVersionId` and marks the previous release non-current.\n+\n+The catalog reads only current, public or unlisted releases. Starting a game records the exact `publishedVersionId`; later releases cannot alter that session. Preview sessions instead carry a draft snapshot and are visibly labeled. Every session event has a monotonic sequence and globally unique idempotency key.\n+\n+The progression engine advances automatic blocks, evaluates variables/conditions, grants artifacts once, and opens standardized verification requests. Text answers, player confirmations, timers, Captain decisions, helper submissions, and the simulator converge on that engine. It rejects duplicate, stale, wrong-session, wrong-version, wrong-block, and wrong-request submissions.\n+\n+## Assets and reusable libraries\n+\n+Uploads validate MIME allowlists, size, and magic bytes. Originals are retained; images receive WebP thumbnail, preview, optimized, and mobile derivatives with checksums. Duplicate content returns the existing logical asset. The seed is idempotent and additive: it preserves legacy Campaign rows and rebuilds only the known development Studio tale.\n+\n+For local SQLite:\n+\n+```powershell\n+npm run db:generate\n+npm run db:migrate\n+npm run db:seed\n+```\n+\n+For a fresh MySQL database, apply the three numbered scripts in order as documented in `docs/deployment.md`.\n+\n+## Operational checklist\n+\n+Before release, run `npm run validate`. It prepares a narrow, auditable provider seam so a later phone/helper implementation cannot become a second progression engine.\n+\n+## Reference preparation\n+\n+Creators can organize assets into location or artifact collections, including positive and negative reference sets, and assign a collection to a location. Locations retain a JSON verification profile for future provider configuration. Published snapshots freeze those records and referenced asset variants with the rest of a release.\n+\n+## Pairing\n+\n+An authenticated Captain creates a short-lived pairing through `POST /api/helper/pair` with `sessionId` and a stable local `deviceId`. The server permits only active, published, non-preview sessions, stores a hash of the token, and returns the raw bearer token once. Tokens expire after 15 minutes and can be revoked in the data model.\n+\n+Phase 1 intentionally provides the API boundary rather than a camera UI. A future QR or numeric-code ceremony should transmit the returned token over an authenticated local channel, avoid query-string logging, and rotate/revoke on re-pair.\n+\n+## Verification envelope\n+\n+The helper submits `POST /api/helper/verification` with `Authorization: Bearer <pairing token>` and a versioned body:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"schemaVersion\": 1,\n+  \"eventId\": \"provider-unique-event\",\n+  \"idempotencyKey\": \"provider-stable-retry-key\",\n+  \"eventType\": \"verification.observation\",\n+  \"providerType\": \"visionLocation\",\n+  \"providerInstanceId\": \"helper-device-id\",\n+  \"sessionId\": \"session-id\",\n+  \"publishedVersionId\": \"published-version-id\",\n+  \"blockId\": \"current-block-id\",\n+  \"verificationRequestId\": \"pending-request-id\",\n+  \"observedAt\": \"2026-07-17T20:00:00.000Z\",\n+  \"result\": \"match\",\n+  \"confidence\": 0.92,\n+  \"evidence\": { \"referenceCollectionId\": \"collection-id\" }\n+}\n+```\n+\n+The API validates shape, bounds confidence to 0..1, updates pairing presence, and calls the same `submitVerification` function used by other providers. The engine binds the event to the pending request and checks session, published version, current block, provider type, and request status before advancing. Duplicate retries return the prior accepted result; contradictory or stale submissions are rejected and retained as verification evidence where appropriate.\n+\n+## Simulator\n+\n+`POST /api/captain/simulate` is development-only and requires a Captain session plus CSRF. It creates the same envelope and enters the same progression function with `sourceType=simulator`; it is not a shortcut or a production provider. The Captain session screen exposes match, no-match, and uncertain controls when a compatible request is pending.\n+\n+## Later implementation rules\n+\n+- Keep frame capture and model inference outside the authoritative web process.\n+- Upload derived evidence by default, not raw frames; define retention and consent before storing imagery.\n+- Sign provider releases and identify the provider instance/model in evidence.\n+- Calibrate confidence per verification profile; never interpret confidence alone as permission to progress.\n+- Preserve human Captain override and complete audit events.\n+- Add rate limits, token revocation UI, device heartbeat, and encrypted transport before real-device rollout.\n+- Test false positive, false negative, duplicate, delayed, reordered, wrong-version, and offline/reconnect cases against the current contract suite.\n*** End Patch";
