field: \"alignment\",\r\n          });\r\n      }\r\n      const targets = block.connections.map((connection) => connection.targetBlockId);\r\n      outgoing.set(block.id, targets);\r\n      if (block.blockType === \"choice\") {\r\n        const choices = block.configuration.choices;\r\n        if (!Array.isArray(choices) || choices.length < 2)\r\n          error({\r\n            code: \"CHOICE_COUNT\",\r\n            message: `${block.title} needs at least two choices.`,\r\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\r\n            blockId: block.id,\r\n            field: \"choices\",\r\n          });\r\n        for (const choice of Array.isArray(choices) ? choices : []) {\r\n          const target =\r\n            choice && typeof choice === \"object\" ? String((choice as Record<string, unknown>).targetBlockId ?? \"\") : \"\";\r\n          if (!blockIds.has(target))\r\n            error({\r\n              code: \"CHOICE_TARGET\",\r\n              message: `${block.title} has a missing choice target.`,\r\n              chapterId: chapter.id,\r\n              blockId: block.id,\r\n              field: \"choices\",\r\n            });\r\n        }\r\n      }\r\n      if (block.blockType === \"condition\") {\r\n        for (const field of [\"successTargetBlockId\", \"failureTargetBlockId\"] as const)\r\n          if (!blockIds.has(String(block.configuration[field] ?? \"\")))\r\n            error({\r\n              code: \"CONDITION_TARGET\",\r\n              message: `${block.title} has a missing ${field === \"successTargetBlockId\" ? \"success\" : \"failure\"} target.`,\r\n              chapterId: chapter.id,\r\n              blockId: block.id,\r\n              field,\r\n            });\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  if (!taleCompleteCount)\r\n    error({ code: \"NO_TALE_COMPLETE\", message: \"Add a Tale Complete block so the runtime has a safe endpoint.\" });\r\n  if (taleCompleteCount > 1)\r\n    warn({\r\n      code: \"MULTIPLE_TALE_COMPLETE\",\r\n      message: \"The tale has multiple completion endpoints. Confirm each branch is intentional.\",\r\n    });\r\n\r\n  const entry = allBlocks[0]?.id;\r\n  if (entry) {\r\n    const visited = new Set<string>();\r\n    const queue = [entry];\r\n    while (queue.length) {\r\n      const current = queue.shift()!;\r\n      if (visited.has(current)) continue;\r\n      visited.add(current);\r\n      for (const target of outgoing.get(current) ?? []) if (!visited.has(target)) queue.push(target);\r\n    }\r\n    for (const block of allBlocks)\r\n      if (block.isEnabled && !visited.has(block.id))\r\n        warn({\r\n          code: \"UNREACHABLE_BLOCK\",\r\n          message: `${block.title} cannot be reached through the saved story graph.`,\r\n          chapterId: block.chapterId,\r\n          blockId: block.id,\r\n        });\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const checkedAt = new Date();\r\n  const result: DraftValidationResult = {\r\n    valid: errors.length === 0,\r\n    errors,\r\n    warnings,\r\n    checkedAt: checkedAt.toISOString(),\r\n  };\r\n  await db.taleDraft.update({\r\n    where: { id: studio.draft.id },\r\n    data: {\r\n      validationState: result.valid ? \"VALID\" : \"INVALID\",\r\n      validationSummary: JSON.stringify(result),\r\n      lastValidatedAt: checkedAt,\r\n    },\r\n  });\r\n  return { ...result, autosaveVersion: studio.draft.autosaveVersion };\r\n}\r\nimport { createHash } from \"node:crypto\";\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\r\nimport { eventBus } from \"@/lib/events\";\r\nimport { getStudioTale } from \"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\r\nimport type { PublishedTaleSnapshot } from \"@/tall-tale/types\";\r\nimport { validateTaleDraft } from \"@/tall-tale/validation\";\r\n\r\nexport class PublishValidationError extends Error {\r\n  constructor(public readonly validation: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof validateTaleDraft>>) {\r\n    super(\"Draft validation failed. Resolve the blocking issues before publishing.\");\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function snapshotFromStudio(studio: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getStudioTale>>): PublishedTaleSnapshot {\r\n  return {\r\n    schemaVersion: 1,\r\n    tale: {\r\n      id: studio.tale.id,\r\n      slug: studio.tale.slug,\r\n      title: studio.tale.title,\r\n      subtitle: studio.tale.subtitle,\r\n      shortDescription: studio.tale.shortDescription,\r\n      longDescription: studio.tale.longDescription,\r\n      coverAssetId: studio.tale.coverAssetId,\r\n      theme: studio.tale.theme,\r\n      visibility: studio.tale.visibility,\r\n      playerCountMin: studio.tale.playerCountMin,\r\n      playerCountMax: studio.tale.playerCountMax,\r\n      estimatedDuration: studio.tale.estimatedDuration,\r\n      contentWarnings: studio.tale.contentWarnings,\r\n    },\r\n    chapters: studio.draft.chapters.map((chapter, chapterIndex) => ({\r\n      id: chapter.id,\r\n      title: chapter.title,\r\n      subtitle: chapter.subtitle,\r\n      description: chapter.description,\r\n      coverAssetId: chapter.coverAssetId,\r\n      estimatedDuration: chapter.estimatedDuration,\r\n      isOptional: chapter.isOptional,\r\n      metadata: chapter.metadata,\r\n      orderIndex: chapterIndex,\r\n      entryBlockId: chapter.blocks[0]?.id ?? null,\r\n      completionBlockId:\r\n        [...chapter.blocks]\r\n          .reverse()\r\n          .find((block) => block.blockType === \"chapterComplete\" || block.blockType === \"taleComplete\")?.id ??\r\n        chapter.blocks.at(-1)?.id ??\r\n        null,\r\n      blocks: chapter.blocks.map((block, blockIndex) => ({\r\n        id: block.id,\r\n        chapterId: chapter.id,\r\n        blockType: block.blockType,\r\n        title: block.title,\r\n        internalLabel: block.internalLabel,\r\n        configuration: block.configuration,\r\n        presentation: block.presentation,\r\n        completion: block.completion,\r\n        creatorNotes: null,\r\n        isEnabled: block.isEnabled,\r\n        schemaVersion: block.schemaVersion,\r\n        orderIndex: blockIndex,\r\n        nextBlockId: block.connections[0]?.targetBlockId ?? null,\r\n        connections: block.connections.map((connection, connectionIndex) => ({\r\n          ...connection,\r\n          orderIndex: connectionIndex,\r\n        })),\r\n      })),\r\n    })),\r\n    assets: studio.assets.map((asset) => ({\r\n      id: asset.id,\r\n      mediaType: asset.mediaType,\r\n      displayName: asset.displayName,\r\n      description: asset.description,\r\n      mimeType: asset.mimeType,\r\n      width: asset.width,\r\n      height: asset.height,\r\n      roles: asset.roles,\r\n      variants: asset.variants.map((variant) => ({\r\n        id: variant.id,\r\n        role: variant.role,\r\n        mimeType: variant.mimeType,\r\n        processingState: variant.processingState,\r\n      })),\r\n    })) as PublishedTaleSnapshot[\"assets\"],\r\n    locations: studio.locations.map((location) => ({ ...location, captainNotes: undefined })),\r\n    artifacts: studio.artifacts,\r\n    publishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function publishTale(\r\n  taleId: string,\r\n  publisherId: string,\r\n  releaseNotes: string,\r\n  expectedAutosaveVersion?: number,\r\n) {\r\n  const validation = await validateTaleDraft(taleId);\r\n  if (!validation.valid) throw new PublishValidationError(validation);\r\n  if (expectedAutosaveVersion !== undefined && validation.autosaveVersion !== expectedAutosaveVersion)\r\n    throw new Error(\"The draft changed before publishing. Review the latest saved version and try again.\");\r\n  const studio = await getStudioTale(taleId);\r\n  if (studio.draft.autosaveVersion !== validation.autosaveVersion)\r\n    throw new Error(\"The draft changed during validation. Publish again after reviewing the current draft.\");\r\n  const snapshot = snapshotFromStudio(studio);\r\n  const contentSnapshot = JSON.stringify(snapshot);\r\n  const checksum = createHash(\"sha256\").update(contentSnapshot).digest(\"hex\");\r\n  const version = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const latest = await tx.publishedTaleVersion.findFirst({ where: { taleId }, orderBy: { versionNumber: \"desc\" } });\r\n    const versionNumber = (latest?.versionNumber ?? 0) + 1;\r\n    await tx.publishedTaleVersion.updateMany({ where: { taleId, isCurrent: true }, data: { isCurrent: false } });\r\n    const created = await tx.publishedTaleVersion.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        taleId,\r\n        versionNumber,\r\n        versionLabel: versionNumber === 1 ? \"1.0\" : `1.${versionNumber - 1}`,\r\n        publishedBy: publisherId,\r\n        releaseNotes: releaseNotes.trim() || null,\r\n        contentSnapshot,\r\n        checksum,\r\n        isCurrent: true,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await tx.tallTale.update({\r\n      where: { id: taleId },\r\n      data: { status: \"PUBLISHED\", latestPublishedVersionId: created.id },\r\n    });\r\n    return created;\r\n  });\r\n  eventBus.emit(\"tall-tale:catalog\", {\r\n    type: \"catalog.updated\",\r\n    taleId,\r\n    versionId: version.id,\r\n    at: version.publishedAt.toISOString(),\r\n  });\r\n  return {\r\n    id: version.id,\r\n    versionNumber: version.versionNumber,\r\n    versionLabel: version.versionLabel,\r\n    checksum: version.checksum,\r\n    publishedAt: version.publishedAt.toISOString(),\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function parsePublishedSnapshot(raw: string): PublishedTaleSnapshot {\r\n  const snapshot = JSON.parse(raw) as PublishedTaleSnapshot;\r\n  if (snapshot.schemaVersion !== 1 || !Array.isArray(snapshot.chapters))\r\n    throw new Error(\"This published tale uses an unsupported schema version.\");\r\n  return snapshot;\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n17\r\nCreate a polished Studio landing page containing\r\n• Create New Tall Tale\r\n• Continue editing recent drafts\r\n• Published tales\r\n• Archived tales\r\n• Search\r\n• Sort\r\n• Duplicate tale\r\n• Archive tale\r\n• Open player preview\r\n• Open Captain controls\r\n• Last real save timestamp\r\n• Draft validation state\r\n• Latest published version\r\nEach tale card should clearly distinguish Draft, Published, Draft with unpublished changes, and Archived.\r\n6.2 New Tall Tale flow\r\nCreate a guided but quick creation flow with\r\n• Title\r\n• Slug generated but editable with validation\r\n• Subtitle\r\n• Short description\r\n• Long description\r\n• Cover image selection or upload\r\n• Theme selection or inherited project default\r\n• Player count range\r\n• Estimated duration\r\n• Visibility\r\n• Initial chapter title\r\nAfter creation, open the editor immediately.\r\n6.3 Editor shell\r\nThe editor should include\r\nTop bar\r\n• Tall Tale title and draft indicator\r\n• Back to Studio\r\n• Undo\r\n• Redo\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 18 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n18\r\n• Real autosave state\r\n• Preview\r\n• Play From Here when a block is selected\r\n• Validate\r\n• Publish\r\n• More menu for settings, duplicate, archive, version history\r\nLeft panel\r\n• Block Library tab\r\n• Chapters tab\r\n• Story Outline tab\r\n• Search blocks\r\n• Collapsible categories: Story, Direction and Location, Media, Reveal, Interaction, Logic\r\nCenter canvas\r\n• Vertical chapter timeline\r\n• Chapter headers\r\n• Block cards in order\r\n• Drag handles\r\n• Insertion indicators\r\n• Selected-block state\r\n• Multi-select only if safely implemented\r\n• Empty chapter drop zone\r\n• Add block button between cards\r\n• Clear connection/flow indicators\r\n• Collapsible chapters\r\n• Optional compact overview mode\r\nRight inspector\r\n• Selected block title and type\r\n• Block content fields\r\n• Presentation settings\r\n• Completion/verification settings\r\n• Asset pickers\r\n• Private creator notes\r\n• Validation errors and warnings\r\n• Delete block\r\n• Duplicate block\r\n• Move to chapter\r\nBottom or slide-up asset drawer\r\n• Images\r\n• Video\r\n• Audio\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 19 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n19\r\n• Documents\r\n• Collections\r\n• Search\r\n• Filters\r\n• Upload drop zone\r\n• Recently used\r\n• Unused assets\r\n• Drag asset onto compatible block fields\r\nUse the layout pattern that best fits the existing UI framework, but preserve these functional regions.\r\n6.4 Drag-and-drop behavior\r\nUse an established accessible drag-and-drop library already present in the project or add a well-supported one appropriate to \r\nthe framework.\r\nRequired behavior\r\n• Drag a block type from the library into a chapter.\r\n• Drag existing blocks within a chapter.\r\n• Drag blocks across chapters.\r\n• Show exact insertion position.\r\n• Persist order.\r\n• Update default next connections safely.\r\n• Support keyboard reordering or equivalent accessible controls.\r\n• Prevent accidental loss if persistence fails.\r\n• Roll back the local visual order or clearly show unsaved failure if the server rejects the change.\r\n• Do not trigger drag when the user is selecting text or using controls inside a block.\r\n6.5 Undo and redo\r\nImplement practical undo/redo for editor operations if compatible with the current architecture. At minimum support recent local \r\nstructural and field changes before autosave commits. Do not fake undo buttons.\r\n6.6 Autosave and concurrency\r\nImplement debounced autosave with truthful status. Prevent one stale browser tab from silently overwriting newer changes.\r\nUse optimistic concurrency through a revision number, updatedAt token, ETag, or equivalent.\r\nWhen a conflict occurs\r\n• Stop claiming the draft is saved.\r\n• Preserve the user's unsaved local work.\r\n• Explain that the draft changed elsewhere.\r\n• Offer a safe reload/compare path consistent with project capabilities.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 29 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n29\r\n• Cancellation where practical\r\n• Duplicate detection using checksum\r\n• Friendly handling of duplicate names\r\nDo not require creators to manage server paths or filenames.\r\n8.2 Supported Phase 1 media\r\nAt minimum\r\n• PNG\r\n• JPEG\r\n• WebP\r\n• AVIF if existing tooling supports it reliably\r\n• GIF only if the product intentionally supports animation\r\n• MP4/WebM for video according to browser and server support\r\n• MP3/Ogg/WAV according to existing stack\r\n• PDF or document assets only if the player already supports them; otherwise store them as creator resources without \r\npretending they render in-player\r\nEnforce configured size limits and MIME validation server-side, not only by filename extension.\r\n8.3 Image processing\r\nPreserve the original upload. Generate derivatives appropriate to the existing backend:\r\n• Thumbnail\r\n• Medium preview\r\n• Optimized player version\r\n• Optional blurred placeholder\r\n• Optional mobile derivative\r\nStore dimensions and metadata. Strip dangerous metadata where appropriate while preserving required orientation.\r\n8.4 Storage architecture\r\nUse the project's existing object storage or file-storage abstraction. If none exists, create a storage service interface that \r\nsupports local development storage now and future object storage later.\r\nDo not store large raw binary media directly inside ordinary relational table rows unless the existing architecture explicitly \r\nrequires it.\r\nUse safe opaque storage keys, not untrusted user filenames as paths.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 30 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n30\r\n8.5 Asset browser\r\nRequired filters\r\n• Media type\r\n• Chapter\r\n• Location\r\n• Artifact\r\n• Role\r\n• Tag\r\n• Collection\r\n• Recently used\r\n• Unused\r\nRequired actions\r\n• Select\r\n• Multi-select where useful\r\n• Edit metadata\r\n• Assign tags\r\n• Assign roles\r\n• Add to collection\r\n• Replace file\r\n• Download original for authorized creator\r\n• Archive/soft-delete\r\n• View usages\r\n8.6 Usage tracking and safe deletion\r\nBefore deletion, show every block, location, artifact, cover, or collection that uses the asset.\r\nDo not allow destructive deletion of a referenced asset without an explicit replacement or safe unlink process.\r\n8.7 Replace asset behavior\r\nReplacing the file behind a logical asset must preserve the asset ID and all references. Keep prior file revisions for rollback or \r\naudit if practical.\r\n8.8 Asset picker\r\nCreate a reusable asset picker component for inspector fields. It should support:\r\n• Search\r\n• Filters\r\n• Preview\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 31 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n31\r\n• Upload new\r\n• Select existing\r\n• Clear selection\r\n• Required-role hints\r\n• Compatibility checks by media type\r\n8.9 Asset collections\r\nAllow creators to create collections from the Asset Library and from location/artifact editors.\r\nExample collection layout\r\nSailor's Bounty Message Rock\r\nPresentation\r\n• Normal rock\r\n• Carved rock\r\n• Close-up message\r\n• Night reveal video\r\nFuture recognition references\r\n• Front angle\r\n• Left angle\r\n• Right angle\r\n• Distance view\r\n• Lantern view\r\nNegative references\r\n• Nearby wrong rock\r\n• Southern cave wall\r\n• Similar shoreline\r\nPhase 1 does not analyze these references, but it must preserve their roles and collection relationships.\r\n9. LOCATION AND ARTIFACT LIBRARIES\r\n9.1 Locations page\r\nProvide a location management view with\r\n• Create location\r\n• Search\r\n• Sort\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 32 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n32\r\n• Display image\r\n• Map image\r\n• Region\r\n• Player description\r\n• Captain notes\r\n• Reference collection\r\n• Used-by blocks\r\n• Duplicate\r\n• Archive\r\n9.2 Location editor\r\nInclude tabs or sections for\r\n• Overview\r\n• Player presentation\r\n• Maps and imagery\r\n• Verification references\r\n• Usage\r\n• Captain notes\r\nThe verification references section should explain that images can be collected now for a future vision helper. It should support \r\nassigning locationReference and negativeReference roles without claiming that recognition is active.\r\n9.3 Artifacts page\r\nProvide an artifact library with\r\n• Artwork\r\n• Name\r\n• Ordinary in-game object\r\n• Collection group\r\n• Reveal media\r\n• Lore\r\n• Used-by blocks\r\n• Archive\r\n9.4 Artifact persistence in sessions\r\nWhen a Collection Update or Artifact Reveal grants an artifact, store it against the session or player inventory according to \r\nexisting product behavior. Ensure idempotency and correct history display.\r\n10. PREVIEW AND TESTING WORKFLOWS\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 33 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n33\r\n10.1 Block preview\r\nFrom any selected block, allow the creator to render only that block using its draft configuration and assets.\r\nRequirements\r\n• Isolated preview state\r\n• No real session mutation\r\n• Accurate desktop and mobile viewport preview\r\n• Reduced-motion preview option\r\n• Replay transition\r\n• View validation errors\r\n10.2 Play From Here\r\nCreate a temporary preview session pinned to the current draft revision.\r\nRequirements\r\n• Start from selected block\r\n• Optionally seed required prior variables/artifacts\r\n• Clearly label Preview Mode\r\n• Never appear as a real active player session\r\n• Never write to real player history, achievements, or completed-tale records\r\n• Allow Captain simulation in a separate preview control panel or embedded test drawer\r\n• Dispose or expire preview sessions\r\n10.3 Full Tale preview\r\nAllow a creator to play the entire draft without publishing, still isolated from real progress.\r\n10.4 Device preview\r\nAt minimum, provide desktop and common mobile viewport presets. Do not pretend a pixel-perfect emulator is a real device, \r\nbut make responsive testing convenient.\r\n11. DRAFT VALIDATION AND PUBLISHING\r\n11.1 Validation engine\r\nBefore publishing, validate the complete draft.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 34 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n34\r\nErrors that block publishing should include\r\n• Missing title or invalid slug\r\n• No chapters\r\n• Chapter without an entry block\r\n• Broken or missing default connections\r\n• Block references a deleted or unavailable asset\r\n• Required field missing\r\n• Choice target missing\r\n• Condition target missing\r\n• Unreachable required block\r\n• Obvious infinite loop where detectable\r\n• Missing Tale Complete endpoint unless intentionally configured otherwise\r\n• Invalid text-answer configuration\r\n• Unsupported media type\r\n• Asset processing failed\r\n• Future vision provider selected as active even though not available in Phase 1\r\n• Private/internal asset assigned to a public player field\r\nWarnings may include\r\n• Unused assets\r\n• Empty optional descriptions\r\n• No cover image\r\n• Very large media\r\n• No mobile crop\r\n• Missing alt text\r\n• Chapter estimated duration missing\r\n• Block cannot be reached except through Captain jump\r\nDisplay validation issues in a panel with links that focus the affected chapter, block, asset, location, or artifact.\r\n11.2 Publish flow\r\nPublishing must\r\n1. Re-run server-side validation.\r\n2. Create an immutable published version.\r\n3. Snapshot or bind every required chapter, block, connection, public asset reference, location presentation, artifact \r\npresentation, condition, and completion configuration.\r\n4. Assign version number/label.\r\n5. Record publisher and timestamp.\r\n6. Mark it as current when requested.\r\n7. Make it eligible for the player catalog according to visibility and availability.\r\n8. Leave the working draft available for future edits.\r\n9. Record release notes.\r\n10. Broadcast catalog invalidation/update through the existing data-refresh mechanism where applicable.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 35 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n35\r\n11.3 Unpublished changes\r\nAfter publishing, any draft edit must visibly change the tale state to \"Published with unpublished changes\" rather than silently \r\naltering the published version.\r\n11.4 Version history\r\nProvide a version history page showing\r\n• Version\r\n• Publish timestamp\r\n• Publisher\r\n• Release notes\r\n• Current/non-current status\r\n• Number of active sessions pinned to version\r\n• Preview published version\r\n• Compare summary if feasible\r\n• Restore/copy published version into a new draft, without mutating the old version\r\n11.5 Catalog eligibility\r\nA published Tall Tale should appear on the player page only when\r\n• A current published version exists.\r\n• Visibility permits the current player.\r\n• It is not archived.\r\n• Availability rules, if any, pass.\r\nDraft-only tales must never appear.\r\n12. PLAYER EXPERIENCE INTEGRATION\r\n12.1 Dynamic tale catalog\r\nReplace or augment any hard-coded tale list with a real catalog query backed by published Tall Tales.\r\nEach player-facing card should support\r\n• Cover art\r\n• Title\r\n• Subtitle or description\r\n• Estimated duration\r\n• Player count\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 36 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n36\r\n• Status: new, in progress, completed, replayable, unavailable\r\n• Continue button when progress exists\r\n• Start button when eligible\r\n• Replay button when allowed\r\n• Version pinning handled internally\r\n12.2 New tale availability\r\nWhen an authorized creator publishes a new Tall Tale, it must become visible on the player page through the normal data \r\npath. No source-code edit, static JSON edit, manual route addition, or restart-specific hack should be necessary beyond \r\nnormal deployment/cache invalidation.\r\n12.3 Starting a tale\r\nStarting must\r\n• Create a real player session.\r\n• Pin it to the current published version.\r\n• Enter the first chapter and entry block.\r\n• Record history events.\r\n• Associate or invite a Captain according to current product rules.\r\n• Render the correct block through the shared player block registry.\r\n12.4 Runtime block rendering\r\nThe player runtime must render published block configurations, not Studio-only draft objects.\r\nBlock transitions should\r\n• Persist progress server-side.\r\n• Be idempotent.\r\n• Resume correctly after refresh.\r\n• Avoid replaying one-time grants.\r\n• Allow replaying presentation where explicitly permitted without re-completing progression.\r\n• Coordinate audio cleanup.\r\n• Respect reduced motion.\r\n12.5 History\r\nCompleted and prior sessions should remain viewable using the published version they used. Do not resolve old sessions \r\nagainst whichever draft happens to exist today.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 40 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n40\r\n• Create/update/reorder/duplicate/delete blocks\r\n• Manage connections\r\n• Upload/list/update/replace/archive assets\r\n• Manage tags and collections\r\n• Manage locations\r\n• Manage artifacts\r\n• Validate draft\r\n• Create preview session\r\n• Publish draft\r\n• List published versions\r\nPlayer APIs/services\r\n• List eligible published tales\r\n• Read public tale summary\r\n• Start/resume session\r\n• Read canonical current player state\r\n• Submit allowed player interaction\r\n• Submit text answer\r\n• Confirm player action\r\n• Read collection/history\r\nCaptain APIs/services\r\n• List controlled/active sessions\r\n• Read session detail\r\n• Approve/reject verification\r\n• Override verification\r\n• Release hint\r\n• Pause/resume\r\n• Rollback/jump\r\n• Trigger permitted presentation\r\nFuture helper API/service boundary\r\n• Pair helper\r\n• Read current scoped verification request\r\n• Submit standardized verification observation/result\r\n• Heartbeat/status\r\n• Revoke pairing\r\nOnly scaffold the future helper boundary and simulator now. Do not expose insecure unauthenticated advancement endpoints.\r\n16. SECURITY, PRIVACY, AND MEDIA SAFETY\r\n• Enforce authorization server-side.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 41 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n41\r\n• Validate all block configuration server-side.\r\n• Sanitize rich text or use a safe structured editor format.\r\n• Prevent path traversal in uploads.\r\n• Validate MIME type and file signatures where practical.\r\n• Store uploads under opaque keys.\r\n• Prevent draft/private media from leaking through public URLs or payloads.\r\n• Use signed URLs or authorized media endpoints if the existing storage model requires access control.\r\n• Rate limit answer submissions, Captain actions, upload endpoints, and future helper events as appropriate.\r\n• Do not log correct riddle answers, sensitive tokens, or full private asset URLs unnecessarily.\r\n• Record security-relevant Captain overrides.\r\n• Keep future vision evidence local by default in the eventual helper design. Phase 1 should not claim to upload or retain \r\nscreenshots.\r\n17. MIGRATION OF EXISTING CONTENT\r\nIf existing Tall Tale content is hard-coded, stored in static files, or represented by a simpler schema:\r\n• Create a migration or import path into the new Tall Tale, chapter, block, asset, and published-version model.\r\n• Preserve the current playable Forever Treasure experience.\r\n• Preserve current URLs or add safe redirects.\r\n• Preserve current active progress where feasible.\r\n• Do not require the user to manually rebuild existing content in the Studio merely to keep the site working.\r\n• Document any content that cannot be migrated automatically.\r\nCreate one valid editable draft corresponding to the current tale and one published version that reproduces the current player \r\nexperience as closely as practical.\r\n18. VISUAL AND INTERACTION REQUIREMENTS\r\n18.1 Studio visual language\r\nThe Studio should feel like a premium Cartographer's Table rather than a generic CMS.\r\nUse the existing design system and consider\r\n• Layered parchment surfaces\r\n• Subtle map-grid or chart lines\r\n• Brass/wood accents\r\n• Inked section labels\r\n• Restrained glass only where already used\r\n• Soft elevation and shadows\r\n• Clearly differentiated interactive controls\r\n• Animated but efficient panel transitions\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 42 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n42\r\nDo not sacrifice readability for theme.\r\n18.2 Motion\r\nStudio motion\r\n• Smooth panel opening\r\n• Clear drag lift and drop settling\r\n• Chapter collapse/expand\r\n• Asset drawer movement\r\n• Save-state transitions\r\n• Preview opening\r\nPlayer motion\r\n• More cinematic and deliberate\r\n• Transition durations configurable per block\r\n• Major image transformations capable of multi-second sequences\r\n• No blank waiting periods disguised as cinematic pacing\r\n18.3 Empty and error states\r\nDesign useful states for\r\n• No Tall Tales\r\n• No chapters\r\n• Empty chapter\r\n• No assets\r\n• Upload failed\r\n• Asset processing\r\n• Draft conflict\r\n• Validation failed\r\n• No published version\r\n• No active Captain sessions\r\n• Waiting for Captain\r\n• Connection lost\r\n• Preview session expired\r\n18.4 Confirmation dialogs\r\nUse explicit confirmation for\r\n• Archive tale\r\n• Delete referenced block\r\n• Delete/replace referenced asset\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 43 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n43\r\n• Publish\r\n• Rollback player session\r\n• Jump player session\r\n• Reset preview\r\n• Discard unsaved changes\r\n19. PERFORMANCE AND RELIABILITY\r\n• Lazy-load thumbnails and large media.\r\n• Avoid loading original full-resolution images into every asset grid card.\r\n• Paginate or virtualize large asset libraries.\r\n• Debounce search and autosave.\r\n• Cache published catalog safely with explicit invalidation.\r\n• Ensure block editor rerenders remain manageable with long tales.\r\n• Do not upload the same file repeatedly during autosave.\r\n• Resume or safely retry interrupted uploads where practical.\r\n• Use background derivative generation if the current stack supports jobs; otherwise return a truthful processing state.\r\n• Never block the entire editor while one thumbnail processes.\r\n20. TESTING REQUIREMENTS\r\nAdd automated tests using the repository's existing test stack.\r\n20.1 Unit tests\r\nCover\r\n• Block schema validation\r\n• Block registry behavior\r\n• Slug generation/validation\r\n• Draft validation\r\n• Connection resolution\r\n• Text-answer normalization\r\n• Verification-provider dispatch\r\n• Idempotency handling\r\n• Artifact grant idempotency\r\n• Condition evaluation\r\n• Published-version immutability\r\n• Catalog eligibility\r\n20.2 Service/integration tests\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 44 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n44\r\nCover\r\n• Create tale -> add chapter -> add blocks -> upload asset -> publish -> player catalog displays tale\r\n• Draft edits after publishing do not alter published session behavior\r\n• Start session pins correct version\r\n• Captain approval satisfies pending request and advances once\r\n• Duplicate approval does not advance twice\r\n• Player confirmation uses same verification outcome path\r\n• Simulated vision match uses same verification outcome path\r\n• Stale simulated vision event is rejected\r\n• Wrong-block simulated event is rejected\r\n• Artifact reveal refresh does not duplicate collection item\r\n• Preview session does not appear in real player history\r\n• Asset replacement preserves block references\r\n• Deleting a referenced asset is blocked or safely handled\r\n• Current existing tale still plays after migration\r\n20.3 End-to-end tests\r\nWhere the project supports browser E2E testing, cover the main golden path\r\n1. Creator opens Studio.\r\n2. Creates Tall Tale.\r\n3. Creates first chapter.\r\n4. Drags Narrative, Riddle, Arrival Check, Image Transformation, Artifact Reveal, and Tale Complete blocks into the timeline.\r\n5. Uploads before and after images.\r\n6. Configures alignment and previews transformation.\r\n7. Validates and publishes.\r\n8. Opens player catalog and sees the tale.\r\n9. Starts a session.\r\n10. Player reaches Arrival Check.\r\n11. Captain sees pending request and approves.\r\n12. Player receives transition without manual refresh.\r\n13. Image transformation plays.\r\n14. Artifact is granted once.\r\n15. Tale completes and appears in history.\r\n20.4 Accessibility tests\r\nAt minimum\r\n• Keyboard navigation through Studio controls\r\n• Accessible names for icon buttons\r\n• Focus handling in dialogs and drawers\r\n• Drag-and-drop alternative controls\r\n• Alt-text validation for player-facing images\r\n• Reduced-motion behavior\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 45 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n45\r\n• Status announcements for saving, publishing, errors, and Captain approval\r\n20.5 Migration tests\r\n• Existing data migrates successfully.\r\n• Rollback or backup path is documented.\r\n• Existing published tale remains playable.\r\n• No orphaned current sessions are created.\r\n21. OBSERVABILITY AND DIAGNOSTICS\r\nAdd structured diagnostics consistent with the project\r\n• Draft save failures\r\n• Publish validation failures\r\n• Publish completion\r\n• Asset upload/processing failures\r\n• Session progression failures\r\n• Verification request creation and resolution\r\n• Captain overrides\r\n• Duplicate/idempotent event handling\r\n• Unknown block type\r\n• Real-time connection failures\r\nDo not log sensitive answer values or private tokens.\r\nCreate a creator-visible diagnostics area or clear error details where appropriate, not merely console errors.\r\n22. DOCUMENTATION\r\nAdd or update project documentation covering\r\n• Tall Tale Studio overview\r\n• Domain model\r\n• Block registry and how to add a new block type\r\n• Draft versus published versions\r\n• Asset storage and derivatives\r\n• Player session/version pinning\r\n• Captain control flow\r\n• Verification provider abstraction\r\n• Future local helper pairing/event contract\r\n• Development verification simulator\r\n• Migration of existing content\r\n• Required environment variables\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 46 =====\r\nTALL TALE STUDIO  |  PHASE 1 CODEX IMPLEMENTATION BRIEF\r\n46\r\n• Storage configuration\r\n• Test commands\r\n• Build/run commands\r\nInclude a concise \"Future Vision Helper Integration\" document with\r\n• Pairing concept\r\n• Event schema\r\n• Security assumptions\r\n• Request lifecycle\r\n• Idempotency rules\r\n• Match/not-match/uncertain semantics\r\n• Why the helper may satisfy only an existing pending verification request\r\n• Which Phase 1 tables/services/endpoints it will use\r\n23. IMPLEMENTATION ORDER\r\nUse the following order unless repository realities require a documented adjustment.\r\nPhase A - Audit and domain foundation\r\n• Inspect current architecture.\r\n• Define migrations and model relationships.\r\n• Add block registry and schemas.\r\n• Add draft/published distinction.\r\n• Add asset storage abstraction.\r\n• Add progression and verification abstractions.\r\nPhase B - Runtime integration\r\n• Migrate or adapt existing tale content.\r\n• Render published blocks in the player experience.\r\n• Implement session pinning and progression events.\r\n• Route Captain approval through common verification.\r\nPhase C - Studio shell\r\n• Studio landing page.\r\n• New tale flow.\r\n• Chapter/timeline editor.\r\n• Inspector.\r\n• Autosave and validation status.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

\"The new tale could not be created.\");\r\n            setBusy(false);\r\n            return;\r\n          }\r\n          router.push(`/studio/tales/${body.id}`);\r\n        }}\r\n      >\r\n        <Link href=\"/studio\">â† Back to Studio</Link>\r\n        <p className=\"eyebrow\">Lay a fresh chart</p>\r\n        <h1>Create a New Tall Tale</h1>\r\n        <p>Start with the voyage identity and first chapter. value={title}\r\n              onChange={(event) => {\r\n                setTitle(event.target.value);\r\n                if (!slugEdited) setSlug(slugify(event.target.value));\r\n              }}\r\n );\r\n}\r\n\"use client\";\r\n\r\n/* eslint-disable @next/next/no-img-element -- Published asset URLs are authorized version-bound media responses. */\r\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\r\nimport { memory</p>\r\n        <h1>{state.tale.title}</h1>\r\n        <p>\r\n          {state.session.status === \"COMPLETED\"\r\n            ? `Completed ${new Date(state.session.completedAt ?? state.session.updatedAt).toLocaleString()}.`\r\n            : `This voyage is ${state.session.status.toLocaleLowerCase()}.`}\r\n        </p>\r\n        <h2>Recorded events</h2>\r\n        <ol>\r\n          {[...state.events].reverse().map((event) => (\r\n            <li key={event.id}>\r\n              <time>{new Date(event.createdAt).toLocaleString()}</time>\r\n              <strong>{event.eventType.replaceAll(/([A-Z])/g, \" $1\")}</strong>\r\n            </li>\r\n          ))}\r\n        </ol>\r\n        <Link className=\"brass-button\" href=\"/tales\">\r\n          Return to catalog\r\n        </Link>\r\n      </section>\r\n    </main>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\r\n\r\nexport async function GET() {\r\n  const tales = await db.tallTale.findMany({\r\n    where: { archivedAt: null, status: \"PUBLISHED\", visibility: \"PUBLIC\", latestPublishedVersionId: { not: null } },\r\n    orderBy: [{ featured: \"desc\" }, { sortOrder: \"asc\" }, { updatedAt: \"desc\" }],\r\n    include: { versions: { where: { isCurrent: true }, take: 1 } },\r\n  });\r\n  return NextResponse.json({\r\n    tales: tales\r\n      .filter((tale) => tale.versions.length)\r\n      .map((tale) => ({\r\n        id: tale.id,\r\n        slug: tale.slug,\r\n        title: tale.title,\r\n        subtitle: tale.subtitle,\r\n        shortDescription: tale.shortDescription,\r\n        coverAssetId: tale.coverAssetId,\r\n        coverUrl: tale.coverAssetId\r\n          ? `/api/media/${tale.coverAssetId}?variant=PREVIEW&version=${tale.versions[0].id}`\r\n          : null,\r\n        estimatedDuration: tale.estimatedDuration,\r\n        playerCountMin: tale.playerCountMin,\r\n        playerCountMax: tale.playerCountMax,\r\n        version: tale.versions[0].versionLabel,\r\n        versionId: tale.versions[0].id,\r\n      })),\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\r\n\r\nexport async function GET(_: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ taleSlug: string }> }) {\r\n  const tale = await db.tallTale.findFirst({\r\n    where: {\r\n      slug: (await context.params).taleSlug,\r\n      archivedAt: null,\r\n      status: \"PUBLISHED\",\r\n      visibility: { in: [\"PUBLIC\", \"UNLISTED\"] },\r\n    },\r\n    include: { versions: { where: { isCurrent: true }, take: 1 } },\r\n  });\r\n  const version = tale?.versions[0];\r\n  if (!tale || !version) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This Tall Tale is not available.\" }, { status: 404 });\r\n  return NextResponse.json({\r\n    tale: {\r\n      id: tale.id,\r\n      slug: tale.slug,\r\n      title: tale.title,\r\n      subtitle: tale.subtitle,\r\n      shortDescription: tale.shortDescription,\r\n      longDescription: tale.longDescription,\r\n      coverUrl: tale.coverAssetId ? `/api/media/${tale.coverAssetId}?variant=PREVIEW&version=${version.id}` : null,\r\n      estimatedDuration: tale.estimatedDuration,\r\n      playerCountMin: tale.playerCountMin,\r\n      playerCountMax: tale.playerCountMax,\r\n      contentWarnings: tale.contentWarnings,\r\n      version: version.versionLabel,\r\n    },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

});\r\n  } catch (cause) {\r\n    return apiError(cause);\r\n  }\r\n}\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\r\nimport { requireGmCapability, verifyCsrf } from \"@/lib/security\";\r\nimport ((!version || version.startsWith(\"draft:\")) && !(await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\")))\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This draft asset is private.\" }, { status: 403 });\n    const { variant, buffer } = await resolveAssetVariant(\n      (await context.params).assetId,\n      (url.searchParams.get(\"variant\") ?? \"OPTIMIZED\").toUpperCase(),\n      version,\n    );\n block = next.chapters.flatMap((chapter) => chapter.blocks).find((candidate) => candidate.id === selectedId);\n      if (block) mutator(block);\n    });\n  }\n\n  async function validate() {\n    if (!draft || !data) return;\n    if (dirty && !(await save(draft, false))) return;\n    setSaveState(\"Validatingâ€¦\");\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/studio/tales/${taleId}/validate`, {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": data.csrfToken },\n    });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as typeof validation & { error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok || !body) {\n      setError(body?.error ?? \"Validation failed.\");\n      return;\n    }\n    setValidation(body);\n    setSaveState(body.valid ? \"Draft validation passed\" : \"Draft validation failed\");\n  }\n  async function publish() {\n    if (!draft || !data) return;\n    if (dirty && !(await save(draft, false))) return;\n    const notes = window.prompt(\"Release notes for this immutable version:\", \"Phase 1 authoring release\");\n    if (notes === null || !window.confirm(\"Publish this saved draft as a new immutable player version?\")) return;\n    setSaveState(\"Publishingâ€¦\");\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/studio/tales/${taleId}/publish`, {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-csrf-token\": data.csrfToken },\n      body: JSON.stringify({ releaseNotes: notes }),\n    });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as { versionLabel?: string; validation?: typeof validation; error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok) {\n      setError(body.error ?? \"Publishing failed.\");\n      if (body.validation) setValidation(body.validation);\n      setSaveState(\"Publish failed\");\n      return;\n    }\n    setSaveState(`Published as version ${body.versionLabel}`);\n    await load();\n  }\n  async function preview(blockId?: string) {\n    if (!draft || !data) return;\n    if (dirty && !(await save(draft, false))) return;\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/studio/tales/${taleId}/preview`, {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-csrf-token\": data.csrfToken },\n      body: JSON.stringify({ blockId }),\n    });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as { url?: string; error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok || !body.url) return setError(body.error ?? \"Preview could not be started.\");\n    window.open(body.url, \"_blank\", \"noopener,noreferrer\");\n  }\n\n  async function upload(files: FileList | null) {\n    if (!files?.length || !data) return;\n    const form = new FormData();\n    for (const file of Array.from(files)) form.append(\"files\", file);\n    setSaveState(`Uploading ${files.length} file${files.length === 1 ? \"\" : \"s\"}â€¦`);\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/studio/tales/${taleId}/assets`, {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": data.csrfToken },\n      body: form,\n    });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as { error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok) {\n      setError(body.error ?? \"Upload failed.\");\n      setSaveState(\"Upload failed\");\n      return;\n    }\n    await load();\n    setAssetDrawer(true);\n  }\n\n  async function replaceAsset(asset: Asset, files: FileList | null) {\n    const file = files?.[0];\n    if (!file || !data) return;\n    if (!window.confirm(`Replace ${asset.displayName} while preserving its logical asset identity?`)) return;\n    const form = new FormData();\n    form.append(\"file\", file);\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/studio/assets/${asset.id}`, {\n      method: \"PUT\",\n      headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": data.csrfToken },\n      body: form,\n    });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as { error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok) return setError(body.error ?? \"Asset replacement failed.\");\n    await load();\n  }\n\n  async function editAsset(asset: Asset) {\n    if (!data) return;\n    const displayName = window.prompt(\"Display name:\", asset.displayName);\n    if (displayName === null) return;\n    const description = window.prompt(\"Description:\", asset.description ?? \"\");\n    if (description === null) return;\n    const tags = window.prompt(\"Comma-separated tags:\", asset.tags.join(\", \"));\n    if (tags === null) return;\n    const roles = window.prompt(\"Comma-separated roles:\", asset.roles.join(\", \"));\n    if (roles === null) return;\n    const collectionIds = window.prompt(\n      `Collection IDs (comma-separated). Available: ${data.collections.map((item) => `${item.name}=${item.id}`).join(\"; \") || \"none\"}`,\n      asset.collectionItems.map((item) => item.collectionId).join(\", \"),\n    );\n    if (collectionIds === null) return;\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/studio/assets/${asset.id}`, {\n      method: \"PATCH\",\n      headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-csrf-token\": data.csrfToken },\n      body: JSON.stringify({\n        displayName,\n        description,\n        tags: tags.split(\",\").map((item) => item.trim()),\n        roles: roles\n          .split(\",\")\n          .map((item) => item.trim())\n          .filter(Boolean),\n        collectionIds: collectionIds\n          .split(\",\")\n          .map((item) => item.trim())\n          .filter(Boolean),\n      }),\n    });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as { error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok) return setError(body.error ?? \"Asset metadata could not be saved.\");\n    await load();\n  }\n\n  async function libraryAction(\n    entity: \"collection\" | \"location\" | \"artifact\",\n    action: \"create\" | \"update\" | \"archive\",\n    id?: string,\n  ) {\n    if (!data) return;\n    let values: JsonObject = {};\n    if (action === \"create\" || action === \"update\") {\n      const existing =\n        entity === \"collection\"\n          ? data.collections.find((item) => item.id === id)\n          : entity === \"location\"\n            ? data.locations.find((item) => item.id === id)\n            : data.artifacts.find((item) => item.id === id);\n      const name = window.prompt(`${action === \"create\" ? \"Name the new\" : \"Rename\"} ${entity}:`, existing?.name);\n      if (!name) return;\n      values = { name };\n      if (entity === \"collection\") {\n        values.description = window.prompt(\"Collection description (optional):\", existing?.description ?? \"\") ?? \"\";\n        values.collectionType =\n          window.prompt(\n            \"Collection type (GENERAL, LOCATION_REFERENCE, NEGATIVE_REFERENCE, or ARTIFACT):\",\n            existing?.collectionType ?? \"GENERAL\",\n          ) ?? \"GENERAL\";\n      }\n      if (entity === \"location\") {\n        values.region = window.prompt(\"Region (optional):\", existing?.region ?? \"\") ?? \"\";\n        values.playerFacingDescription =\n          window.prompt(\"Player-facing description:\", existing?.playerFacingDescription ?? \"\") ?? \"\";\n        values.captainNotes = window.prompt(\"Private Captain notes:\", existing?.captainNotes ?? \"\") ?? \"\";\n        values.referenceCollectionId =\n          window.prompt(\"Reference collection ID (optional):\", existing?.referenceCollectionId ?? \"\") ?? \"\";\n      }\n      if (entity === \"artifact\") {\n        values.ordinaryGameObjectLabel =\n          window.prompt(\"Ordinary in-game object (optional):\", existing?.ordinaryGameObjectLabel ?? \"\") ?? \"\";\n        values.shortDescription = window.prompt(\"Short description:\", existing?.shortDescription ?? \"\") ?? \"\";\n        values.loreDescription = window.prompt(\"Lore description:\", existing?.loreDescription ?? \"\") ?? \"\";\n      }\n    } else if (!window.confirm(`Archive this ${entity}?`)) return;\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/studio/tales/${taleId}/library`, {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-csrf-token\": data.csrfToken },\n      body: JSON.stringify({ entity, action, id, data: values }),\n    });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as { error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok) return setError(body.error ?? \"Library update failed.\");\n    await load();\n  }\n\n  if (!authenticated)\n    return (\n      <main className=\"studio-auth-gate\">\n        <section>\n          <h1>Creator authentication required</h1>\n          <Link className=\"brass-button\" href=\"/quartermaster\">\n            Open Quartermaster login\n          </Link>\n        </section>\n      </main>\n    );\n  if (!data || !draft) return <main className=\"studio-loading\">{error || \"Unrolling the Tall Tale chartâ€¦\"}</main>;\n\n  const nav = [\"story\", \"settings\", \"assets\", \"locations\", \"artifacts\", \"versions\"] as const;\n  return (\n    <main className=\"tale-editor\">\n      <header className=\"editor-topbar\">\n        <div>\n          <Link href=\"/studio\">â† Studio</Link>\n          <span className=\"draft-mark\">Draft</span>\n          <h1>{draft.tale.title}</h1>\n        </div>\n        <div className=\"editor-history\">\n          <button disabled={!past.length} onClick={undo} aria-label=\"Undo last edit\">\n            â†¶ Undo\n          </button>\n          <button disabled={!future.length} onClick={redo} aria-label=\"Redo edit\">\n            â†· Redo\n          </button>\n        </div>\n        <p\n          className={`save-state ${saveState.includes(\"failed\") || saveState.includes(\"Conflict\") ? \"error\" : \"\"}`}\n          role=\"status\"\n          aria-live=\"polite\"\n        >\n          {saveState}\n        </p>\n        <div className=\"editor-primary-actions\">\n          <button onClick={() => void preview()}>Preview</button>\n          <button disabled={!selected} onClick={() => void preview(selected?.block.id)}>\n            Play From Here\n          </button>\n          <button onClick={() => void validate()}>Validate</button>\n          <button className=\"publish-button\" onClick={() => void publish()}>\n            Publish\n          </button>\n        </div>\n      </header>\n      <nav className=\"editor-section-nav\" aria-label=\"Tall Tale authoring sections\">\n        {nav.map((item) => (\n          <Link\n            key={item}\n            className={initialSection === item ? \"active\" : \"\"}\n            href={item === \"story\" ? `/studio/tales/${taleId}` : `/studio/tales/${taleId}/${item}`}\n          >\n            {item}\n          </Link>\n        ))}\n      </nav>\n      {error && (\n        <div className=\"editor-error\" role=\"alert\">\n          <span>{error}</span>\n          <button onClick={() => setError(\"\")}>Dismiss</button>\n        </div>\n      )}\n      {validation && (\n        <aside className={`validation-panel ${validation.valid ? \"valid\" : \"invalid\"}`}>\n          <header>\n            <strong>\n  …28 tokens truncated…      body.usages?.length\n                          ? `Still used by: ${body.usages.map((item) => item.label).join(\", \")}`\n                          : (body.error ?? \"Could not archive asset.\"),\n                      );\n                    else await load();\n                  }}\n                >\n                  Archive\n                </button>\n              </article>\n            ))}\n          </div>\n          <h3>Collections</h3>\n          <div className=\"record-list\">\n            {data.collections.map((item) => (\n              <article key={item.id}>\n                <strong>{item.name}</strong>\n                <span>{item.collectionType}</span>\n                <button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"collection\", \"update\", item.id)}>Edit</button>\n                <button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"collection\", \"archive\", item.id)}>Delete</button>\n              </article>\n            ))}\n          </div>\n        </LibraryPanel>\n      )}\n      {initialSection === \"locations\" && (\n        <LibraryPanel\n          title=\"Locations\"\n          eyebrow=\"Destinations and verification references\"\n          action={<button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"location\", \"create\")}>Create location</button>}\n        >\n          <p className=\"panel-note\">\n            Reference and negative-reference collections can be prepared now for the future vision helper. Recognition\n            is not active in Phase 1.\n          </p>\n          <div className=\"record-grid\">\n            {data.locations.map((item) => (\n              <article key={item.id}>\n                <p className=\"card-kicker\">{item.region || \"Uncharted region\"}</p>\n                <h3>{item.name}</h3>\n                <p>{item.playerFacingDescription || \"No player description yet.\"}</p>\n                <small>\n                  {item.referenceCollectionId ? \"Reference collection assigned\" : \"No verification references\"}\n                </small>\n                <button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"location\", \"update\", item.id)}>Edit</button>\n                <button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"location\", \"archive\", item.id)}>Archive</button>\n              </article>\n            ))}\n          </div>\n        </LibraryPanel>\n      )}\n      {initialSection === \"artifacts\" && (\n        <LibraryPanel\n          title=\"Artifacts\"\n          eyebrow=\"Lore and collection rewards\"\n          action={<button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"artifact\", \"create\")}>Create artifact</button>}\n        >\n          <div className=\"record-grid\">\n            {data.artifacts.map((item) => (\n              <article key={item.id}>\n                <p className=\"card-kicker\">{item.ordinaryGameObjectLabel || \"Story object\"}</p>\n                <h3>{item.name}</h3>\n                <p>{item.shortDescription || item.loreDescription || \"No lore has been written.\"}</p>\n                <button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"artifact\", \"update\", item.id)}>Edit</button>\n                <button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"artifact\", \"archive\", item.id)}>Archive</button>\n              </article>\n            ))}\n          </div>\n        </LibraryPanel>\n      )}\n      {initialSection === \"versions\" && (\n        <LibraryPanel\n          title=\"Version History\"\n          eyebrow=\"Immutable releases\"\n          action={<button onClick={() => void publish()}>Publish current draft</button>}\n        >\n          <div className=\"version-list\">\n            {!data.versions.length && <p>No published version exists yet.</p>}\n            {data.versions.map((version) => (\n              <article key={version.id}>\n                <span className={version.isCurrent ? \"current\" : \"\"}>v{version.versionLabel}</span>\n                <div>\n                  <strong>{new Date(version.publishedAt).toLocaleString()}</strong>\n                  <p>{version.releaseNotes || \"No release notes.\"}</p>\n                </div>\n                <small>\n                  {version.activeSessions} pinned session{version.activeSessions === 1 ? \"\" : \"s\"}\n                </small>\n              </article>\n            ))}\n          </div>\n        </LibraryPanel>\n      )}\n      {(assetDrawer || initialSection === \"assets\") && (\n        <aside className={`asset-drawer ${assetDrawer ? \"open\" : \"page-open\"}`} aria-label=\"Asset drawer\">\n          <header>\n            <div>\n              <p className=\"eyebrow\">Media hold</p>\n              <h2>Upload and reuse assets</h2>\n            </div>\n            <button onClick={() => setAssetDrawer(false)} aria-label=\"Close asset drawer\">\n              Ã—\n            </button>\n          </header>\n          <label\n            className=\"upload-drop\"\n            onDragOver={(event) => event.preventDefault()}\n            onDrop={(event) => {\n              event.preventDefault();\n              void upload(event.dataTransfer.files);\n            }}\n          >\n            <input\n              type=\"file\"\n              multiple\n              accept=\"image/png,image/jpeg,image/webp,image/avif,video/mp4,video/webm,audio/mpeg,audio/ogg,audio/wav,application/pdf\"\n              onChange={(event) => void upload(event.target.files)}\n            />\n            <strong>Drop files or choose from this device</strong>\n            <span>Originals are preserved; image thumbnails and player variants are generated.</span>\n          </label>\n          <div className=\"drawer-assets\">\n            {data.assets.slice(0, 12).map((asset) => (\n              <button\n                key={asset.id}\n                onClick={() => {\n                  if (selected)\n                    updateSelected((block) => {\n                      const firstAssetField = selectedDefinition?.fields.find(\n                        (field) =>\n                          field.kind === \"asset\" &&\n                          (!field.mediaTypes?.length || field.mediaTypes.includes(asset.mediaType)),\n                      );\n                      if (firstAssetField) block.configuration[firstAssetField.key] = asset.id;\n                    });\n                }}\n              >\n                <span>\n                  {asset.mediaType === \"IMAGE\" ? (\n                    <img src={`/api/media/${asset.id}?variant=THUMBNAIL`} alt=\"\" />\n                  ) : (\n                    asset.mediaType\n                  )}\n                </span>\n                <strong>{asset.displayName}</strong>\n              </button>\n            ))}\n          </div>\n        </aside>\n      )}\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction DropZone({ onDrop }: { onDrop: (event: React.DragEvent) => void }) {\n  return (\n    <div\n      className=\"timeline-drop\"\n      onDragOver={(event) => {\n        event.preventDefault();\n        event.dataTransfer.dropEffect = event.dataTransfer.effectAllowed === \"copy\" ? \"copy\" : \"move\";\n      }}\n      onDrop={onDrop}\n    >\n      <span>Drop here</span>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction Field({\n  field,\n  value,\n  assets,\n  locations,\n  artifacts,\n  onChange,\n}: {\n  field: InspectorField;\n  value: unknown;\n  assets: Asset[];\n  locations: LibraryRecord[];\n  artifacts: LibraryRecord[];\n  onChange: (value: unknown) => void;\n}) {\n  const label = (\n    <span>\n      {field.label}\n      {field.required && <b aria-label=\"required\"> *</b>}\n    </span>\n  );\n  if (field.kind === \"textarea\")\n    return (\n      <label>\n        {label}\n        <textarea rows={5} value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value)} />\n        {field.help && <small>{field.help}</small>}\n      </label>\n    );\n  if (field.kind === \"boolean\")\n    return (\n      <label className=\"check-field\">\n        <input type=\"checkbox\" checked={Boolean(value)} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.checked)} />\n        {label}\n      </label>\n    );\n  if (field.kind === \"number\")\n    return (\n      <label>\n        {label}\n        <input\n          type=\"number\"\n          value={typeof value === \"number\" ? value : \"\"}\n          onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value === \"\" ? null : Number(event.target.value))}\n        />\n      </label>\n    );\n  if (field.kind === \"select\")\n    return (\n      <label>\n        {label}\n        <select value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value)}>\n          {field.options?.map((option) => (\n            <option key={option.value} value={option.value}>\n              {option.label}\n            </option>\n          ))}\n        </select>\n      </label>\n    );\n  if (field.kind === \"asset\")\n    return (\n      <label>\n        {label}\n        <select value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value || null)}>\n          <option value=\"\">No asset selected</option>\n          {assets\n            .filter((asset) => !field.mediaTypes?.length || field.mediaTypes.includes(asset.mediaType))\n            .map((asset) => (\n              <option key={asset.id} value={asset.id}>\n                {asset.displayName} Â· {asset.mediaType.toLocaleLowerCase()}\n              </option>\n            ))}\n        </select>\n      </label>\n    );\n  if (field.kind === \"location\" || field.kind === \"artifact\") {\n    const records = field.kind === \"location\" ? locations : artifacts;\n    return (\n      <label>\n        {label}\n        <select value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value || null)}>\n          <option value=\"\">Choose {field.kind}</option>\n          {records.map((item) => (\n            <option key={item.id} value={item.id}>\n              {item.name}\n            </option>\n          ))}\n        </select>\n      </label>\n    );\n  }\n  if (field.kind === \"json\")\n    return (\n      <label>\n        {label}\n        <textarea\n          rows={5}\n          defaultValue={JSON.stringify(\n            value ?? ([\"acceptedAnswers\", \"hints\", \"choices\"].includes(field.key) ? [] : {}),\n            null,\n            2,\n          )}\n          onBlur={(event) => {\n            try {\n              onChange(JSON.parse(event.target.value));\n              event.target.setCustomValidity(\"\");\n            } catch {\n              event.target.setCustomValidity(\"Enter valid JSON.\");\n              event.target.reportValidity();\n            }\n          }}\n        />\n      </label>\n    );\n  return (\n    <label>\n      {label}\n      <input value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value)} />\n      {field.help && <small>{field.help}</small>}\n    </label>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction AlignmentEditor({\n  value,\n  assets,\n  beforeId,\n  afterId,\n  onChange,\n}: {\n  value: unknown;\n  assets: Asset[];\n  beforeId: string;\n  afterId: string;\n  onChange: (value: JsonObject) => void;\n}) {\n  const alignment =\n    value && typeof value === \"object\" && !Array.isArray(value)\n      ? (value as JsonObject)\n      : { x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1, rotation: 0, opacity: 50, focalX: 50, focalY: 50 };\n  const before = assets.find((asset) => asset.id === beforeId);\n  const after = assets.find((asset) => asset.id === afterId);\n  const set = (key: string, next: number) => onChange({ ...alignment, [key]: next });\n  return (\n    <fieldset className=\"alignment-editor\">\n      <legend>Before / after alignment</legend>\n      <div className=\"alignment-stage\">\n        {before && <img src={`/api/media/${before.id}?variant=PREVIEW`} alt=\"Before alignment reference\" />}\n        {after && (\n          <img\n            src={`/api/media/${after.id}?variant=PREVIEW`}\n            alt=\"After alignment reference\"\n            style={{\n              opacity: Number(alignment.opacity ?? 50) / 100,\n              transform: `translate(${Number(alignment.x ?? 0)}px, ${Number(alignment.y ?? 0)}px) scale(${Number(alignment.scale ?? 1)}) rotate(${Number(alignment.rotation ?? 0)}deg)`,\n            }}\n          />\n        )}\n      </div>\n      {(\n        [\n          [\"opacity\", 0, 100, 1],\n          [\"x\", -200, 200, 1],\n          [\"y\", -200, 200, 1],\n          [\"scale\", 0.5, 2, 0.01],\n          [\"rotation\", -20, 20, 0.5],\n        ] as const\n      ).map(([key, min, max, step]) => (\n        <label key={key}>\n          <span>\n            {key} <b>{String(alignment[key] ?? (key === \"scale\" ? 1 : 0))}</b>\n          </span>\n          <input\n            type=\"range\"\n            min={min}\n            max={max}\n            step={step}\n            value={Number(alignment[key] ?? (key === \"scale\" ? 1 : 0))}\n            onChange={(event) => set(key, Number(event.target.value))}\n          />\n        </label>\n      ))}\n      <button\n        type=\"button\"\n        onClick={() => onChange({ x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1, rotation: 0, opacity: 50, focalX: 50, focalY: 50 })}\n      >\n        Reset alignment\n      </button>\n    </fieldset>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction LibraryPanel({\n  title,\n  eyebrow,\n  action,\n  children,\n}: {\n  title: string;\n  eyebrow: string;\n  action: React.ReactNode;\n  children: React.ReactNode;\n}) {\n  return (\n    <section className=\"editor-single-panel library-panel\">\n      <header>\n        <div>\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">{eyebrow}</p>\n          <h2>{title}</h2>\n        </div>\n        {action}\n      </header>\n      {children}\n    </section>\n  );\n}\n\r\nimport { TaleEditor } from \"@/components/studio/TaleEditor\";\nimport { requireGmCapability } from \"@/lib/security\";\nexport async function TaleEditorSection({\n  taleId,\n  section,\n}: {\n  taleId: string;\n  section: \"settings\" | \"assets\" | \"locations\" | \"artifacts\" | \"versions\";\n}) {\n  return (\n    <TaleEditor\n      taleId={taleId}\n      initialSection={section}\n      authenticated={Boolean(await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\"))}\n    />\n  );\n}\n\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport { requireGmCapability, verifyCsrf } from \"@/lib/security\";\nimport { apiError } from \"@/tall-tale/api\";\nimport { archiveStudioTale, duplicateStudioTale, getStudioTale } from \"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\n\nexport async function GET(_: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ taleId: string }> }) {\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\");\n  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Creator authentication required.\" }, { status: 401 });\n  try {\n    return NextResponse.json({ csrfToken: session.csrfToken, ...(await getStudioTale((await context.params).taleId)) });\n  } catch (cause) {\n    return apiError(cause);\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function POST(request: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ taleId: string }> }) {\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\");\n  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Creator authentication required.\" }, { status: 401 });\n  if (!(await verifyCsrf(session)))\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"The creator session expired.\" }, { status: 403 });\n  try {\n    const { action } = (await request.json()) as { action: \"duplicate\" | \"archive\" | \"restore\" };\n    const { taleId } = await context.params;\n    if (action === \"duplicate\") return NextResponse.json(await duplicateStudioTale(taleId, session.userId));\n    if (action === \"archive\" || action === \"restore\")\n      return NextResponse.json(await archiveStudioTale(taleId, action === \"archive\"));\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unknown tale action.\" }, { status: 400 });\n  } catch (cause) {\n    return apiError(cause);\n  }\n}\n\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport { requireGmCapability, verifyCsrf } from \"@/lib/security\";\nimport { apiError } from \"@/tall-tale/api\";\nimport { startPreviewSession } from \"@/tall-tale/progression\";\nimport { setTaleSessionCookie } from \"@/tall-tale/session-cookie\";\n\nexport async function POST(request: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ taleId: string }> }) {\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\");\n  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Creator authentication required.\" }, { status: 401 });\n  if (!(await verifyCsrf(session)))\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"The creator session expired.\" }, { status: 403 });\n  try {\n    const body = (await request.json().catch(() => ({}))) as { blockId?: string };\n    const preview = await startPreviewSession((await context.params).taleId, session.userId, body.blockId);\n    await setTaleSessionCookie(preview.sessionId, preview.token);\n    return NextResponse.json({\n      ...preview,\n      token: undefined,\n slug,\r\n      archivedAt: null,\r\n      latestPublishedVersionId: { not: null },\r\n      visibility: { in: [\"PUBLIC\", \"UNLISTED\"] },\r\n    },\r\n    include: { versions: { where: { isCurrent: true }, take: 1 } },\r\n  });\r\n  const version = tale?.versions[0];\r\n  if (!tale || !version) throw new Error(\"This Tall Tale is not currently available.\");\r\n  const snapshot = parsePublishedSnapshot(version.contentSnapshot);\r\n  const first = blocksOf(snapshot)[0];\r\n  if (!first) throw new Error(\"This published tale has no playable entry block.\");\r\n  const token = correlationId: key,\r\n    });\r\n    const event = await enterBlock(tx, session, snapshot, first, \"player\", null, key);\r\n    return { session, event };\r\n  });\r\n  emit(created.session.id, created.event);\r\n  return { sessionId: created.session.id, token, taleSlug: slug, versionId: version.id };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function startPreviewSession(taleId: string, creatorId: string, randomUUID();\r\n  const created = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const session = await tx.taleSession.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        taleId,\r\n        ownerLabel: \"Studio },\r\n    });\r\n    await appendEvent(tx, session, {\r\n      eventType: \"previewStarted\",\r\n      sourceType: \"studio\",\r\n      sourceId: creatorId,\r\n      blockId: first.id,\r\n      idempotencyKey: `${key}:session`,\r\n      payload: { draftRevisionId: studio.draft.id },\r\n      correlationId: key,\r\n    });\r\n    const event = await enterBlock(tx, session, snapshot, first, \"studio\", creatorId, key);\r\n    return { session, event };\r\n  });\r\n  emit(created.session.id, created.event);\r\n  return { sessionId: created.session.id, token, taleSlug: studio.tale.slug, versionId: `draft:${studio.draft.id}` };\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction safeConfig(block: PublishedBlock) {\r\n  const result = structuredClone(block.configuration);\r\n  delete result.acceptedAnswers;\r\n    providerType: request.providerType,\r\n          requestedAt: request.requestedAt.toISOString(),\r\n          expiresAt: request.expiresAt?.toISOString() ?? null,\r\n          status: request.status,\r\n        }\r\n      : null,\r\n    inventory: parseJsonArray<string>(session.inventory),\r\n    variables: captain ? parseJsonObject(session.variables) : undefined,\r\n    events: event.sequence,\r\n      payload: parseJsonObject(event.payload),\r\n      createdAt: event.createdAt.toISOString(),\r\n    })),\r\n    assets: snapshot.assets.map((asset) => ({\r\n      ...asset,\r\n interactWithTaleSession(\r\n  sessionId: string,\r\n  token: string,\r\n  input: {\r\n    action: \"continue\" | \"confirm\" | \"answer\" | \"choice\" | \"timer\";\r\n    idempotencyKey: string;\r\n    answer?: string;\r\n    targetBlockId?: string;\r\n  },\r\n) {\r\n  const session = await db.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: sessionId }, include: { version: true } });\r\n  if (digest(token) !== session.accessTokenHash)\r\n    throw new Error(\"This voyage session is not available to this browser.\");\r\n  if (session.status !== \"ACTIVE\") throw new Error(`This session is ${session.status.toLowerCase()}.`);\r\n  const snapshot = snapshotOf(session);\r\n  const block = blockById(snapshot, session.currentBlockId);\r\n  if (!block) throw new Error(\"The current story block is unavailable.\");\r\n  const request = await db.taleVerificationRequest.findFirst({\r\n    where: { sessionId, blockId: block.id, status: \"PENDING\" },\r\n    orderBy: { requestedAt: \"desc\" = parseJsonObject(request.configurationSnapshot);\r\n    const answer = normalized(input.answer ?? \"\", config);\r\n    const accepted =\r\n      Array.isArray(config.acceptedAnswers) &&\r\n      config.acceptedAnswers.some(\r\n        (candidate) => typeof candidate === \"string\" && normalized(candidate, config) === answer,\r\n      );\r\n    if (!accepted) {\r\n      const event = await idempotencyKey: `${input.idempotencyKey}:rejected`,\r\n          payload: { providerType: \"textAnswer\" },\r\n          verificationRequestId: request.id,\r\n        }),\r\n      );\r\n      emit(sessionId, event);\r\n      return { accepted: false, state: await getTaleSessionState(sessionId, token) };\r\n    }\r\n  } else if (input.action === \"timer\") {\r\n    if (!request || request.providerType !== \"timer\" || !request.expiresAt || request.expiresAt > new Date())\r\n      throw new Error(\"The configured wait is not complete.\");\r\n  } else if (input.action === \"choice\") {\r\n    if (\r\n      !block.connections.some(\r\n        (connection) => connection.connectionType === \"CHOICE\" && connection.targetBlockId === input.targetBlockId,\r\n      )\r\n request.providerType },\r\n        verificationRequestId: request.id,\r\n        correlationId: input.idempotencyKey,\r\n      });\r\n    return completeBlock(tx, current, snapshot, block, \"player\", null, input.idempotencyKey, input.targetBlockId);\r\n  });\r\n  emit(sessionId, event);\r\n  return { accepted: true, state: await getTaleSessionState(sessionId, token) };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async submission.idempotencyKey,\r\n        providerType: submission.providerType,\r\n        providerInstanceId: submission.providerInstanceId,\r\n        result: submission.result,\r\n        confidence: submission.confidence,\r\n        evidence: JSON.stringify(submission.evidence ?? {}),\r\n        observedAt: new Date(submission.observedAt),\r\n        accepted: true,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    const current = await tx.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({\r\n      where: { id: request.sessionId },\r\n      include: { version: true },\r\n    });\r\n    if (submission.result !== \"match\")\r\n sourceId: actor.sourceId,\r\n        blockId: block.id,\r\n        idempotencyKey: `${submission.idempotencyKey}:outcome`,\r\n        payload: { providerType: submission.providerType, confidence: request.id },\r\n      data: { status: \"SATISFIED\", resolvedAt: new Date(), satisfiedByEventId: submission.eventId },\r\n    });\r\n    await appendEvent(tx, current, {\r\n      eventType: block.id,\r\n      idempotencyKey: `${submission.idempotencyKey}:satisfied`,\r\n      payload: { providerType: submission.providerType, confidence: submission.confidence },\r\n      verificationRequestId: request.id,\r\n      correlationId: submission.eventId,\r\n    });\r\n    return completeBlock(\r\n      tx,\r\n      current,\r\n      snapshot,\r\n      block,\r\n schemaVersion: 1,\r\n        eventId: randomUUID(),\r\n        idempotencyKey: input.idempotencyKey,\r\n        eventType: \"verification.result\",\r\n        providerType: request.providerType as evidence: { reason: input.reason ?? \"Captain action\", override: input.action === \"override\" },\r\n      },\r\n      { sourceType: input.action === \"override\" ? \"captainOverride\" : \"captainManual\", sourceId: actorId },\r\n    );\r\n  }\r\n  const event = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const current = await tx.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: sessionId status: input.action === \"pause\" ? \"PAUSED\" : \"ACTIVE\" },\r\n      });\r\n      return appendEvent(tx, current, {\r\n        eventType: input.action === \"pause\" ? \"sessionPaused\" : \"sessionResumed\",\r\n        sourceType: \"captain\",\r\n        sourceId: actorId,\r\n        blockId: current.currentBlockId,\r\n        idempotencyKey: input.idempotencyKey,\r\n        payload: {   idempotencyKey: input.idempotencyKey,\r\n        payload: { reason: input.reason ?? null },\r\n      });\r\n    const targetId = input.action === \"rollback\" ? session.events[1]?.blockId : tx.taleVerificationRequest.updateMany({\r\n      where: { sessionId, status: \"PENDING\" },\r\n      data: { status: \"CANCELLED\", resolvedAt: new Date() },\r\n    });\r\n    await async function listCaptainSessions() {\r\n  const sessions = await db.taleSession.findMany({\r\n    orderBy: { updatedAt: \"desc\" },\r\n    take: 100,\r\n    include: { tale: true, version: true, verificationRequests: { where: { status: \"PENDING\" }, take: 1 } },\r\n  });\r\n  return sessions.map((session) => ({\r\n    id: session.id,\r\n    taleId: session.taleId,\r\n    taleTitle: session.tale.title,\r\n    versionLabel: session.version?.versionLabel ?? \"Draft preview\",\r\n    ownerLabel: session.ownerLabel,\r\n    status: session.status,\r\n    previewMode: session.previewMode,\r\n    currentBlockId: session.currentBlockId,\r\n    pendingVerification: session.verificationRequests[0]\r\n      ? {\r\n          id: session.verificationRequests[0].id,\r\n          providerType: session.verificationRequests[0].providerType,\r\n          requestedAt: session.verificationRequests[0].requestedAt.toISOString(),\r\n        }\r\n      : null,\r\n    lastEventAt: session.updatedAt.toISOString(),\r\n    connected: Boolean(session.lastHeartbeatAt && Date.now() - session.lastHeartbeatAt.getTime() < 45000),\r\n  }));\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function createHelperPairing(sessionId: string, deviceId: string) {\r\n  const session = await db.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: sessionId } });\r\n  if (!session.publishedVersionId || session.previewMode || ![\"ACTIVE\", \"PAUSED\"].includes(session.status))\r\n    throw new Error(\"Helper devices can only pair with an active published tale session.\");\r\n  const token = randomBytes(32).toString(\"base64url\");\r\n  const pairing = await db.taleHelperPairing.create({\r\n    data: { sessionId, deviceId, tokenHash: digest(token), expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 15 * 60 * 1000) },\r\n  });\r\n  return { pairingId: pairing.id, token, expiresAt: pairing.expiresAt.toISOString() };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function submitHelperVerification(token: string, submission: VerificationSubmission) {\r\n  const pairing = await db.taleHelperPairing.findFirst({\r\n    where: { tokenHash: digest(token), status: \"ACTIVE\", revokedAt: null, expiresAt: { gt: new Date() } },\r\n  });\r\n  if (!pairing || pairing.sessionId !== submission.sessionId) throw new VerificationRejectedError(\"invalidPairing\");\r\n  await db.taleHelperPairing.update({ where: { id: pairing.id ## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-17T22:41:22.632Z

The audit is paying off: the service layer already has duplicate/archive operations, but the editor never exposed them, and helper pairing stopped at “pair + submit.” I’m wiring those dormant capabilities into complete, auditable workflows now, including scoped helper status/heartbeat and Captain revocation—without giving the helper any authority to advance the story directly.
