[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.9 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.8 seconds\nOutput:\n\r\n> ---\r\nname: control-in-app-browser\r\ndescription: \"Control the in-app Browser for opening, navigating, inspecting visible or interactive page state, \r\nclicking, typing, screenshots, and local web testing. It can have existing signed-in sessions. For semantic operations \r\non linked resources, prefer a purpose-built connector, API, or CLI when available.\"\r\n---\r\n\r\n# Browser\r\n## Stop: choose the right surface before any browser action\r\n\r\nExplicit browser intent wins: if the user names the in-app browser or Chrome, or asks to open, show, or navigate to a \r\npage; inspect its visual or interactive state; or interact with its UI, continue with Browser and do not substitute a \r\nconnector.\r\n\r\nOtherwise, treat a URL or open browser tab as context, not browser intent. Earlier Browser use does not make later \r\nsemantic work browser-first. Before each semantic operation on a linked resource, you MUST query available and \r\ndeferred tools for an applicable connector, API, or CLI. Reading this skill or scanning visible tools does not count. \r\nDo not initialize Browser for that operation until the query is complete. Use the non-browser tool when available. If \r\nit handles the current operation, continue the larger workflow without Browser for that operation. Use Browser when no \r\nsuch tool exists, the tool cannot access the resource or lacks a required capability, or UI work remains; use \r\navailable browser context before asking the user to repeat it.\r\n\r\nUse this skill for browser automation tasks such as inspecting pages, navigating, testing local apps, clicking, \r\ntyping, taking screenshots, and reading visible page state.\r\n\r\nIf this plugin is listed as available in the session, treat that as mandatory reading before browser work. Open and \r\nfollow this skill before saying that Browser is unavailable and before falling back to standalone Playwright or \r\nComputer Use.\r\n\r\nDo not skip this skill just because Computer Use MCP tool calls are directly visible or appear easier to invoke. The \r\npresence of Computer Use tools is not evidence that Computer Use is the preferred browser surface.\r\n\r\n## Setup Documentation\r\nUse `await agent.documentation.get(\"<name>\")` when one of these setup topics applies:\r\n- `bootstrap-troubleshooting`: read when browser setup succeeds but discovery or selection fails\r\n- `chrome-troubleshooting`: read when Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication fails\r\n\r\n## Bootstrap\r\nThese setup details are internal. User-facing progress updates should be less technical in nature. Never mention `Node \r\nREPL`, `node_repl`, `REPL`, JavaScript sessions, module exports, reading documentation, or loading instructions unless \r\na user is asking for that exact information. If setup or recovery is needed, describe it naturally as connecting to \r\nthe browser or retrying the browser connection.\r\n\r\nThe `browser-client` module is the core entry point for browser use, and is available under \r\n`scripts/browser-client.mjs` in this plugin's root directory. ALWAYS import it using an absolute path. IMPORTANT: If \r\nthis path cannot be found, stop and report that this plugin is missing `scripts/browser-client.mjs`. NEVER use the \r\nbuilt in `browser-client` library.\r\n\r\nRun browser setup code through the Node REPL `js` tool. In this environment the callable tool id typically appears as \r\n`mcp__node_repl__js`. If it is not already available, use tool discovery for `node_repl js` without setting a result \r\nlimit. You need the `js` execution tool: `js_reset` only clears state, and `js_add_node_module_dir` only changes \r\npackage resolution. Do not call either helper while trying to expose `js`. If `js` is still not available, search \r\nagain for `node_repl js` with `limit: 10`.\r\n\r\nInitialize the runtime once per fresh Node session. If `agent.browsers` already exists, reuse it; do not import or \r\ninitialize another browser runtime.\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.agent?.browsers == null) {\r\n  const { setupBrowserRuntime } = await import(\"<plugin root>/scripts/browser-client.mjs\");\r\n  await setupBrowserRuntime({ globals: globalThis });\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnce a browser connection is established, reuse its existing browser binding across later turns and do not reread this \r\nskill. Once you have read a browser's complete documentation, do not read it again unless you select a different \r\nbrowser.\r\n\r\nA tab binding is separate from its browser binding. If a later turn reports that a tab is missing, stale, closed, or \r\nnot part of the current browser session, discard that tab binding and obtain or create a fresh tab from the existing \r\nbrowser binding. An empty `browser.tabs.list()` or `browser.user.openTabs()` result is normal after tab cleanup and \r\ndoes not invalidate the browser binding. Never call `agent.browsers.get*` to recover a tab; only an explicit \r\nbrowser-disconnected error invalidates the binding.\r\n\r\n## Browser selection\r\n\r\nThe scenarios below are for the initial browser selection only. Before calling any `agent.browsers.get*` method, reuse \r\nan existing `globalThis.browser`, `globalThis.iab`, or `globalThis.chrome` binding that already serves the task. A new \r\nuser turn does not invalidate a browser binding or require another selection or documentation call.\r\n\r\nSelect the initial browser with exactly one of these scenarios, in the order\r\nshown. An explicit request for the in-app browser or Chrome always wins over URL\r\nselection. Never call `getForUrl()` when the user names a browser.\r\n\r\nApp-provided in-app-browser context is ambient UI state, not a user instruction to select or switch browsers. Only the \r\ntext of the user's request can explicitly choose a browser.\r\n\r\nDo not inspect browser cookies, local storage, profiles, passwords, or session stores. Browser discovery must remain \r\nread-only.\r\n\r\nWhen authentication blocks requested browser navigation, do not replace it with web search, a search engine, another \r\nsite, or another source merely to bypass sign-in.\r\n\r\n### The user explicitly requests a browser\r\n\r\nA plugin mention in the user's request explicitly names its browser.\r\n`[@Browser](plugin://browser@openai-bundled)` names the in-app browser.\r\n`[@Chrome](plugin://chrome@openai-bundled)`,\r\n`[@chrome-internal](plugin://chrome-internal@openai-bundled)`, and\r\n`[@chrome-dev](plugin://chrome-dev@openai-bundled)` name Chrome. Follow the\r\ncorresponding explicit-browser scenario below.\r\n\r\nThe in-app browser is available only when the Browser skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests \r\nthe in-app browser and that skill is available, use a distinct persistent binding and immediately read its complete \r\ndocumentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.iab == null) {\r\n  globalThis.iab = await agent.browsers.get(\"iab\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await iab.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests the in-app browser but the Browser skill is not available, report that the in-app \r\nbrowser is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nChrome is available only when the Chrome skill is listed for the session. Files outside of the project root are not resolved.\n\nThe reason files are not resolved outside of the project root is to improve cache validation, reduce filesystem watching overhead, and reduce the number of resolving steps needed.\n\nNext.js automatically detects the root directory of your project. It does so by looking for one of these files:\n\n  * `pnpm-lock.yaml`\n  * `package-lock.json`\n  * `yarn.lock`\n  * `bun.lock`\n  * `bun.lockb`\n\nIf you have a different project structure, for example if you don't use workspaces, you can manually set the `root` option:\n\nnext.config.js\n    \n    `const path = require('path')\n    module.exports = {\n      turbopack: {\n        root: path.join(__dirname, '..'),\n      },\n    }`\n\nTo resolve files from linked dependencies outside the project root (via `npm link`, `yarn link`, `pnpm link`, etc.), you must configure the `turbopack.root` to the parent directory of both the project and the linked dependencies.\n--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\nnext.config.js: output | Next.js (https://nextjs.org/docs/15/app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/output)\nciteturn0search1 [wordlim: 200] Published: 9 months ago; Crawled: 4 months ago; To leverage the `.nft.json` files emitted to the `.next` output directory, you can read the list of files in each trace that are relative to the `.nft.json` file and then copy them to your deployment location. ...       outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(__dirname, '.. ... Previous optimizePackageImports\n      // this includes files from the monorepo base two directories up\n      outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(__dirname, '../../'),\n    }`\n\n  * There are some cases in which Next.js might fail to include required files, or might incorrectly include unused files. In those cases, you can leverage `outputFileTracingExcludes` and `outputFileTracingIncludes` respectively in `next.config.js`. Each option accepts an object whose keys are route globs (matched with picomatch against the route path, e.g. `/api/hello`) and whose values are glob patterns resolved from the project root that specify files to include or exclude in the trace.\n\nnext.config.js\n    \n    `module.exports = {\n      outputFileTracingExcludes: {\n        '/api/hello': ['./un-necessary-folder/**/*'],\n      },\n      outputFileTracingIncludes: {\n        '/api/another': ['./necessary-folder/**/*'],\n        '/api/login/\\\\[\\\\[\\\\.\\\\.\\\\.slug\\\\]\\\\]': [\n          './node_modules/aws-crt/dist/bin/**/*',\n        ],\n      },\n    }`\n\nUsing a `src/` directory does not change how you write these options:\n\n  * Keys still match the route path (`'/api/hello'`, `'/products/[id]'`, etc.).\n  * Values can reference paths under `src/` since they are resolved relative to the project root.\n\nnext.config.js\n    \n    `module.exports = {\n      outputFileTracingIncludes: {\n        '/products/*': ['src/lib/payments/**/*'],\n        '/*': ['src/config/runtime/**/*.json'],\n      },\n      outputFileTracingExcludes: {\n        '/api/*': ['src/temp/**/*', 'public/large-logs/**/*'],\n      },\n    }`\n\nYou can also target all routes using a global key like `'/*'`:\n\nnext.config.js\n    \n    `module.exports = {\n      outputFileTracingIncludes: {\n        '/*': ['src/i18n/locales/**/*.json'],\n      },\n    }`\n\nThese options are applied to server traces and do not affect routes that do not produce a server trace file:\n\n  * Edge Runtime routes are not affected.\n  * Fully static pages are not affected.\n\nIn monorepos or when you need to include files outside the app folder, combine `outputFileTracingRoot` with includes:\n\nnext.config.js\n    \n    `const path = require('path')\n     \n    module.exports = {\n      // Trace from the monorepo root\n      outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(__dirname, '../../'),\n      outputFileTracingIncludes: {\n        '/route1': ['../shared/assets/**/*'],\n      },\n    }`\n\n> Good to know:\n> \n>   * Prefer forward slashes (`/`) in patterns for cross-platform compatibility.\n>   * Keep patterns as narrow as possible to avoid oversized traces (avoid `**/*` at the repo root).\n\nCommon include patterns for native/runtime assets:\n\nnext.config.js\n    \n    `module.exports = {\n      outputFileTracingIncludes: {\n        '/*': ['node_modules/sharp/**/*', 'node_modules/aws-crt/dist/bin/**/*'],\n      },\n with Turbopack. ...         root: path.join(__dirname, '..'), ... > Good to know: Prior to Next.js version 13.4.4, `turbo.rules` was named `turbo.loaders` and only accepted file extensions like `.mdx` instead of `*.mdx`.\nIt does so by looking for one of these files:\n\n  * `pnpm-lock.yaml`\n  * `package-lock.json`\n  * `yarn.lock`\n  * `bun.lock`\n  * `bun.lockb`\n\nIf you have a different project structure, for example if you don't use workspaces, you can manually set the `root` option:\n\nnext.config.js\n    \n    `const path = require('path')\n    module.exports = {\n      turbopack: {\n        root: path.join(__dirname, '..'),\n      },\n    }`\n\n### Configuring webpack loaders\n\nIf you need loader support beyond what's built in, many webpack loaders already work with Turbopack. There are currently some limitations:\n\n  * Only a core subset of the webpack loader API is implemented. Currently, there is enough coverage for some popular loaders, and we'll expand our API support in the future.\n  * Only loaders that return JavaScript code are supported. Loaders that transform files like stylesheets or images are not currently supported.\n  * Options passed to webpack loaders must be plain JavaScript primitives, objects, and arrays. For example, it's not possible to pass `require()` plugin modules as option values.\n--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\nGuides: Development Environment | Next.js (https://nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/local-development)\nciteturn0search3 [wordlim: 200] Published: 4 months ago; Crawled: 4 months ago; If you're using a third-party terminal like iTerm or Ghostty, add that to the list. ... You can interpret the file using `npx next internal trace [path-to-file]`: ...     `npx next internal turbo-trace-server .next/dev/trace-turbopack` ... Share the trace file generated in the Turbopack Tracing section and share it on GitHub Discussions or Discord.\n## Tools for finding problems\n\n### Detailed fetch logging\n\nUse the `logging.fetches` option in your `next.config.js` file, to see more detailed information about what's happening during development:\n    \n    `module.exports = {\n      logging: {\n        fetches: {\n          fullUrl: true,\n        },\n      },\n    }`\n\nLearn more about fetch logging.\n\n### Turbopack tracing\n\nTurbopack tracing is a tool that helps you understand the performance of your application during local development. It provides detailed information about the time taken for each module to compile and how they are related.\n\n  1. Make sure you have the latest version of Next.js installed.\n\n  2. Generate a Turbopack trace file:\n\n[Button: pnpm][Button: npm][Button: yarn][Button: bun]\n\nTerminal\n    \n    `NEXT_TURBOPACK_TRACING=1 pnpm dev`\n\n  3. Navigate around your application or make edits to files to reproduce the problem.\n\n  4. Stop the Next.js development server.\n\n  5. A file called `trace-turbopack` will be available in the `.next/dev` folder.\n\n  6. You can interpret the file using `npx next internal trace [path-to-file]`:\n    \n    `npx next internal trace .next/dev/trace-turbopack`\n\nOn versions where `trace` is not available, the command was named `turbo-trace-server`:\n    \n    `npx next internal turbo-trace-server .next/dev/trace-turbopack`\n\n  7. Once the trace server is running you can view the trace at https://trace.nextjs.org/.\n\n  8. By default the trace viewer will aggregate timings, in order to see each individual time you can switch from \"Aggregated in order\" to \"Spans in order\" at the top right of the viewer.\n\n> Good to know: The trace file is place under the development server directory, which defaults to `.next/dev`. This is controllable using the `isolatedDevBuild` flag in your Next config file.\n\n### Still having problems?\n\nShare the trace file generated in the Turbopack Tracing section and share it on GitHub Discussions or Discord.\n--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\nnext.config.js Options: output | Next.js (https://nextjs.org/docs/13/pages/api-reference/next-config-js/output)\nciteturn0search4 [wordlim: 200] Published: 3.1 years ago; Crawled: 5 months ago; To leverage the `.nft.json` files emitted to the `.next` output directory, you can read the list of files in each trace that are relative to the `.nft.json` file and then copy them to your deployment location. ...         outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(__dirname, '.. ...           // control the log level of the turbotrace, default is `error` ...           // if there is `process.cwd()` expression in your code, you can set this option to tell `turbotrace` the value of `process.cwd()` while tracing.\nTo include files outside of this folder you can set `experimental.outputFileTracingRoot` in your `next.config.js`.\n\npackages/web-app/next.config.js\n    \n    `module.exports = {\n      experimental: {\n        // this includes files from the monorepo base two directories up\n        outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(__dirname, '../../'),\n      },\n    }`\n\n  * There are some cases in which Next.js might fail to include required files, or might incorrectly include unused files. In those cases, you can leverage `experimental.outputFileTracingExcludes` and `experimental.outputFileTracingIncludes` respectively in `next.config.js`. Each config accepts an object with minimatch globs for the key to match specific pages and a value of an array with globs relative to the project's root to either include or exclude in the trace.\n\nnext.config.js\n    \n    `module.exports = {\n      experimental: {\n        outputFileTracingExcludes: {\n          '/api/hello': ['./un-necessary-folder/**/*'],\n        },\n        outputFileTracingIncludes: {\n          // control if the log of turbotrace should contain the details of the analysis, default is `false`\n          logDetail?: boolean\n          // show all log messages without limit\n          // turbotrace only show 1 log message for each categories by default\n          logAll?: boolean\n          // control the context directory of the turbotrace\n          // files outside of the context directory will not be traced\n          // set the `experimental.outputFileTracingRoot` has the same effect\n          // if the `experimental.outputFileTracingRoot` and this option are both set, the `experimental.turbotrace.contextDirectory` will be used\n          contextDirectory?: string\n          // if there is `process.cwd()` expression in your code, you can set this option to tell `turbotrace` the value of `process.cwd()` while tracing.\n          // for example the require(process.cwd() + '/package.json') will be traced as require('/path/to/cwd/package.json')\n          processCwd?: string\n          // control the maximum memory usage of the `turbotrace`, in `MB`, default is `6000`.\n          memoryLimit?: number\n        },\n      },\n    }`\n\nWas this helpful?--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\nAPI Reference: Turbopack | Next.js (https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/turbopack)\nciteturn0search5 [wordlim: 200] Published: 4 months ago; Sign in through Quartermaster and use Studio to edit it. Asset originals and generated derivatives live under ignored `.data/tall-tale-assets` by default. Override that location with `TALL_TALE_ASSET_ROOT`; set the upload ceiling with `TALL_TALE_MAX_UPLOAD_MB`.\n+The seed creates `development-studio-voyage`, its editable draft, and published version 1.0 without replacing the original campaign seed. Sign in through Quartermaster and use Studio to edit it. Asset originals and generated derivatives live under ignored `.data/tall-tale-assets` by default. Override that location with an absolute `TALL_TALE_ASSET_ROOT`; set the upload ceiling with `TALL_TALE_MAX_UPLOAD_MB`.\n*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

The repository's end-of-task synchronization workflow inspects additions, modifications, renames, moves, and deletions here and, after safety checks, can commit and push them through the same approved branch workflow used for `Codex_Chats`. The files are not copied into the conversation archive or packaged into a second store.\r\n\r\n## Working from another computer\r\n\r\nBefore relying on or editing these documents:\r\n\r\n1. Confirm that this is the intended repository and branch with `git remote -v`, `git branch --show-current`, and `git status --short --branch`.\r\n2. Preserve and review any local modifications. Do not reset, clean, restore, or force-push them away.\r\n3. Run `git fetch --prune origin`, then compare the branch with its upstream using `git rev-list --left-right --count \"HEAD...@{upstream}\"`.\r\n4. If the branch is only behind and the working tree is clean, update it with `git pull --ff-only`. If it is ahead, diverged, or locally modified, reconcile the work deliberately before editing these documents.\r\n5. If Git reports a conflict, preserve both versions and resolve it visibly. Do not push unresolved conflict markers. For an unmergeable binary document, retain clearly named local and remote variants for review when repository conventions permit.\r\n\r\nThis pull-before-edit check matters: a Codex session on a different computer must synchronize the latest repository state before treating local `Development_Docs` content as current.\r\n\r\n## Safety and change behavior\r\n\r\nDo not store secrets, credentials, private keys, access or API tokens, production database exports, private authentication caches, or sensitive company information here unless both the repository and the specific contents have been explicitly approved for that purpose. This repository may be publicly visible. Temporary, recovery, credential-shaped, and obvious secret files are ignored, and the synchronizer also checks changed file names, bounded textual content, and file sizes before staging.\r\n\r\nDeleting a tracked file locally and committing that deletion also removes it from the GitHub version after the commit is pushed. Git history can still preserve earlier versions of documents that were changed or deleted, subject to the repository's retention and history policies.\r\n\r\nPreserve authored formatting and native file types. Do not rewrite a document merely to normalize it, and do not use this directory as a dumping ground for terminal output, build artifacts, dependency archives, database dumps, application installers, or routine generated test results.\r\n\r\nGit tracks files rather than empty directories. requireGmCapability, verifyCsrf } from \"@/lib/security\";\r\nimport { apiError } from \"@/tall-tale/api\";\r\nimport { ingestAsset } from \"@/tall-tale/assets\";\r\nimport { getStudioTale } from \"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\r\nimport { consumeRateLimit, rateLimitHeaders } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\r\n\r\nexport async function GET(_: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ taleId: string }> }) {\r\n  if (!(await requireGmCapability(\"MANAGE_ASSETS\")))\r\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Asset permission required.\" }, { status: 403 });\r\n  try {\r\n    const studio = await getStudioTale((await context.params).taleId);\r\n    return NextResponse.json({ assets: studio.assets, collections: studio.collections });\r\n  } catch (cause) {\r\n    return apiError(cause);\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function POST(request: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ taleId: string }> }) {\r\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"MANAGE_ASSETS\");\r\n  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Asset permission required.\" }, { status: 403 });\r\n  if (!(await verifyCsrf(session)))\r\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"The creator session expired.\" }, { status: 403 });\r\n  try {\r\n    const rate = consumeRateLimit(`tale-upload:${session.userId}`, { limit: 30, windowMs: 15 * 60_000 });\r\n    if (!rate.allowed)\r\n      return NextResponse.json(\r\n        { error: \"The upload limit was reached. Wait before adding more media.\" },\r\n        { status: 429, headers: rateLimitHeaders(rate) },\r\n      );\r\n    const data = await request.formData();\r\n    const files = data.getAll(\"files\").filter((item): item is File => item instanceof File);\r\n    if (!files.length) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Choose at least one file.\" }, { status: 400 });\r\n    const assets = [];\r\n    for (const file of files) assets.push(await ingestAsset((await context.params).taleId, file, session.userId));\r\n    return NextResponse.json({ assets }, { status: 201 });\r\n  } catch (cause) {\r\n    return apiError(cause);\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

path.normalize(configured);\r\n  }\r\n  return path.join(process.cwd(), \".data\", \"tall-tale-assets\");\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction keyPath(storageKey: string) {\r\n  if (!/^[a-f0-9-]+\\/[a-f0-9-]+\\.[a-z0-9]+$/.test(storageKey)) throw new Error(\"Invalid opaque storage key.\");\r\n  const root = storageRoot();\r\n  const resolved = path.join(/* turbopackIgnore: true */ root, storageKey);\r\n  if (!resolved.startsWith(`${root}${path.sep}`)) throw new Error(\"Invalid storage path.\");\r\n  return resolved;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport interface TaleAssetStorage {\r\n  write(storageKey: string, buffer: Buffer): Promise<void>;\r\n  read(storageKey: string): Promise<Buffer>;\r\n}\r\n\r\nclass LocalTaleAssetStorage implements TaleAssetStorage {\r\n  async write(storageKey: string, buffer: Buffer) {\r\n    const target = keyPath(storageKey);\r\n    await mkdir(/* turbopackIgnore: true */ path.dirname(target), { recursive: true });\r\n    await writeFile(/* turbopackIgnore: true */ target, buffer, { flag: \"wx\" });\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  read(storageKey: string) {\r\n    return readFile(/* turbopackIgnore: true */ keyPath(storageKey));\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nconst assetStorage: TaleAssetStorage = new LocalTaleAssetStorage();\r\n\r\nfunction hasValidSignature(buffer: Buffer, mimeType: string) {\r\n  if (mimeType === \"image/png\") return buffer.subarray(0, 8).equals(Buffer.from([137, 80, 78, 71, 13, 10, 26, 10]));\r\n  if (mimeType === \"image/jpeg\") return buffer[0] === 0xff && buffer[1] === 0xd8;\r\n  if (mimeType === \"image/webp\")\r\n    return buffer.subarray(0, 4).toString() === \"RIFF\" && buffer.subarray(8, 12).toString() === \"WEBP\";\r\n  if (mimeType === \"image/avif\") return buffer.subarray(4, 12).toString().includes(\"ftyp\");\r\n  if (mimeType === \"application/pdf\") return buffer.subarray(0, 5).toString() === \"%PDF-\";\r\n  if (mimeType === \"video/mp4\") return buffer.subarray(4, 12).toString().includes(\"ftyp\");\r\n  if (mimeType === \"video/webm\" || mimeType === \"audio/webm\")\r\n    return buffer.subarray(0, 4).equals(Buffer.from([0x1a, 0x45, 0xdf, 0xa3]));\r\n  if (mimeType === \"audio/mpeg\")\r\n    return buffer.subarray(0, 3).toString() === \"ID3\" || (buffer[0] === 0xff && (buffer[1] & 0xe0) === 0xe0);\r\n  if (mimeType === \"audio/ogg\") return buffer.subarray(0, 4).toString() === \"OggS\";\r\n  if (mimeType === \"audio/wav\")\r\n    return buffer.subarray(0, 4).toString() === \"RIFF\" && buffer.subarray(8, 12).toString() === \"WAVE\";\r\n  return false;\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function store(storageKey: string, buffer: Buffer) {\r\n  await assetStorage.write(storageKey, buffer);\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function derivativeSet(buffer: Buffer, folder: string) {\r\n  const metadata = await sharp(buffer).metadata();\r\n  const definitions = [\r\n    { role: \"THUMBNAIL\", width: 360, quality: 72 },\r\n    { role: \"PREVIEW\", width: 960, quality: 78 },\r\n    { role: \"OPTIMIZED\", width: 1800, quality: 84 },\r\n    { role: \"MOBILE\", width: 900, quality: 80 },\r\n  ];\r\n  const items: Array<{\r\n    role: string;\r\n    storageKey: string;\r\n    mimeType: string;\r\n    buffer: Buffer;\r\n    width: number | null;\r\n    height: number | null;\r\n    checksum: string;\r\n  }> = [];\r\n  for (const definition of definitions) {\r\n    const output = await sharp(buffer)\r\n      .rotate()\r\n      .resize({ width: definition.width, withoutEnlargement: true })\r\n      .webp({ quality: definition.quality })\r\n      .toBuffer({ resolveWithObject: true });\r\n    const storageKey = `${folder}/${randomUUID()}.webp`;\r\n    items.push({\r\n      role: definition.role,\r\n      storageKey,\r\n      mimeType: \"image/webp\",\r\n      buffer: output.data,\r\n      width: output.info.width,\r\n      height: output.info.height,\r\n      checksum: createHash(\"sha256\").update(output.data).digest(\"hex\"),\r\n    });\r\n  }\r\n  return { width: metadata.width ?? null, height: metadata.height ?? null, items };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function ingestAsset(taleId: string, file: File, userId: string, replaceAssetId?: string) {\r\n  const definition = supported.get(file.type);\r\n  if (!definition)\r\n    throw new Error(\"Unsupported file type. Use PNG, JPEG, WebP, AVIF, MP4, WebM, MP3, Ogg, WAV, or PDF.\");\r\n  const maximum = Number(process.env.TALL_TALE_MAX_UPLOAD_MB ?? 25) * 1024 * 1024;\r\n  if (!file.size || file.size > maximum)\r\n    throw new Error(`Files must be smaller than ${Math.round(maximum / 1024 / 1024)} MB.`);\r\n  const buffer = Buffer.from(await file.arrayBuffer());\r\n  if (!hasValidSignature(buffer, file.type)) throw new Error(\"The file contents do not match the reported media type.\");\r\n  const checksum = createHash(\"sha256\").update(buffer).digest(\"hex\");\r\n  if (!replaceAssetId) {\r\n    const duplicate = await db.taleAsset.findUnique({\r\n      where: { taleId_checksum: { taleId, checksum } },\r\n      include: { variants: true, tags: { include: { tag: true } }, roles: true },\r\n    });\r\n    if (duplicate) {\r\n      logger.info(\r\n        { area: \"tall-tale-assets\", taleId, assetId: duplicate.id },\r\n        \"Duplicate upload reused existing asset\",\r\n      );\r\n      return { asset: duplicate, duplicate: true };\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  const folder = randomUUID();\r\n  const originalKey = `${folder}/${randomUUID()}${definition.extension}`;\r\n  await store(originalKey, buffer);\r\n  let width: number | null = null;\r\n  let height: number | null = null;\r\n  let derivatives: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof derivativeSet>>[\"items\"] = [];\r\n  if (definition.mediaType === \"IMAGE\") {\r\n    const processed = await derivativeSet(buffer, folder);\r\n    width = processed.width;\r\n    height = processed.height;\r\n    derivatives = processed.items;\r\n    for (const item of derivatives) await store(item.storageKey, item.buffer);\r\n  }\r\n  const asset = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const record = replaceAssetId\r\n      ? await tx.taleAsset.update({\r\n          where: { id: replaceAssetId },\r\n          data: {\r\n            displayName: file.name.replace(/\\.[^.]+$/, \"\"),\r\n            originalFilename: file.name,\r\n            mimeType: file.type,\r\n            fileSize: file.size,\r\n            width,\r\n            height,\r\n            checksum,\r\n            mediaType: definition.mediaType,\r\n            deletedAt: null,\r\n          },\r\n        })\r\n      : await tx.taleAsset.create({\r\n          data: {\r\n            taleId,\r\n            mediaType: definition.mediaType,\r\n            displayName: file.name.replace(/\\.[^.]+$/, \"\"),\r\n            originalFilename: file.name,\r\n            mimeType: file.type,\r\n            fileSize: file.size,\r\n            width,\r\n            height,\r\n            checksum,\r\n            createdBy: userId,\r\n          },\r\n        });\r\n    const original = await tx.taleAssetVariant.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        assetId: record.id,\r\n        role: \"ORIGINAL\",\r\n        storageKey: originalKey,\r\n        mimeType: file.type,\r\n        fileSize: file.size,\r\n        width,\r\n        height,\r\n        checksum,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    let optimizedVariantId: string | null = null;\r\n    for (const item of derivatives) {\r\n      const derivative = await tx.taleAssetVariant.create({\r\n        data: {\r\n          assetId: record.id,\r\n          role: item.role,\r\n          storageKey: item.storageKey,\r\n          mimeType: item.mimeType,\r\n          fileSize: item.buffer.length,\r\n          width: item.width,\r\n          height: item.height,\r\n          checksum: item.checksum,\r\n        },\r\n      });\r\n      if (item.role === \"OPTIMIZED\") optimizedVariantId = derivative.id;\r\n    }\r\n    await tx.taleAsset.update({\r\n      where: { id: record.id },\r\n      data: { currentVariantId: optimizedVariantId ?? original.id },\r\n    });\r\n    return tx.taleAsset.findUniqueOrThrow({\r\n      where: { id: record.id },\r\n      include: { variants: { orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" } }, tags: { include: { tag: true } }, roles: true },\r\n    });\r\n  });\r\n  logger.info(\r\n    {\r\n      area: \"tall-tale-assets\",\r\n      taleId,\r\n      assetId: asset.id,\r\n      mediaType: asset.mediaType,\r\n      replacement: Boolean(replaceAssetId),\r\n    },\r\n    \"Tall Tale asset ingested\",\r\n  );\r\n  return { asset, duplicate: false };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function assetUsages(taleId: string, assetId: string) {\r\n  const studio = await getStudioTale(taleId);\r\n  const usages: Array<{ type: string; id: string; label: string; field?: string }> = [];\r\n  if (studio.tale.coverAssetId === assetId)\r\n    usages.push({ type: \"tale\", id: taleId, label: \"Tall Tale cover\", field: \"coverAssetId\" });\r\n  for (const chapter of studio.draft.chapters) {\r\n    if (chapter.coverAssetId === assetId)\r\n      usages.push({ type: \"chapter\", id: chapter.id, label: `${chapter.title} cover`, field: \"coverAssetId\" });\r\n    for (const block of chapter.blocks)\r\n      for (const [field, value] of Object.entries(block.configuration))\r\n        if (value === assetId) usages.push({ type: \"block\", id: block.id, label: block.title, field });\r\n  }\r\n  for (const location of studio.locations)\r\n    for (const field of [\"mapAssetId\", \"displayAssetId\"] as const)\r\n      if (location[field] === assetId) usages.push({ type: \"location\", id: location.id, label: location.name, field });\r\n  for (const artifact of studio.artifacts)\r\n    for (const field of [\"artworkAssetId\", \"revealVideoAssetId\", \"modelAssetId\"] as const)\r\n      if (artifact[field] === assetId) usages.push({ type: \"artifact\", id: artifact.id, label: artifact.name, field });\r\n  return usages;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function updateAsset(\r\n  assetId: string,\r\n  input: { displayName?: string; description?: string; tags?: string[]; roles?: string[]; collectionIds?: string[] },\r\n) {\r\n  const asset = await db.taleAsset.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: assetId } });\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    await tx.taleAsset.update({\r\n      where: { id: assetId },\r\n      data: { displayName: input.displayName?.trim() || undefined, description: input.description?.trim() || null },\r\n    });\r\n    if (input.tags) {\r\n      await tx.taleAssetTagLink.deleteMany({ where: { assetId } });\r\n      for (const name of [...new Set(input.tags.map((tag) => tag.trim().toLocaleLowerCase()).filter(Boolean))]) {\r\n        const tag = await tx.taleAssetTag.upsert({\r\n          where: { taleId_name: { taleId: asset.taleId, name } },\r\n          update: {},\r\n          create: { taleId: asset.taleId, name },\r\n        });\r\n        await tx.taleAssetTagLink.create({ data: { assetId, tagId: tag.id } });\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n    if (input.roles) {\r\n      await tx.taleAssetRole.deleteMany({ where: { assetId } });\r\n      for (const role of [...new Set(input.roles)]) await tx.taleAssetRole.create({ data: { assetId, role } });\r\n    }\r\n    if (input.collectionIds) {\r\n      await tx.taleAssetCollectionItem.deleteMany({ where: { assetId } });\r\n      for (const collectionId of input.collectionIds)\r\n        await tx.taleAssetCollectionItem.create({ data: { assetId, collectionId } });\r\n    }\r\n    return tx.taleAsset.findUniqueOrThrow({\r\n      where: { id: assetId },\r\n      include: { variants: true, tags: { include: { tag: true } }, roles: true, collectionItems: true },\r\n    });\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function archiveAsset(assetId: string) {\r\n  const asset = await db.taleAsset.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: assetId } });\r\n  const usages = await assetUsages(asset.taleId, assetId);\r\n  if (usages.length) return { archived: false, usages };\r\n  await db.taleAsset.update({ where: { id: assetId }, data: { deletedAt: new Date() } });\r\n  return { archived: true, usages: [] };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function resolveAssetVariant(assetId: string, requestedRole: string, versionIdentity?: string) {\r\n  let allowedVariantIds: Set<string> | null = null;\r\n  if (versionIdentity && !versionIdentity.startsWith(\"draft:\")) {\r\n    const version = await db.publishedTaleVersion.findUnique({ where: { id: versionIdentity } });\r\n    if (!version) throw new Error(\"Published asset version not found.\");\r\n    const asset = parsePublishedSnapshot(version.contentSnapshot).assets.find((item) => item.id === assetId);\r\n    if (!asset) throw new Error(\"Asset is not part of this published version.\");\r\n    allowedVariantIds = new Set(asset.variants?.map((variant) => variant.id) ?? []);\r\n  }\r\n  const candidates = await db.taleAssetVariant.findMany({ where: { assetId }, orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" } });\r\n  const allowed = allowedVariantIds\r\n    ? candidates.filter((candidate) => allowedVariantIds.has(candidate.id))\r\n    : candidates;\r\n  const variant =\r\n    allowed.find((candidate) => candidate.role === requestedRole) ??\r\n    allowed.find((candidate) => candidate.role === \"OPTIMIZED\") ??\r\n    allowed.find((candidate) => candidate.role === \"ORIGINAL\");\r\n  if (!variant) throw new Error(\"Asset file is unavailable.\");\r\n  return { variant, buffer: await assetStorage.read(variant.storageKey) };\r\n}\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\r\nimport { DraftConflictError } from \"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\r\nimport { PublishValidationError } from \"@/tall-tale/publishing\";\r\nimport { VerificationRejectedError } from \"@/tall-tale/progression\";\r\nimport { logger } from \"@/lib/logger\";\r\n\r\nexport function apiError(cause: unknown) {\r\n  logger.warn(\r\n    {\r\n      area: \"tall-tale-api\",\r\n      errorType: cause instanceof Error ? cause.constructor.name : typeof cause,\r\n      errorCode:\r\n        cause instanceof DraftConflictError\r\n          ? \"DRAFT_CONFLICT\"\r\n          : cause instanceof PublishValidationError\r\n            ? \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"\r\n            : cause instanceof VerificationRejectedError\r\n              ? cause.reason\r\n              : \"REQUEST_FAILED\",\r\n    },\r\n    \"Tall Tale request rejected\",\r\n  );\r\n  if (cause instanceof DraftConflictError)\r\n    return NextResponse.json(\r\n      { error: cause.message, code: \"DRAFT_CONFLICT\", currentVersion: cause.currentVersion },\r\n      { status: 409 },\r\n    );\r\n  if (cause instanceof PublishValidationError)\r\n    return NextResponse.json(\r\n      { error: cause.message, code: \"VALIDATION_FAILED\", validation: cause.validation },\r\n      { status: 422 },\r\n    );\r\n  if (cause instanceof VerificationRejectedError)\r\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: cause.message, code: cause.reason }, { status: 409 });\r\n  const message = cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"The request could not be completed.\";\r\n  if (message.includes(\"Unique constraint\"))\r\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"That name or address is already in use.\" }, { status: 409 });\r\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.1 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.9 seconds\nOutput:\nimport { NewTaleForm } from \"@/components/studio/NewTaleForm\";\r\nimport { requireGmCapability } from \"@/lib/security\";\r\nexport const dynamic = \"force-dynamic\";\r\nexport default async function NewTalePage() {\r\n  return <NewTaleForm authenticated={Boolean(await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\"))} />;\r\n}\r\n\"use client\";\r\n\r\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\r\nimport { useEffect, useState } from \"react\";\r\nimport { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\r\n\r\nfunction slugify(value: string) {\r\n  return value\r\n    .toLocaleLowerCase()\r\n    .normalize(\"NFKD\")\r\n    .replace(/[\\u0300-\\u036f]/g, \"\")\r\n    .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\")\r\n    .replace(/^-|-$/g, \"\")\r\n    .slice(0, 80);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function NewTaleForm({ authenticated }: { authenticated: boolean }) {\r\n  const router = useRouter();\r\n  const [csrf, setCsrf] = useState(\"\");\r\n  const [title, setTitle] = useState(\"\");\r\n  const [slug, setSlug] = useState(\"\");\r\n  const [slugEdited, setSlugEdited] = useState(false);\r\n  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(false);\r\n  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\r\n  const [coverFile, setCoverFile] = useState<File | null>(null);\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    if (authenticated)\r\n      void fetch(\"/api/studio/tales\")\r\n        .then((response) => response.json())\r\n        .then((body: { csrfToken?: string }) => setCsrf(body.csrfToken ?? \"\"));\r\n  }, [authenticated]);\r\n  if (!authenticated)\r\n    return (\r\n      <main className=\"studio-auth-gate\">\r\n        <section>\r\n          <h1>Creator authentication required</h1>\r\n          <Link className=\"brass-button\" href=\"/quartermaster\">\r\n            Open Quartermaster login\r\n          </Link>\r\n        </section>\r\n      </main>\r\n    );\r\n  return (\r\n    <main className=\"new-tale-page\">\r\n      <form\r\n        className=\"new-tale-sheet\"\r\n        onSubmit={async (event) => {\r\n          event.preventDefault();\r\n          setBusy(true);\r\n          setError(\"\");\r\n          const values = Object.fromEntries(new FormData(event.currentTarget));\r\n          const response = await fetch(\"/api/studio/tales\", {\r\n            method: \"POST\",\r\n            headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-csrf-token\": csrf },\r\n            body: JSON.stringify({\r\n              ...values,\r\n              title,\r\n              slug,\r\n              playerCountMin: Number(values.playerCountMin),\r\n              playerCountMax: Number(values.playerCountMax),\r\n              estimatedDuration: Number(values.estimatedDuration) || undefined,\r\n            }),\r\n          });\r\n          const body = (await response.json()) as { id?: string; error?: string };\r\n          if (!response.ok || !body.id) {\r\n            setError(body.error ?? \"The new tale could not be created.\");\r\n            setBusy(false);\r\n            return;\r\n          }\r\n          if (coverFile) {\r\n            const upload = new FormData();\r\n            upload.append(\"files\", coverFile);\r\n            const uploadResponse = await fetch(`/api/studio/tales/${body.id}/assets`, {\r\n              method: \"POST\",\r\n              headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": csrf },\r\n              body: upload,\r\n            });\r\n            const uploaded = (await uploadResponse.json()) as {\r\n              assets?: Array<{ asset?: { id?: string } }>;\r\n              error?: string;\r\n            };\r\n            const coverAssetId = uploaded.assets?.[0]?.asset?.id;\r\n            if (!uploadResponse.ok || !coverAssetId) {\r\n              setError(\r\n                `The tale was created, but its cover could not be uploaded: ${uploaded.error ?? \"unknown upload error\"}`,\r\n              );\r\n              setBusy(false);\r\n              return;\r\n            }\r\n            const studioResponse = await fetch(`/api/studio/tales/${body.id}`, { cache: \"no-store\" });\r\n            const studio = (await studioResponse.json()) as {\r\n              tale?: Record<string, unknown>;\r\n              draft?: { autosaveVersion: number; chapters: unknown[] };\r\n              error?: string;\r\n            };\r\n            if (!studioResponse.ok || !studio.tale || !studio.draft) {\r\n              setError(\r\n                `The tale and cover were created, but the cover could not be assigned: ${studio.error ?? \"draft unavailable\"}`,\r\n              );\r\n              setBusy(false);\r\n              return;\r\n            }\r\n            const saveResponse = await fetch(`/api/studio/tales/${body.id}/draft`, {\r\n              method: \"PATCH\",\r\n              headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-csrf-token\": csrf },\r\n              body: JSON.stringify({\r\n                autosaveVersion: studio.draft.autosaveVersion,\r\n                tale: { ...studio.tale, coverAssetId },\r\n                chapters: studio.draft.chapters,\r\n              }),\r\n            });\r\n            if (!saveResponse.ok) {\r\n              const saved = (await saveResponse.json()) as { error?: string };\r\n              setError(\r\n                `The tale and cover were created, but the cover could not be assigned: ${saved.error ?? \"save failed\"}`,\r\n              );\r\n              setBusy(false);\r\n              return;\r\n            }\r\n          }\r\n          router.push(`/studio/tales/${body.id}`);\r\n        }}\r\n      >\r\n        <Link href=\"/studio\">â† Back to Studio</Link>\r\n        <p className=\"eyebrow\">Lay a fresh chart</p>\r\n        <h1>Create a New Tall Tale</h1>\r\n        <p>Start with the voyage identity and first chapter. Every field remains editable.</p>\r\n        <div className=\"form-grid\">\r\n          <label className=\"wide\">\r\n            <span>Title</span>\r\n            <input\r\n              value={title}\r\n              onChange={(event) => {\r\n                setTitle(event.target.value);\r\n                if (!slugEdited) setSlug(slugify(event.target.value));\r\n              }}\r\n              required\r\n              maxLength={160}\r\n            />\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label className=\"wide\">\r\n            <span>Address</span>\r\n            <span className=\"field-prefix\">/play/</span>\r\n            <input\r\n              value={slug}\r\n              onChange={(event) => {\r\n                setSlugEdited(true);\r\n                setSlug(event.target.value.toLocaleLowerCase());\r\n              }}\r\n              required\r\n              pattern=\"[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*\"\r\n            />\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label>\r\n            <span>Subtitle</span>\r\n            <input name=\"subtitle\" maxLength={240} />\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label>\r\n            <span>Theme</span>\r\n            <select name=\"theme\">\r\n              <option value=\"CARTOGRAPHERS_TABLE\">Cartographer&apos;s Table</option>\r\n              <option value=\"MOONLIT_JOURNAL\">Moonlit Journal</option>\r\n              <option value=\"CAPTAINS_CABIN\">Captain&apos;s Cabin</option>\r\n            </select>\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label className=\"wide\">\r\n            <span>Short description</span>\r\n            <textarea name=\"shortDescription\" maxLength={600} rows={3} />\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label className=\"wide\">\r\n            <span>Long description</span>\r\n            <textarea name=\"longDescription\" maxLength={20000} rows={5} />\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label>\r\n            <span>Minimum players</span>\r\n            <input name=\"playerCountMin\" type=\"number\" min={1} max={20} defaultValue={1} />\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label>\r\n            <span>Maximum players</span>\r\n            <input name=\"playerCountMax\" type=\"number\" min={1} max={20} defaultValue={4} />\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label>\r\n            <span>Estimated minutes</span>\r\n            <input name=\"estimatedDuration\" type=\"number\" min={1} max={10000} defaultValue={90} />\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label>\r\n            <span>Visibility</span>\r\n            <select name=\"visibility\">\r\n              <option value=\"PRIVATE\">Private draft</option>\r\n              <option value=\"UNLISTED\">Unlisted after publish</option>\r\n              <option value=\"PUBLIC\">Public after publish</option>\r\n            </select>\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label className=\"wide\">\r\n            <span>Initial chapter title</span>\r\n            <input name=\"initialChapterTitle\" defaultValue=\"Chapter One\" required />\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label className=\"wide\">\r\n            <span>Cover image (optional)</span>\r\n            <input\r\n              type=\"file\"\r\n              accept=\"image/png,image/jpeg,image/webp,image/avif\"\r\n              onChange={(event) => setCoverFile(event.target.files?.[0] ?? null)}\r\n            />\r\n            <small>The original is preserved and optimized player variants are generated after creation.</small>\r\n          </label>\r\n        </div>\r\n        {error && (\r\n          <p className=\"studio-error\" role=\"alert\">\r\n            {error}\r\n          </p>\r\n        )}\r\n        <div className=\"form-actions\">\r\n          <Link href=\"/studio\">Cancel</Link>\r\n          <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy}>\r\n            {busy ? \"Opening the chartâ€¦\" : \"Create and open editor\"}\r\n          </button>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </form>\r\n    </main>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

destroyed.\r\n• Before/after transformation images can be aligned and previewed.\r\nPublishing\r\n• Server-side validation prevents broken publication.\r\n• Publishing creates an immutable version.\r\n• Draft changes after publishing do not mutate the published version.\r\n• Version history is visible.\r\n• The newly published tale appears on the player catalog through real data.\r\nPlayer\r\n• The player can start the new tale.\r\n• The session pins the published version.\r\n• Blocks render from the published block registry.\r\n• Progress survives refresh.\r\n• One-time effects remain idempotent.\r\n• Completion appears in player history.\r\nCaptain\r\n• The Captain sees active sessions and current block.\r\n• The Captain sees pending verification.\r\n• Captain approval advances the player through the standard verification flow.\r\n• Reject/retry and override behavior are recorded.\r\n• Rollback and jump are controlled and audited.\r\n• Player state updates and uncertain events.\r\n• Simulated match advances only the correct pending request.\r\n• Duplicate, stale, wrong-session, wrong-version, and wrong-block events are rejected or safely ignored.\r\n• No helper can send an unrestricted \"advance\" command.\r\n• Future reference images and negative references can already be stored in collections.\r\nQuality\r\n• Existing tale behavior is preserved SOURCE CONTROL AND CHANGE DISCIPLINE\r\n• Work only in the intended repository.\r\n• Inspect git status before changing anything.\r\n• Preserve unrelated pre-existing changes.\r\n• Do not reset, discard, overwrite, or \"clean up\" unrelated work.\r\n• Keep migrations additive and reversible where practical.\r\n• Use focused commits if repository policy permits commits.\r\n• Do not push, deploy, tag, or merge unless separately authorized.\r\n• Do not alter production secrets or credentials.\r\n• Do not replace current data with sample data.\r\n• Seed/demo content must be clearly separated from real content.\r\n27. FINAL PRODUCT INTENT\r\nThis platform should let a creator build a Tall Tale as a sequence of meaningful, reusable story blocks instead of hand-coding \r\nevery adventure. The Studio, player experience, Captain controls, and future vision helper are not separate products. They are \r\ndifferent interfaces over one shared, versioned story model and one authoritative progression engine.\r\nThe correct architectural relationship is\r\nTall Tale Studio\r\n  -> creates a draft story graph and reusable assets\r\n  -> validates and publishes an immutable version\r\n  -> automatically appears in the player catalog\r\n  -> starts version-pinned player sessions\r\n  -> produces pending interaction or verification requests\r\n  -> receives player, Captain, timer, or future vision outcomes through one standardized \r\nverification path\r\n  -> advances through one authoritative progression engine\r\n  -> records an auditable history\r\n  -> preserves completed adventures and memories exactly as they were played\r\nImplement the full Phase 1 foundation with enough polish that it feels intentional now and enough modularity that automatic \r\nvisual verification can be attached later without rebuilding the platform.\r\nEND OF IMPLEMENTATION PROMPT\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 2.9 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 2.9 seconds\nOutput:\nHASH=012887C6E7970017316B7CFC12EE414CE029103C197F2EA34CB0B22749083102 PATH=\\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\Downloads\\Tall_Tale_Studio_Phase_1_Codex_Implementation_Prompt.pdf\r\nHASH=012887C6E7970017316B7CFC12EE414CE029103C197F2EA34CB0B22749083102 PATH=\\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\forever-treasure-companion\\Development_Docs\\Tall_Tale_Studio_Phase_1_Codex_Implementation_Prompt.pdf\r\neaa649fe4a0a1dec5eb7b8fbfb950fb1380959e8\neaa649fe4a0a1dec5eb7b8fbfb950fb1380959e8\nfeature/cinematic-animation-rebuild\nhttps://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\n# Tall Tale Studio Phase 1\r\n\r\n## Delivered workflow\r\n\r\nTall Tale Studio is an additive authoring and play system. It does not replace the original single-campaign companion.\r\n\r\n- `/studio` lists drafts, validation state, release state, assets, and sessions.\r\n- `/studio/tales/new` creates a private tale and first chapter.\r\n- `/studio/tales/[id]` edits a vertical story graph with optimistic autosave, undo/redo, dnd-kit pointer/keyboard sorting, and explicit add/move controls.\r\n- Settings, assets, locations, artifacts, and version history have dedicated nested routes.\r\n- `/tales` is the dynamic public catalog. `/play/[taleSlug]` starts a version-pinned session.\r\n- `/captain` lists live sessions; `/captain/sessions/[id]` controls progression and exposes the development verification simulator.\r\n\r\nCreator and publisher operations require a server-side GM session, explicit capabilities, and CSRF. Player access tokens are random, stored only as hashes, and retained in a strict HTTP-only browser cookie rather than the session URL. Media storage keys are opaque and validated before filesystem access.\r\n\r\nThe editor includes searchable Block Library, Chapters, and Story Outline tabs; collapsible chapters; visible flow/drop indicators; and responsive inspector behavior. A selected block can be rendered in an isolated desktop or mobile viewport with reduced-motion and replay controls, while Full Tale Preview and Play From Here use expiring draft sessions. The More menu exposes settings, version history, duplicate, and guarded archive actions.\r\n\r\n## Authoring model\r\n\r\nThe registry in `src/tall-tale/block-registry.ts` is the source of truth for all Phase 1 blocks:\r\n\r\n1. Narrative\r\n2. Captain's Note\r\n3. Riddle\r\n4. Information\r\n5. Travel Direction\r\n6. Location\r\n7. Arrival Check\r\n8. Image\r\n9. Image Transformation\r\n10. Cinematic\r\n11. Audio\r\n12. Artifact Reveal\r\n13. Hidden Message Reveal\r\n14. Collection Update\r\n15. Confirmation\r\n16. Choice\r\n17. Text Answer\r\n18. Captain Approval\r\n19. Wait\r\n20. Condition\r\n21. Set Variable\r\n22. Chapter Complete\r\n23. Tale Complete\r\n\r\nEach entry owns a stable type ID, category, defaults, inspector metadata, Zod configuration schema, asset-field declarations, and verification-provider dispatch. Unknown types render a safe diagnostic card and do not execute arbitrary behavior.\r\n\r\nThe editor sends the complete normalized draft with its `autosaveVersion`. The server claims that exact token before replacing chapter and block rows. A stale browser receives `DRAFT_CONFLICT`; its local state is not discarded. Choice and condition targets become explicit `BlockConnection` records. Duplication assigns new chapter/block IDs and remaps branch targets.\r\n\r\n## Publishing and sessions\r\n\r\nPublishing runs server-side graph, configuration, asset, and reachability validation. Blocking errors prevent a release; warnings remain visible. A successful publish serializes tale metadata, chapters, blocks, connections, locations, artifacts, and exact asset variants into a checksummed immutable snapshot. The transaction advances `latestPublishedVersionId` and marks the previous release non-current.\r\n\r\nThe catalog lists only current public releases; current unlisted releases remain available by their direct address. Starting a game records the exact `publishedVersionId`; later releases cannot alter that session. Preview sessions instead carry a draft snapshot and are visibly labeled. Every session event has a monotonic sequence and globally unique idempotency key.\r\n\r\nVersion History can preview any immutable release or copy it into a newly numbered draft based on that release without mutating the published snapshot or discarding the prior draft. The catalog reports New, In Progress, or Completed/Replayable for the authenticated browser session and routes its primary action to Start, Continue, or Replay accordingly.\r\n\r\nThe progression engine advances automatic blocks, evaluates variables/conditions, grants artifacts once, and opens standardized verification requests. Text answers, player confirmations, timers, Captain decisions, helper submissions, and the simulator converge on that engine. It rejects duplicate, stale, wrong-session, wrong-version, wrong-block, and wrong-request submissions.\r\n\r\n## Assets and reusable libraries\r\n\r\nUploads validate MIME allowlists, size, and magic bytes. Originals are retained; images receive WebP thumbnail, preview, optimized, and mobile derivatives with checksums. Duplicate content returns the existing logical asset. Replacement adds new variants to the same asset ID, preserving existing draft references and older version snapshots. Published media requests are restricted to variants captured by that version.\r\n\r\nAssets support display metadata, tags, semantic roles, collections, search/filtering by media/context/role/tag/collection/usage, recent and unused views, lazy thumbnails, paged expansion, drag-to-field assignment, usage lookup, original download, replacement, and guarded archive. A local `TaleAssetStorage` implementation isolates storage operations behind a replaceable interface. Locations support searchable/sortable records, duplicate/edit/archive, used-by counts, player copy, private Captain notes, map/display assets, a reference collection, and a future verification profile. Artifacts provide the parallel searchable/duplicable workflow for lore, ordinary-object labels, artwork/reveal/model assets, inventory categories, and persistence.\r\n\r\nInteractive player, Captain, upload, and helper routes apply bounded per-process rate limits in addition to authorization, CSRF, idempotency, and optimistic concurrency. Structured logs cover publish, asset ingestion, session starts, accepted/rejected verification, and sanitized API failures without recording answers, credentials, bearer tokens, or raw evidence.\r\n\r\n## Data and migration\r\n\r\nSQLite migration `20260717213000_tall_tale_studio_phase1` and MySQL migration `0003_tall_tale_studio_phase1` add the same domain. The seed is idempotent and additive: it preserves legacy Campaign rows and rebuilds only the known development Studio tale.\r\n\r\nFor local SQLite:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\nnpm run db:generate\r\nnpm run db:migrate\r\nnpm run db:seed\r\n```\r\n\r\nFor a fresh MySQL database, apply the three numbered scripts in order as documented in `docs/deployment.md`.\r\n\r\n## Operational checklist\r\n\r\nBefore release, run `npm run validate`. Manually confirm narrow/mobile editor navigation, draft conflict messaging with two windows, published-session pinning after publishing a newer release, asset archive protection, Captain disconnect/reconnect state, and helper rejection cases. Keep `.data/tall-tale-assets` or the configured durable asset root backed up alongside the database.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

## Tool Result: call_UMHvBT0GMUCxAQj8xQmSQDBs

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.9 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.9 seconds\nOutput:\n# Known limitations\r\n\r\n- The repository is public. Final story, riddles, personal material, surprise content, production credentials, and private media remain intentionally blocked.\r\n- Invitation seal, journal clasp, voyage compass, and finale mechanism have typed Rive contracts but no final designer-authored `.riv` binaries. Production renders honest original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The sole working `.riv` file is an MIT-licensed development-only runtime sample.\r\n- Current Lottie effects are original shape-layer ambience/punctuation, not final designer-exported Bodymovin artwork.\r\n- Finale unlock/assembly remains structural and cannot enter unsupported `READY` or later states through current controls.\r\n- SSE fan-out is process-local. Database replay is durable, but multi-instance production needs Redis or equivalent pub/sub.\r\n- Interactive route rate limits are process-local. Multi-instance production needs a shared limiter such as Redis in addition to the existing authorization and idempotency controls.\r\n- Initial Ship's Log projection is capped at 250 entries; cursor pagination is future scale work.\r\n- The parallel MySQL migration still needs exercise against a real MySQL 8 integration environment.\r\n- Development FPS/long-task metrics are observational and local, not production telemetry. Final Rive binaries require another mobile GPU/memory profile.\r\n- The complete mutation workflow runs once in Chromium to avoid shared-database contention; privacy, access gates, landing behavior, accessibility, responsive layouts, and mobile behavior also run in WebKit.\r\n- Windows LAN access is opt-in and can still be blocked by firewall or corporate network policy.\r\n- Final licensed audio, production monitoring, CI, deployment, and private content authoring remain later work.\r\n- Tall Tale asset binaries use one local filesystem root in Phase 1. Object storage, antivirus scanning, resumable large uploads, and asynchronous media processing remain production hardening work.\r\n- The future vision provider is deliberately inactive. Phase 1 supplies reference collections, a scoped helper pairing token, a versioned verification contract, strict progression rejection rules, and a development simulator; it does not capture camera frames or claim recognition accuracy.\r\n# Future vision helper boundary\r\n\r\nPhase 1 does not perform image recognition. It prepares a narrow, auditable provider seam so a later phone/helper implementation cannot become a second progression engine.\r\n\r\n## Reference preparation\r\n\r\nCreators can organize assets into location or artifact collections, including positive and negative reference sets, and assign a collection to a location. Locations retain a JSON verification profile for future provider configuration. Published snapshots freeze those records and referenced asset variants with the rest of a release.\r\n\r\n## Pairing\r\n\r\nAn authenticated Captain creates a short-lived pairing through `POST /api/helper/pair` with `sessionId` and a stable local `deviceId`. The server permits only active, published, non-preview sessions, stores a hash of the token, and returns the raw bearer token once. Tokens expire after 15 minutes. `GET /api/helper/status` acts as the scoped heartbeat: it updates presence and returns only session status, the pinned published version, current block, and current verification request. A Captain can revoke a device immediately through `DELETE /api/helper/pair/[pairingId]`; revocation is recorded as an ordered session event.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 intentionally provides the API boundary rather than a camera UI. A future QR or numeric-code ceremony should transmit the returned token over an authenticated local channel, avoid query-string logging, and rotate/revoke on re-pair.\r\n\r\n## Verification envelope\r\n\r\nThe helper submits `POST /api/helper/verification` with `Authorization: Bearer <pairing token>` and a versioned body:\r\n\r\n```json\r\n{\r\n  \"schemaVersion\": 1,\r\n  \"eventId\": \"provider-unique-event\",\r\n  \"idempotencyKey\": \"provider-stable-retry-key\",\r\n  \"eventType\": \"verification.observation\",\r\n  \"providerType\": \"visionLocation\",\r\n  \"providerInstanceId\": \"helper-device-id\",\r\n  \"sessionId\": \"session-id\",\r\n  \"publishedVersionId\": \"published-version-id\",\r\n  \"blockId\": \"current-block-id\",\r\n  \"verificationRequestId\": \"pending-request-id\",\r\n  \"observedAt\": \"2026-07-17T20:00:00.000Z\",\r\n  \"result\": \"match\",\r\n  \"confidence\": 0.92,\r\n  \"evidence\": { \"referenceCollectionId\": \"collection-id\" }\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe API validates shape, bounds confidence to 0..1, updates pairing presence, applies a token-keyed rate limit without logging the token, and calls the same `submitVerification` function used by other providers. The engine binds the event to the pending request and checks session, published version, current block, provider type, and request status before advancing. Duplicate retries return the prior accepted result; contradictory or stale submissions are rejected and retained as verification evidence where appropriate. The helper never receives an unrestricted advance, jump, rollback, or Captain action endpoint.\r\n\r\n## Simulator\r\n\r\n`POST /api/captain/simulate` is development-only and requires a Captain session plus CSRF. It creates the same envelope and enters the same progression function with `sourceType=simulator`; it is not a shortcut or a production provider. The Captain session screen exposes match, no-match, and uncertain controls when a compatible request is pending.\r\n\r\n## Later implementation rules\r\n\r\n- Keep frame capture and model inference outside the authoritative web process.\r\n- Upload derived evidence by default, not raw frames; define retention and consent before storing imagery.\r\n- Sign provider releases and identify the provider instance/model in evidence.\r\n- Calibrate confidence per verification profile; never interpret confidence alone as permission to progress.\r\n- Preserve human Captain override and complete audit events.\r\n- Add a polished pairing/revocation ceremony and encrypted device-to-server transport before real-device rollout; the scoped heartbeat, API revocation, and process-local rate guards already exist.\r\n- Test false positive, false negative, duplicate, delayed, reordered, wrong-version, and offline/reconnect cases against the current contract suite.\r\n# Deployment\r\n\r\nTarget: Debian, Node 24, MySQL 8, NGINX, HTTPS, and systemd. Do not deploy from development seed credentials.\r\n\r\n1. Create MySQL database/user and set `DATABASE_URL=mysql://...`.\r\n2. For a new database run `npm ci`, `npm run db:generate:mysql`, `npm run db:migrate:mysql:init`, `npm run db:migrate:mysql:companion`, and `npm run db:migrate:mysql:studio`, then `npm run build`. Record future MySQL migrations under `prisma/mysql-migrations` before production use.\r\n3. Install `deploy/forever-treasure.service`, place secrets in `/etc/forever-treasure.env`, and proxy with `deploy/nginx.conf`.\r\n4. Send application logs to journald; retain NGINX access/error logs under `/var/log/nginx`.\r\n\r\nBack up with daily encrypted `mysqldump --single-transaction`, retain seven daily/four weekly copies off-host, and test quarterly restore into a separate database. Restore by stopping the service, importing a verified dump, applying pending migrations, and starting/reconciling. Generate secrets with `openssl rand -base64 48`.\r\n\r\nSet `TALL_TALE_ASSET_ROOT` to durable storage outside the release directory and back it up with the database. Published snapshots retain asset variant identities, so a database-only restore is incomplete. Phase 1 uses local filesystem storage; horizontally scaled production needs shared object storage before enabling multiple application nodes.\r\n# The Forever Treasure Companion\r\n\r\nProduction-oriented foundation and fully automated local demo for a private nautical-fantasy date-night Tall Tale. All committed story material is generic development seed content; no final surprise content belongs in this public repository.\r\n\r\n## Current phase\r\n\r\nThe cinematic companion now also includes Tall Tale Studio Phase 1: an authenticated no-code authoring workspace, reusable asset/location/artifact libraries, immutable publishing, a dynamic player catalog, version-pinned live sessions, and Captain controls. A shared animation director still coordinates the original harbor, journal, chart, artifact, and finale experience without allowing presentation timing to outrun server truth. All committed content remains fictional development seed material.\r\n\r\n## Stack\r\n\r\n- Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, strict TypeScript\r\n- Prisma 6.19 with SQLite for local/test and an equivalent MySQL production schema\r\n- GSAP 3.15 for orchestrated scenes and SVG/text work; Motion 12 for React interaction and presence\r\n- StPageFlip for the journal; local Rive and Lottie runtimes with explicit static fallbacks\r\n- bcrypt password/access-code hashing, server-side database sessions, CSRF tokens, and rate limiting\r\n- Vitest and Playwright\r\n\r\n## Reproducible setup\r\n\r\nRequirements: Windows PowerShell 5.1+, Node.js 24, npm 11, and Git. MySQL 8 is only required for production parity; the supported local/demo/test strategy is SQLite.\r\n\r\n### Fastest Windows demo\r\n\r\nAfter cloning, double-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd`, or run:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\nnpm run dev:full\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe launcher creates an ignored `.env` when absent, installs the exact lockfile, generates Prisma, applies migrations, seeds generic disposable data, starts the app, checks health, and prints the URLs and credentials. Network/UNC workspaces are mirrored to an ignored local runtime under `%LOCALAPPDATA%` because Node and SQLite are unreliable on network shares. Stop it with `npm run dev:stop`.\r\n\r\n- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\r\n- Tall Tale catalog: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tales`\r\n- Player phrase: `development-moonwake`\r\n- GM: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\r\n- Studio: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/studio`\r\n- Captain: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/captain`\r\n- GM development[REDACTED: LOGIN CREDENTIAL]\r\n\r\nThese values are disposable local defaults, never production credentials. To opt into LAN access, run `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan`; Windows Firewall and network policy still determine whether another device can connect.\r\n\r\n### Portable manual setup\r\n\r\n```bash\r\ngit clone https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\r\ncd treasurehuntSoT\r\nnpm ci\r\ncp .env.example .env\r\nnpm run db:generate\r\nnpm run db:migrate\r\nnpm run db:seed\r\nnpm run dev\r\n```\r\n\r\nSet unique local values in `.env`; the seedâ€™s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\r\n\r\n## Commands\r\n\r\n| Command                           | Purpose                                                                |\r\n| --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |\r\n| `npm run dev:full`                | Idempotent setup, migration, seed, health check, and background server |\r\n| `npm run dev:stop`                | Safely stop only the recorded development process                      |\r\n| `npm run validate`                | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof          |\r\n| `npm run dev`                     | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                        |\r\n| `npm run build`                   | Production build                                                       |\r\n| `npm start`                       | Start production build                                                 |\r\n| `npm test`                        | Deterministic domain, director, ownership, asset, and page tests       |\r\n| `npm run assets:validate`         | Validate local Lottie/Rive contracts and fallback paths                |\r\n| `npm run test:e2e`                | Five Chromium/WebKit checks plus one Chromium mutation workflow        |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate`              | Apply local SQLite migrations                                          |\r\n| `npm run db:seed`                 | Load replaceable development content                                   |\r\n| `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage` | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset                |\r\n| `npm run db:generate:mysql`       | Generate production MySQL client                                       |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:studio` | Apply the production-parity Tall Tale Studio migration                 |\r\n\r\nFull setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Studio architecture and operations are in [Tall Tale Studio](docs/tall-tale-studio.md); the future recognition seam is in [vision helper boundary](docs/future-vision-helper.md). Animation ownership, scene contracts, assets, performance, and the development lab are indexed from [animation architecture](docs/animation/architecture.md).\r\n\r\n## Repository workflow\r\n\r\n`main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\r\n\r\n## Important limitations\r\n\r\nThe full story, final Rive artwork/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural. The sole third-party visual sample is MIT-licensed, local, documented, and development-only; production uses original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\r\n\r\n## Next steps\r\n\r\nAdd CI for the complete validation gate, exercise the parallel MySQL schema in an integration environment, and replace process-local SSE fan-out before multi-instance deployment. Real surprise content remains blocked until repository visibility is verified private.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-17T23:53:31.960Z

Finished. The complete Tall Tale Studio Phase 1 prompt is implemented and validated against the exact 51-page PDF; both PDF copies share SHA-256 `012887C6…83102`.

### Delivered

- Full authenticated Studio with tale/chapter/block authoring, accessible drag-and-drop, truthful autosave, conflict detection, duplication, archive, responsive inspector, undo/redo, preview and Play From Here.
- All 23 required blocks: Narrative, Captain’s Note, Riddle, Information, Travel Direction, Location, Arrival Check, Image, Image Transformation, Cinematic, Audio, Artifact Reveal, Hidden Message Reveal, Collection Update, Confirmation, Choice, Text Answer, Captain Approval, Wait, Condition, Set Variable, Chapter Complete, Tale Complete.
- Complete media pipeline: validated uploads, originals, WebP derivatives, search/filtering, collections, roles, tags, usage protection, logical replacement and transformation alignment.
- Immutable checksummed publishing, visible version history, published-version preview and copy-to-new-draft.
- Dynamic catalog, Start/Continue/Replay states, version-pinned sessions, refresh persistence, idempotent effects and history.
- Captain dashboard, live state, approval/rejection/override, jump/rollback auditing and simulator.
- Versioned future-helper contract with scoped pairing, heartbeat, revocation, persisted verification requests and strict stale/wrong-context rejection.
- SQLite and MySQL parity migrations with additive seed behavior preserving the existing campaign.

Primary documentation: [Tall Tale Studio](<//gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion/docs/tall-tale-studio.md>) and [future helper boundary](<//gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion/docs/future-vision-helper.md>).

### Routes

Studio: `/studio`, `/studio/tales/new`, `/studio/tales/[taleId]`, plus `assets`, `locations`, `artifacts`, `settings`, and `versions`.

Player: `/tales`, `/play/[taleSlug]`, `/play/[taleSlug]/session/[sessionId]`, `/play/[taleSlug]/history`.

Captain: `/captain`, `/captain/sessions/[sessionId]`, `/captain/tales/[taleId]`.

The corresponding Studio, player, Captain, media and helper API routes are implemented under `src/app/api`.

### Verification

The final clean release gate passed:

- 3 migrations applied to a fresh database.
- Prettier, ESLint and strict TypeScript passed.
- 21 test files and 67 unit tests passed.
- Playwright: 13 passed, 5 intentional platform skips.
- Media-rich create/upload/drag/align/publish/play/artifact/history golden path passed.
- Seed and post-acceptance database verification passed.
- Production build completed without warnings.
- Two consecutive production restart proofs passed.
- Manual desktop/mobile browser inspection drove the responsive inspector and interaction-polish fixes.

Real limitations are documented: MySQL still needs a live MySQL 8 integration run; asset storage, SSE and rate limiting are single-instance; the future vision provider remains intentionally inactive; production monitoring, object storage and final licensed media remain later hardening.

### Source control

Repository: `//gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion`
Branch: `feature/cinematic-animation-rebuild`
Starting task commit: `96b9c99`
Current commit: `f8bf41259a5f3b8747da66f2b595f9888e4190ae`

The current commit contains only the mandatory conversation synchronization. Phase 1 source changes remain uncommitted because no source commit/push was authorized and unrelated cinematic/player work was already present. That unrelated work was preserved.

Chat archive: 2 conversations updated; commit `f8bf412` pushed and verified against the remote; archive validates at 10 conversations and 6,505 messages.
Development docs: 0 eligible changes, 0 excluded and 0 conflicts; synchronization validation passed.
